pillars.headline: The offline foundation is genuinely strong: client-UUID sessions with clientId dedup (OfflineSessionService/OfflineResponsePersistence), a version-pinned definition cache with snapshot replay (filloutDefinitionKey, getPinnedOfflineQuestionnaire), a same-origin media cache with Range/background-full-GET (sw.js filloutMediaStrategy, D1), a conflict-free single-batch sync with exponential backoff and orphan-session collection (FilloutSyncEngine), and offline i18n (compile-time Paraglide + cached localizeQuestionnaire). The single biggest gap is that resume is a fiction: QuestionnaireRuntime.resume() never rehydrates currentItemIndex or prior responses (getSessionResponses is dead in prod), so a reload/interruption restarts the participant at question 0 and cross-device resume is impossible — unacceptable for long or reaction-heavy studies. Secondary gaps: no encryption-at-rest and no shared-device purge on a plaintext IndexedDB store (P2-T4 fixes purge only), a fragile app-shell precache that does not guarantee the fillout route loads cold-offline (SW is even disabled in dev), and no sync integrity layer — marking is fire-and-forget with no server ack reconciliation, no record checksums, no queued-count, and no large-payload chunking. Derived-variable/quality-report persistence is already owned by remediation P1-T1; this pillar builds beyond it toward a bulletproof, resumable, encrypted, integrity-checked offline product.

sota_matrix.capability: PWA app-shell precache of the fillout runtime
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: static/sw.js:20-23 precaches only '/' and '/offline.html'; :159-198 caches build-manifest.json chunks (manifest generated by vite plugin, vite.config.ts:16-40) so JS bundles are cached, but the /q/[code] navigation document has no precache rule (falls to staleWhileRevalidate→offlineResponse when never visited) and there is no SPA-fallback (200.html) precache, so a cold-offline visit to an un-visited code cannot boot the runtime. SW also fully disabled+unregistered in dev (services/offline.ts:21-56), so offline is untestable without a prod build.

sota_matrix.capability: Offline media via Cache API + D1 same-origin proxy
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: sw.js:277-406 filloutMediaStrategy: cache-first on stable /api/media/{id}/content key, 206-range handling with backgroundCacheFullMedia, 504 (not HTML) on failure; filloutMedia accounting table + enforceMediaQuota (FilloutOfflineSyncService.ts:170-252, indexeddb.ts:78-97).

OfflineSessionService.createSession uses crypto.randomUUID() (line 22); OfflineResponsePersistence.saveResponse/saveEvent attach clientId=crypto.randomUUID() (:54,:82); server ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING (per ADR 0023 D2).
ResponsePersistenceService.saveResponse writes IndexedDB first then fire-and-forget syncNow (ResponsePersistenceService.ts:55-91); D2 single write path confirmed.

FilloutRuntime.ts:54 owns a dead second VariableEngine; getVariables() returns {} so the save loop at :239-241 never runs; QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() pushes computed vars into session.variables (:1204) and qualityReport into metadata (:1200) but handleComplete (:259-271) drops both. Owned by remediation P1-T1 (F055-F059) — this pillar builds beyond it.

QuestionnaireRuntime.resume() (:274-279) only resumes the response collector; startSession resets currentItemIndex=0 (:553) and nothing restores prior answers. OfflineResponsePersistence.getSessionResponses/getSessionVariables (:110,:130) are called only in tests. A resumed ?sid= session restarts at question 0.

load (+page.ts:115-171) resolves the version pin from the server session and falls back to latest snapshot (pinnedFallback), but never fetches the server's stored responses to rehydrate, and the runtime has no restore hook. Cross-device continuation is impossible.

filloutDefinitionKey composite key (indexeddb.ts:89-97), version-keyed rows coexist (v3 upgrade :190-228), getPinnedOfflineQuestionnaire + pin resolution in +page.ts:106-171; sessions pin (questionnaireId, maj.min.patch) at create.

sota_matrix.capability: Robust sync: backoff + conflict-free + orphan collection
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: FilloutSyncEngine: exp backoff 1s→60s (:23-24,:249-259), single batched api.sessions.sync per session (:229), collectSessionsToSync unions unsynced sessions + orphan child ids (:130-145). Gaps: retry scheduled only on OUTER failure not per-session (:98-111); no large-payload chunking (a reaction run of hundreds of trials sends one body); markVariablesSynced marks ALL a session's vars regardless of payload (OfflineResponsePersistence.ts:213-218).

sota_matrix.capability: Sync integrity: server-ack reconciliation + no silent loss
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: syncSession marks records synced by local id BEFORE/without checking which client_ids the server actually accepted (FilloutSyncEngine.ts:232-240 uses result counts only, not an accepted-id set). No per-record checksum, no write-verify, no dead-letter for permanently failing rows.

indexeddb.ts:114-143 stores filloutResponses/filloutVariables/filloutSessions as plaintext structured clones; grep for crypto.subtle in fillout persistence returns only FraudDetectionService fingerprint hashing (:117), none on the response store.

clearAllData() (indexeddb.ts:436-451) clears only designer tables (questionnaires/syncQueue/resources/drafts), NOT the fillout tables; no post-sync purge and no 'end session / clear this device' action. Owned by remediation P2-T4 (F005) — this pillar layers encryption + key-destruction on top.

sota_matrix.capability: Connectivity UX (offline indicator, queued-count, sync status)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: +page.svelte:562-593 shows offline + syncing/synced/error badges, but there is NO queued-count, no last-synced timestamp, and no manual 'Sync now' / 'Save & exit' control; syncStatus resets to idle after 3s (:250).

Compile-time Paraglide (ADR 0019) bakes chrome strings into the bundle; localizeQuestionnaire/resolveText run against the cached definition (+page.svelte:39-81); per-questionnaire translations travel in the cached filloutQuestionnaires row.
QuotaService.checkQuotas is gated on navigator.onLine (+page.svelte:417); offline participants bypass quotas entirely. Server-side per-cell counting + offline enforcement is remediation P1-T4 (F014).

requestPersistentStorage() called app-wide (root +layout.svelte:46) and getStorageEstimate exists (offline.ts:178-209), but getStorageUsage (indexeddb.ts:401-434) omits all fillout tables and no eviction-pressure guard protects an in-flight session's data.

sota_matrix.capability: Two-path offline coherence (SW queue vs app sync engine)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: sw.js:294-338 fetchWithOfflineQueue queues raw /api mutations to a separate 'pending-requests' store with its own background-sync (:530-597), independent of FilloutSyncEngine's IndexedDB pipeline — two uncoordinated retry mechanisms with no shared idempotency ledger; risk of double-submit/ordering surprises for session.update/submitEvents.

pillars.headline: The precision core is unusually strong for a web platform and is the pillar's real asset: all four stimulus modalities exist and are timed correctly — visual (raf + measured display-latency correction), audio (WebAudio scheduling with output/DAC-latency compensation via getOutputTimestamp), video (requestVideoFrameCallback with expectedDisplayTime and a per-trial frame ring), and image (CORS-safe preloading/warm-up) — plus a per-trial timing_provenance blob, false-start detection, render-error trial invalidation, COOP/COEP isolation, and a device-qualification/calibration gatekeeper. The gaps are on the experiment-design surface around that core, not the clock. Response capture is keyboard-first: mouse/touch are wired but spatial responses are never scored (evaluateCorrectness rejects non-string values) and there is NO gamepad channel or key-hold/release/multi-key support. Only 5 standard paradigms ship (Stroop/Flanker/IAT/n-back/dot-probe) — Go/NoGo, SART, Simon, Posner cueing, visual search, Sternberg, PVT, temporal-order and RSVP are all missing, and the closest competitors (jsPsych/Gorilla/PsychoPy) ship these as first-class. Trial-level feedback is dead (the `feedback` config is normalized but never rendered), stimulus OFFSET is a setTimeout (not vsync-aligned, so brief-exposure/masking/RSVP durations are imprecise), rich per-trial provenance (signed RT, anticipatory flags, display/output latency, phase timeline, video frames) is dropped at the runtime→response mapping, and there is no participant-level counterbalancing.

sota_matrix.capability: Frame-accurate visual onset with measured display-latency correction
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: ReactionEngine.ts:654-662 records raf onset + displayLatencyMs; TimingCalibration.ts:31 estimatedDisplayLatency=meanFrameInterval; TimingGatekeeper.ts:104 getEstimatedDisplayLatencyMs; recordStimulusOnset ReactionEngine.ts:910-936

sota_matrix.capability: Audio stimulus onset corrected for output latency (WebAudio scheduling)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: ReactionEngine.ts:1449-1514 playAudioViaContext: AudioBufferSource, ctx.outputLatency/baseLatency, getOutputTimestamp() contextTime->performanceTime mapping counting output latency exactly once; audio decode cache :1523-1541; HTMLAudio degraded fallback :1143-1154

ReactionEngine.ts:1377-1401 startVideoFrameLogging owns onset via metadata.expectedDisplayTime (method 'rvfc'); per-trial VideoFrameSample ring (types.ts:134-141); raf fallback after 2 frames :665-683

ReactionEngine.ts:266-301 warmUpStimuli, :234-254 seedFromResourceManager, :1299-1323 loadImage sets crossOrigin before src; imageCache reuse; same-origin media proxy ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:119 resolveConfigMediaToProxy
ReactionEngine.ts:731-747 keydown handler + validKeys filtering; getEventTime :956-961 uses event.timeStamp (DOMHighResTimeStamp), method='event.timeStamp'

ReactionEngine.ts:749-782 mouse click + touchstart capture normalized {x,y} with event.timeStamp BUT evaluateCorrectness :896-901 returns false for any non-string value, so spatial responses are never scored; designer exposes no responseMode selector (only validKeys), and compiler forces responseMode:'keyboard' (reaction-compiler.ts:144,733)
grep for gamepad/getGamepads across apps/web/src/lib returns only a designer icon (BlockManager.svelte:11); no navigator.getGamepads polling, no 'gamepad' in ReactionResponseMode (types.ts:3)
ReactionEngine.ts:731 listens only to 'keydown'; no keyup/hold-duration capture, no key chords; ReactionResponseCapture (types.ts:117-131) has no release timestamp or hold duration field

types.ts:181-207 ReactionTrialProvenance; built ReactionEngine.ts:410-426; aggregated reaction-scoring.ts:55-79; persisted server-side responses.timing_provenance (migration 00024; sessions.rs:2777/2935/2948)

sota_matrix.capability: COOP/COEP cross-origin isolation for full timer resolution
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: hooks.server.ts:30-31 sets COOP same-origin + COEP require-corp on fillout routes; vite.config.ts:134-135 dev headers; DeviceQualification.ts:88-96 detects crossOriginIsolated and warns

DeviceQualification.ts qualify() grades green/yellow/red on jitter+timerResolution+capabilities; TimingCalibration.ts collects frame timestamps, meanFrameInterval, jitter, timer resolution; TimingGatekeeper.shared() runs once/session

sota_matrix.capability: Standard paradigm breadth (Go/NoGo, SART, Simon, Posner, visual search, Sternberg, PVT, temporal-order, RSVP)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Present: presets/{stroop,flanker,iat,nBack,dotProbe}.ts (reaction-schema.ts:3-10 ReactionTaskType). Missing: no Go/NoGo, SART, Simon, Posner cueing, visual search, Sternberg, PVT, temporal-order or RSVP presets/task-types (grep returns only a help-doc mention)
ReactionEngine.ts:375-383 responseTimeoutMs (default 2000) via waitForResponse :837-878; stimulus duration runs concurrently with the response window :368-372; timeout flag on result

Practice present: compiler marks isPractice and excludes from scoring (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:173). Feedback DEAD: reaction-normalize.ts:170 reads a feedback flag but grep 'feedback' in ReactionEngine.ts/runtimes returns nothing - no correct/incorrect or RT feedback is rendered, and no criterion-to-advance practice

Within-block seeded shuffle exists (reaction-compiler.ts:78-83; IAT fixed block order in iat.ts). No participant-level counterbalancing: seedRoot is questionnaire/question id (reaction-compiler.ts:28-31), identical across participants; no Latin-square/round-robin condition assignment, no per-participant block-order rotation

sota_matrix.capability: Frame-accurate stimulus OFFSET / frame-count duration spec
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Onset is frame-exact but OFFSET uses window.setTimeout(stimulusDurationMs) ReactionEngine.ts:369-372 - not vsync-aligned, so brief exposures/masking/RSVP durations drift up to a frame; durations are ms-only, no frame-count spec in ReactionTrialConfig (types.ts:91-108)

sota_matrix.capability: Data columns SOTA (RT, accuracy, correctness) + full per-trial export
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Aggregate RT/accuracy/timeouts/byCondition present (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:194-217). Per-trial rows exist in value.responses but the runtime->TrialResponse mapping (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:146-170) DROPS rawRtMs(signed), anticipatory/falseStartCount, displayLatencyMs/outputLatencyMs, videoFrames, phaseTimeline, stimulusOffsetTime - all on ReactionTrialResult (types.ts:215-259) but never persisted; no tidy long-format export

pillars.headline: QDesigner has a genuinely capable single-session flow engine: seeded reproducible randomization (BlockRandomizer + seededRandom), a live scripting hook system (ScriptExecutor onMount/onResponse/onValidate/onNavigate/onPageEnter/onPageExit/onTimer, all invoked from QuestionnaireRuntime), computed variables (VariableEngine), carry-forward answer piping, attention checks wired into a QualityReport, and between-subjects condition assignment. But the pillar falls well short of 2026 SOTA on the highest-value differentiators: the CAT/IRT adaptive engine (CATSession) is fully implemented and unit-tested yet has ZERO fillout wiring; there is no ESM/EMA/longitudinal multi-session scheduling or reminder infrastructure anywhere (no server tables, no scheduler); there is no true save-and-continue (the runtime always restarts at page 0 — no position restore); loop/repeat blocks are silently dead (randomizeBlock expands a loop into N question-id copies that randomizePage's dedup Set immediately collapses back to one — BlockRandomizer.ts:38-52 vs :81-97); per-page/whole-survey/response-deadline timers for form questions are unenforced (PageSettings.timeLimit is defined but read nowhere); and counterbalancing is broken at the wiring seam (participantNumber is never passed to the runtime, so every participant gets Latin-square row 0). The committed remediation plan fixes the plumbing (question-target branching P1-T3, per-cell quota counting P1-T4, variable persistence P1-T1, interpolation unification P1-T6, sandbox hardening P2-T2) but deliberately does not build any of the missing capabilities above — those are the gap to the vision.

sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: QuestionnaireRuntime.handleFlowControl (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1128-1169) + findMatchingFlowRule (:1171-1181) exist, but flow rules are GLOBAL and evaluated only at page-end — findMatchingFlowRule scans questionnaire.flow with no position scoping, so the first matching rule wins for every page transition. Question-id targets no-op (F015, fixed by P1-T3). Needs position-scoped rules + a real branch graph beyond the P1-T3 page-target fix.

LoopConfig exists (questionnaire.ts:371) and BlockRandomizer.randomizeBlock (BlockRandomizer.ts:81-97) expands loop.values into N question-id copies, but randomizePage (:38-59) dedupes via a `seen` Set that collapses all iterations back to a single presentation — loop blocks render exactly once. No per-iteration variable namespacing (updateQuestionVariables keys solely on question.id, :1073-1082, so iterations would overwrite). FlowControl 'loop' (:1137-1153) only re-navigates whole pages by count.

Seeded reproducible randomization is solid: seededRandom.ts + BlockRandomizer (seed from settings.randomizationSeed||id, QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:128-130). Counterbalancing/Counterbalancing.ts getBlockOrder (Latin square) implemented, but participantNumber is NEVER passed from the fillout page to FilloutRuntime (+page.svelte:496-502 omits it) so ConditionAssigner.assign / getBlockOrder always use participantNumber=0 (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:374) → every participant gets row 0.

Two interpolation stores: runtime {{}} via variableInterpolation.ts (fed live vars) and a disconnected ${} singleton pass (F061, fixed by P1-T6). Answer piping into options via CarryForward.ts (resolveCarryForward/applyCarryForward, wired at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1279-1302). No piping across loop iterations or into flow conditions.

VariableEngine (mathjs) + ScriptExecutor with 7 lifecycle hooks all invoked (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:748,975,1036,523,569,588). Gaps are already-planned: sandbox escapable (F002/F004→P2-T2), derived-var persistence dropped (F055-F059→P1-T1), full mathjs eager-loaded (F103→P8-T2). Capability itself is live.

CATSession (analytics/CATEngine.ts:45-209) is a complete 3PL Fisher-information selector with MLE Newton-Raphson theta + SE stopping rules, plus functions/irt.ts — but grep shows it is referenced ONLY by CATEngine.test.ts. No item-bank type in questionnaire-core, no adaptive block, no instantiation in QuestionnaireRuntime/FilloutRuntime. Fully unwired.

QuotaService (QuotaService.ts) evaluates simple string conditions client-side; server quota_status returns total_completed for every cell (F014, per-cell counting fixed by P1-T4). No interlocking/least-full cross-quota cell assignment, and re-check only against urlParams (Map at +page.svelte:419-424), not in-survey demographic answers.

AttentionCheckValidator (runtime/quality/AttentionCheck.ts) is wired: QuestionnaireRuntime validates every response (:968-972,:1029-1033) and folds failures into QualityReport (:1200). No distinct manipulation-check construct, no per-check branching/terminate action, no mid-survey IMC insertion.

Reaction path is frame-accurate; page onTimer hook fires on a hardcoded 1000ms interval (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:586-597). PageSettings.timeLimit (questionnaire.ts:244) and TimingConfig.maxTime (:299) are defined but enforced nowhere for form questions (grep for timeLimit enforcement in runtime = none). No whole-survey countdown, no per-question response deadline with auto-submit/timeout action.

sota_matrix.capability: ESM / EMA + longitudinal multi-session scheduling & reminders
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Nothing exists: no schedule/reminder/wave/longitudinal tables in apps/server/migrations (through 00026), no scheduler, no participant-panel enrollment, no email/push reminder dispatch, no resume-link issuance. Sessions pin a single (questionnaireId, version) run with no concept of a series or a next-prompt time.

ConditionAssigner (experimental/ExperimentalDesign.ts) + condition counts fetched and passed (+page.svelte:485-501), stored in session.metadata.custom (:405-411). But participantNumber unwired (see counterbalancing), assignment snapshot dropped at completion (F056→P1-T1 persists vars), and allocation is client-side over a fetched count snapshot — no server-atomic least-full arm claim, so concurrent starts can unbalance arms.

Progress present (getProgress :325-334, showProgressBar). saveProgress setting exists (questionnaire.ts:208) and offline persistence writes responses, but QuestionnaireRuntime always start()s at navigateToPage(0) (:263) with no position/variable-state restore — no true resume-to-position, no continue-later token or link.

ConsentScreen.svelte + WelcomeScreen.svelte + settings.requireConsent gate entry (+page.svelte). Eligibility screen-out only via FlowControl 'terminate' (:1132-1135) or quota over-quota actions — no dedicated pre-survey screener block with structured screen-out reasons/redirects.

ADR 0022 translations stored in settings.translations (questionnaire.ts:228), LanguagePicker + getTranslations live (memory notes live-verified in Phase 8). Flow-level gaps: language not exposed as a flow variable for branching, and interpolation/piping run on base-language templates before translation resolution is confirmed.

pillars.headline: The raw material for a SOTA feedback suite is unusually complete on paper but badly under-wired in practice. The participant path (statistical-feedback module + runtime/feedback/ScoreInterpreter + ReportGenerator) delivers eight chart types, designable color-coded score bands, a range-with-marker bar, and current-session metrics computed client-side — but the powerful psychometric layer (analytics/ScoreInterpreter with T-scores/z/stanines/percentiles/CI/subscale summation, ScoringPipeline, MissingDataHandler) is dead code never reachable from fillout, and the CompletionScreen's report path is mounted without scoreConfigs or variables (q/[code]/+page.svelte:700-707), so the end-of-survey report is inert. The three biggest gaps to the vision are: (1) no norm/baseline mechanism beyond a cohort endpoint that is 401 for anonymous participants — no bundled norm tables, no self-baseline/pre-post; (2) no dedicated designable end-of-survey REPORT PAGE that composes multiple feedback widgets with conditional sections and piped interpretive text; (3) reverse-scoring, subscale scoring, and missing-data handling are not first-class in the participant scoring path (only hand-authored mathjs formulas). Remediation P1 restores variable persistence + interpolation + require-Continue + anon cohort access, which are prerequisites, but stops well short of the full capability.

sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Scale-level metrics compute offline in engine.ts metricValueFromObject (:120) and via mathjs VariableEngine formulas; but subscale summation exists only in the DEAD analytics/ScoreInterpreter.computeSubscaleScores (ScoreInterpreter.ts:196) and ScoringPipeline (only imported by analytics/index.ts, never by runtime). No subscale config on the questionnaire model.

grep for 'reverse' in questionnaire.ts / VariableEngine finds no field or helper; reverse scoring is only achievable as an ad-hoc mathjs formula (e.g. '6 - q1') with no designer affordance or per-item reverseScored flag. Need a ScaleScoringDef.reverseScoredItemIds + designer toggle.

analytics/MissingDataHandler.ts implements listwise/pairwise/mean/median/LOCF/MI + MCAR but is imported only by analytics/index.ts; the participant scoring path (QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() variable snapshot, ScoreInterpreter) applies no missing-data policy. Need a missingPolicy wired into a runtime ScaleScorer.

Fully implemented in analytics/ScoreInterpreter.generateNormativeComparison (:150, T/z/stanine/percentile) and chart-utils.percentileFromZ (:118) but the class is DEAD for participants; runtime feedback only yields a cohort z-score via server aggregate and range-band labels (runtime/feedback/ScoreInterpreter.ts is range-band-only). Need to wire the analytics interpreter into fillout.

psychometrics.buildPsychometrics computes Cronbach alpha + classical IRT (psychometrics.ts:277,137); scripting-engine functions/psychometric.ts exports SPLIT_HALF/KR20/OMEGA — all pure/offline. (Analyst-facing, not participant feedback, which is the correct scope for reliability.)

engine.ts cohort/participant-vs-cohort modes call sessionAnalyticsService.aggregate/compare, but /api/sessions/aggregate|compare require AuthenticatedUser (sessions.rs:652) so they 401 for anon participants; loadSeries swallows to a 'No data' point (StatisticalFeedback.svelte:54). P1-T5 restores anon access — necessary but only the cohort mechanism.

No norm-table library anywhere; ReportGenerator accepts normativeStats (ReportGenerator.ts:44) but no caller ever supplies it, and there is no store of known-instrument mean/SD constants for percentile/T-score without a live cohort. Need a shipped NormTable[] + lookup + designer picker.

StatisticalSourceMode (engine.ts:12) has no self-baseline/pre-post option; there is no repeated-measures data source that compares a participant's earlier vs later score. Line chart exists but has no trajectory data feed or reliable-change computation.

ScoreInterpreterConfig ranges (min/max/label/description/color) are fully editable in StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte:442-529 with color pickers, and drive both chart ColorRules (StatisticalFeedback.svelte:120) and the PDF (ReportGenerator buildRangeBar). P1-T7 adds the missing per-band description input.

Feedback is a per-item display module placed on an ordinary page — there is no dedicated report-page composition. CompletionScreen supports scoreConfigs+variables+Download Report (CompletionScreen.svelte:14-17,158) but q/[code]/+page.svelte:700-707 mounts it WITHOUT those props, so the end-screen report is inert. Need a ReportPage composition + wire-through.

FeedbackChart.svelte handles bar/line/radar/scatter/histogram/box (:62-78); BellCurveChart (distribution + participant marker) and GaugeChart are separate components dispatched in StatisticalFeedback.svelte:224-252.

No table renderer in the statistical-feedback charts dir (only Bell/Gauge/Feedback). summaryEntries render as CSS stat tiles (StatisticalFeedback.svelte:257) but there is no configurable results table widget with per-scale rows.

sota_matrix.capability: Interpretive text with variable piping (dollar-brace + double-brace)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: ModularRenderer interpolates {{var}} against runtime variables before mount; TextDisplay runs a second ${var} pass against a disconnected scriptingEngine singleton nothing populates (F061). Score-band descriptions are static per band, not piped. P1-T6 unifies the two syntaxes but does not add piping INTO band feedback text.

ReportGenerator.generateReport builds self-contained HTML then calls printHTMLAsPDF → hidden-iframe window.print() (ReportGenerator.ts:266-321) — the OS print dialog, not a file download; header comment misdescribes it as a dependency-free PDF byte-stream (F063). No embedded Chart.js canvases, only an inline SVG bar.

QuestionnaireRuntime.evaluateVisibility (:1103) honors item/page conditions/formulas so a whole feedback item can be shown/hidden conditionally, but there is no per-band or per-message conditional feedback builder (e.g. 'if anxiety High show message X') surfaced in the feedback designer.

StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte offers title/subtitle/source-mode/metric/chart-type, variable pickers from designerStore.questionnaire.variables, multi-scale band editor with color pickers, and an embedded live StatisticalFeedback preview (:567-577). Uses bespoke raw <input> (F038) rather than ui primitives.

pillars.headline: The two-tier role model is real and consistently enforced at the mutation boundary: OrgRole (Owner/Admin/Member/Viewer) and ProjectRole (Owner/Admin/Editor/Viewer) are defined with numeric hierarchies (rbac/models.rs, manager.rs), and every org/project/member/domain/invitation mutation is gated by has_org_role/has_project_role across 65 call sites in 8 handler files, backstopped by dual-path RLS (00014/00020-00023). ADR-0020's change_member_role has correct last-owner and owner-only-grant guards. But this is team-collaboration RBAC, not an enterprise multi-tenant product: project-level READ isolation does not exist (list_projects and access::verify_project_access grant every active org member visibility into all projects, so ProjectRole::Viewer/Editor is a write-only distinction — inconsistent with the questionnaire_definitions RLS policy that DOES scope by project_members), the granular Permission enum (models.rs:66) is entirely dead code with no require_permission gate, and every enterprise capability is absent: no audit log, no API keys/service accounts, no SSO/SAML/OIDC, no SCIM, no explicit ownership transfer, no org-level GDPR export/erasure, no branding application, no cross-project sharing. The biggest single gap is that the system has no concept of scoped, auditable, machine or federated identity — only human users who are all-or-nothing members of an org.

sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: 00014_rls_policies.sql organizations_select/members/projects/sessions policies + 00020-00022 admin+force + 00021 fillout dual-path; per-request GUC in middleware/rls_context.rs:44-52

sota_matrix.capability: Org role matrix (Owner/Admin/Member/Viewer) + enforcement at every mutation
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: rbac/models.rs:3-30 + manager.rs:118-125 hierarchy; organizations.rs gates update(402)/delete(489)/add_member(608-618)/remove_member(681)/change_role(762-783)/invitations(917)/domains(1451) via has_org_role

sota_matrix.capability: Project role matrix (Owner/Admin/Editor/Viewer) enforced for WRITES
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: rbac/models.rs:34-61; projects.rs update(304)/delete(430)/add_member(568)/update_member(673)/remove_member(751) via has_project_role; access.rs:88-117 verify_project_write_access

sota_matrix.capability: Project-level READ isolation (Viewer/Editor scoping honored on reads)
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: GAP: projects.rs list_projects:109-141 JOINs only organization_members (every active org member sees all projects); access.rs:58-82 verify_project_access grants read to any org member; inconsistent with questionnaire_definitions RLS (00014:154-175) which DOES scope by project_members. Need a project_members-scoped read path.
organizations.rs create_invitation(894)/list(842)/accept(1192)/decline(1269)/revoke(1328); organization_invitations table 00001:54-64

organization_domains 00007 with default_role + auto_join; verify_domain organizations.rs:1495-1527 is an auto-verify stub (F016 -> P8-T8 DNS TXT); onboarding discovery stubbed (F017 -> P8-T9). Remediation covers baseline; no HTTP well-known fallback or re-verification cadence.

admin/users/+page.svelte role-change/remove present but uses confirm() (F021) and is Svelte-4 legacy (F029); admin/+layout.ts client-side role guard only. No seat/quota limits, no suspend/reactivate flow though schema has status='suspended' (00001:46).

Server add/update/remove_project_member exist (projects.rs:543-782) but grep of apps/web finds no project-member UI (only api.ts references project_members). Project roles are unassignable from the product.

Permission enum declared rbac/models.rs:66-95 with 19 actions but #[allow(dead_code)] and ZERO usages outside models.rs; no require_permission middleware; authorization is role-level only, no custom roles or permission grants.

change_member_role (organizations.rs:740) can grant 'owner' with last-owner guard but no atomic transfer (demote-previous) semantics; no project ownership-transfer endpoint; org/project creator ownership implicit, no reassignment on member removal.
grep for audit/AuditLog across apps/server/src returns nothing; no table, no write path, no admin surface. Role changes, invitations, deletions leave no trail.
grep for saml/oidc/sso across apps/server returns nothing; auth.rs is email+Argon2 (register:27) only. No IdP config, no external-identity linkage on users.
grep for scim returns nothing; users/members are created only via register + invitation accept. No automated deprovisioning.
organizations table has settings JSONB + logo_url (00001:31-32) and update_organization(385); but no UI applies branding (logo/theme) and no whitelabel surface consumes it.
grep for api_key/service_account returns nothing; only JWT bearer for human users (api/runtime.ts). No scoped machine tokens for exports/integrations.

sota_matrix.capability: Org-level GDPR export + tenant erasure + data residency
sota_matrix.evidence_or_gap: Only individual account deletion is planned (F019 -> P8-T10). No org-scoped data export bundle, no tenant hard-erasure, no residency/region tagging on organizations.
No sharing model; projects are strictly org-scoped and (over-)shared to all org members. No guest collaborators, share links, or explicit cross-project grants.

synthesis.interleave_notes: Remediation is the substrate the epics compile against; the unblock graph is dominated by five high-fan-out tasks. P1-T1 (persist derived variables + quality report) is the single largest unlock — it gates E-OFF-1, E-FLOW-1/3/4/5/6, and E-FEEDBACK-1/3, so it must be the first task merged inside M1, not sequenced arbitrarily. P1-T4 (real per-cell quota) unblocks E-FLOW-6/7 and E-OFF-6; P1-T5 (anon cohort endpoint) unblocks E-FEEDBACK-2; P1-T6/T7 (interpolation + honest stat-feedback panel) unblock E-FEEDBACK-3/5/6; P1-T3 (question→page target resolution) unblocks E-FLOW-8; P1-T2 (fillout CSS tokens) unblocks E-RBAC-8 branding. On the security side, P2-T4 (IndexedDB purge) unblocks E-OFF-2 encryption; P2-T5 (auth hardening) unblocks E-FLOW-2 enrollment and E-RBAC-5/6; P2-T9 (httpOnly refresh cookie) unblocks E-RBAC-6 SSO. The test/type backbone in M2 is a hard gate for the enterprise and reaction tracks: P3-T2 (HTTP harness + cross-tenant DENY suite) unblocks E-RBAC-1, and P4-T4 (typed runtime→module v1 contract) unblocks E-REACT-1 (and transitively the whole reaction track). The feedback pillar cannot start until P5-T2 (canonical stats utils) and P5-T3 (shared chart setup) exist — they feed E-FEEDBACK-1 and E-FEEDBACK-4. In the offline-hardening milestone, P8-T1 (batched sync inserts + set-based analytics) must land before E-OFF-4 and is also a prerequisite for E-FLOW-2 EMA; P8-T3 (WebGL allocation hoist) must land before E-REACT-3 frame-accurate offset; P8-T10 (self-service deletion) must land before E-RBAC-9 org erasure. Where epics depend only on other epics (E-OFF-5→E-OFF-4, E-REACT-2→E-REACT-1, E-RBAC-2→E-RBAC-1→P3-T2, E-FEEDBACK-2→E-FEEDBACK-1), the chain is honored by intra-milestone ordering.

milestones.goal: Close every participant-facing correctness defect and every auth/transport/injection hole before any capability is built on top. Data a participant produces is persisted, sanitized, and quota-enforced; the auth surface is hardened. No new capabilities — this is the platform the epics stand on.
milestones.gate: security-review skill clean on branch; live golden-path QA green (derived variables persist through sync, fillout chrome renders with valid tokens, per-cell quota enforced and fails closed, anon cohort feedback returns data); no console PII in prod bundle; refresh token exists only in httpOnly Secure cookie; anonymous sessions cannot forge user_id (RLS WITH CHECK).
milestones.exit_demo: An anonymous participant completes an offline questionnaire on a shared device: derived variables + quality report survive the final sync, IndexedDB is purged on session end, researcher-authored {@html} is DOMPurify-sanitized, and an auth brute-force attempt hits per-email lockout with dummy-Argon2 constant-time response.

milestones.goal: Make sessions genuinely resumable (the single biggest offline gap: resume() at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:274 only unpauses, currentItemIndex stays 0) and stand up the HTTP harness, typed contracts, and structural cleanup the capability epics compile against.
milestones.gate: Reload/interrupt mid-questionnaire rehydrates exact position + prior responses + variable-engine state; cross-device resume-link works; CI runs HTTP round-trip + cross-tenant mutation-DENIAL + fullstack/visual e2e lanes against a live backend stack; runtime→module v1 contract (P4-T4) and fillout entry (P4-T6) fully typed; OpenAPI regenerated.
milestones.exit_demo: Participant abandons at Q18 of 40 on a laptop and resumes on a phone via link at Q18 with every answer and computed variable intact; CI output shows the fillout-session round-trip and the api/access cross-tenant DENY suite passing.

milestones.goal: Turn the strong precision clock into a complete experiment surface: multi-channel input, vsync-aligned offset, trial feedback, and full per-trial provenance export. This is the reaction pillar's foundational epic set.
milestones.gate: Gamepad + key-hold/release + spatial responses produce correctly scored trials (evaluateCorrectness no longer rejects non-string values); stimulus offset is vsync-aligned with frame-count duration specification (not setTimeout); trial-level feedback renders (the normalized-but-dead feedback config); tidy long-format export carries signed RT, anticipatory flags, display/output latency, and the phase timeline; photosensitivity advisory shown before reaction paradigms.
milestones.exit_demo: A masked dot-probe with a 100 ms vsync-aligned exposure, spatial left/right response captured over a gamepad, per-trial correctness feedback, exports a tidy CSV with full timing provenance columns — preceded by a photosensitivity + reduced-motion advisory.

milestones.goal: Wire the built-but-dead psychometric scoring engine (analytics/ScoreInterpreter, ScoringPipeline, MissingDataHandler) into the participant path and ship a designable end-of-survey report — the feedback pillar is mostly activation, not greenfield.
milestones.gate: CompletionScreen mounted with scoreConfigs+variables (today inert at q/[code]/+page.svelte:700-707); report page composes multiple widgets with subscale/reverse scoring, T/z/stanine/percentile/CI, and missing-data handling; bundled norm-table library + self-baseline/pre-post source; per-band conditional interpretive text with piping; downloadable PDF with embedded charts; entire path works offline; charts carry role=img alt text.
milestones.exit_demo: A participant finishes a multi-subscale inventory and sees a fully offline report: subscale T-scores against a bundled norm table, a pre/post trajectory line, conditional per-band interpretive text, and a downloadable PDF with embedded charts.

milestones.milestone: M5 — SOTA Flow Programmability (plumbing) + Accessibility + Svelte 5 Correctness
milestones.goal: Activate the flow-engine capabilities that are wired-but-dead (loops, timers, counterbalancing, quotas, position-scoped rules) and clear the accessibility and Svelte-5 memoization debt across participant + designer surfaces.
milestones.gate: Loop blocks expand to N real iterations with per-iteration variable namespacing (BlockRandomizer dedup no longer collapses them); per-page/whole-survey/per-question deadlines enforced with configurable timeout actions; participantNumber flows server-atomically to the least-full arm (no more Latin-square row 0 for everyone); interlocking quota cells + pre-survey screener gate; WCAG focus/aria-live/radiogroup fixes green in axe; zero memoization-defeating $derived callables remain.
milestones.exit_demo: A between-subjects study assigns participant #N to the least-full counterbalanced arm, runs a 3-iteration loop block with per-iteration answer piping, enforces a 90 s per-page limit that auto-advances, screens out ineligible participants at entry, and is fully keyboard-navigable with screen-reader question announcements.

milestones.goal: Make the offline product resilient enough for long, reaction-heavy, shared-device field studies: encrypted at rest, cold-offline bootable, integrity-checked, ack-reconciled sync, and honest connectivity UX.
milestones.gate: Fillout IndexedDB encrypted (WebCrypto AES-GCM) with a purge-and-destroy key lifecycle; cold-offline SPA boot reliably loads the fillout route (SW no longer disabled in dev); sync marks only server-ack-reconciled records with per-session retry, large-payload chunking, and a single unified queue; record checksums + write-verify + append-only sync ledger + eviction guards prevent silent loss; participant sees queued-count/last-synced/manual-sync/save-and-clear/offline quota; mathjs lazy-loaded off the critical path.
milestones.exit_demo: An airplane-mode participant completes a long study; the device shows '23 responses queued, encrypted'; on reconnect the ledger reconciles every server ack with zero silent loss, and a forced storage-eviction is caught and surfaced rather than losing data.

milestones.milestone: M7 — Multi-tenant Governance Foundation + Product UX Polish
milestones.goal: Turn team-collaboration RBAC into real multi-tenant isolation with governance, and finish the remaining designer/admin UX polish. E-RBAC-1 is treated as security-adjacent (closes an active cross-project read-exposure gap) and must not be deferred to the enterprise tail.
milestones.gate: Project-scoped READ isolation enforced (verify_project_access + list_projects consult project_members — ProjectRole::Viewer can no longer see all org projects); append-only audit log + admin timeline; granular Permission enum activated with require_permission + custom roles over the dead models.rs:66 enum; project member UI + seat model; explicit org & project ownership transfer; per-org branding; real DNS-TXT domain verification; self-service account deletion (GDPR erasure).
milestones.exit_demo: A ProjectRole::Viewer added to a single project sees only that project (not the whole org); every privileged mutation appears in the org audit timeline; a custom role with a scoped permission set is created and enforced end-to-end; a sole-owner-guarded self-deletion anonymizes PII and revokes tokens.

milestones.milestone: M8 — Enterprise Federation + Adaptive / Longitudinal / Paradigm Frontier
milestones.goal: The highest-order differentiators that require the full foundation: federated identity, the adaptive + longitudinal SOTA flow, and the standard-paradigm library. Highest estimation variance — external-standard and IRT-heavy work.
milestones.gate: OIDC/SAML SSO with JIT org membership; SCIM 2.0 provisioning + API keys/service accounts; org-level GDPR export + tenant erasure + residency tagging; cross-project/external-guest sharing; CAT/IRT adaptive item-bank block live in fillout (CATSession wired — today CATEngine.ts:45 has zero runtime references) with SE-stop; longitudinal/EMA multi-session scheduling + reminders + resume-link enrollment; nine new standard paradigms (Go/NoGo, SART, Simon, Posner, visual search, Sternberg, PVT, temporal-order, RSVP); participant-level counterbalancing + stimulus-set assignment.
milestones.exit_demo: An enterprise tenant provisions users via SCIM, logs in via SAML, runs a 7-day EMA study with push reminders and a CAT-adaptive depression scale that stops at SE<0.3 plus a counterbalanced Go/NoGo block, then exports the whole tenant's data on a GDPR request.

pillar_readiness.verdict: On track — M2 resumable sessions close the structural resume gap and M6 delivers encryption/integrity/ack-reconciliation; residual risk concentrated in the XL/high E-OFF-1 rehydration and the encryption key lifecycle (data-loss surface).

On track — M3 completes multi-channel capture + vsync offset + provenance export on top of an already-strong clock; M8 paradigm library + counterbalancing reach competitor parity. Highest-readiness pillar.

On track but two-speed — M5 activates the dead plumbing (loops/timers/counterbalancing/quotas) cheaply, but the pillar-defining differentiators (E-FLOW-1 CAT wiring, E-FLOW-2 EMA/longitudinal) are XL/high and sit last in M8; they carry the most estimation variance of the whole roadmap.

Achievable — the capability is largely built-but-dead (analytics scoring engine, chart types, bands), so M4 is mostly wiring + norm tables; the one hard item is the XL/high E-FEEDBACK-3 designable report page.

Achievable but back-loaded — M7 delivers the real tenant isolation + audit + custom-permission governance that closes the biggest gaps; M8 federation (SSO/SCIM/API keys) is external-standard-heavy and the riskiest enterprise work.

synthesis.sequencing_changes:
The committed 16-wave remediation order is largely preserved, but five tasks must be pulled forward out of their natural late-phase waves to unblock foundational epics, and one intra-P1 ordering is pinned:
1. PIN P1-T1 as the very first task merged in M1. It is the highest-fan-out unblocker on the board (8 epics across three pillars); sequencing it arbitrarily inside the P1 wave would stall M2/M4 and half of M5/M8.
2. PULL P5-T2 (canonical stats utils) and P5-T3 (shared chart setup) forward from the P5 refactor wave into the M2 backbone. E-FEEDBACK-1 and E-FEEDBACK-4 depend on them; the feedback pillar cannot start until they exist. (This is natural anyway, since all of P5 lands in M2.)
3. PULL P8-T3 (WebGL allocation hoist) forward from the P8 perf wave into M3, ahead of E-REACT-3 frame-accurate offset which builds directly on the hoisted render loop.
4. PULL P8-T1 (batched sync_session inserts + set-based analytics) forward into M6, ahead of E-OFF-4 sync hardening; it is also a hard prerequisite for E-FLOW-2 EMA in M8.
5. PULL P8-T10 (self-service account deletion / erasure path) forward into M7, ahead of E-RBAC-9 org-level erasure which extends it.
6. RECLASSIFY E-RBAC-1 (project read isolation) as security-adjacent rather than a deferrable capability: verify_project_access (access.rs:58) and list_projects (projects.rs:89) currently grant every active org member read access to all projects, so ProjectRole::Viewer is a write-only distinction and cross-project data is exposed. It lands in M7 immediately after its only gate (P3-T2), not in the enterprise tail.
Beyond these, epics are inserted after the remediation wave that unblocks them rather than reordering any fix; P6 (Svelte 5) and P7 (a11y) waves are distributed across the participant-facing milestones (M3/M4/M5) instead of being deferred to the end, because their defects are participant-facing and several P6 memoization bugs can corrupt runtime behavior.
top_risks: E-OFF-1 / E-FLOW-3 resume rehydration is the roadmap's structural linchpin and is XL/high. Today resume() (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:274-280) only unpauses — currentItemIndex stays 0 and no response/variable/reaction-trial replay exists. Rehydrating position + response state + VariableEngine state without corrupting reaction timing provenance is deep; if M2 slips, everything downstream slips.

Five XL/high frontier epics (E-FEEDBACK-3 report page, E-FLOW-1 CAT wiring, E-FLOW-2 EMA scheduling, E-RBAC-6 SSO, E-RBAC-7 SCIM/API-keys) carry external-standard or IRT surface (SAML/OIDC/SCIM 2.0, item-response theory). Each needs its own de-risking spike before its milestone; naive estimates will be optimistic.

Sequencing hazard: shipping any capability epic before P2 security remediation lands widens the attack surface, and E-RBAC-1 must not be deferred — it closes an active cross-project read-exposure gap (ProjectRole is currently write-only). The roadmap gates both, but schedule pressure to 'start features early' would reopen them.

Live-QA gate: per project memory, type/test-green ≠ works here. The reaction and feedback pillars can pass CI while being visually or behaviorally broken (dead configs, unmounted score props). Every participant-facing milestone (M3/M4/M5/M6) needs real-browser QA including offline and cross-origin-isolated (COOP/COEP) modes, which CI does not exercise.

Encryption-at-rest + purge lifecycle (E-OFF-2) on a data-collection product is a two-edged risk: a wrong key lifecycle can lock participants out of their own un-synced offline responses. The purge-and-destroy path must be proven against the sync ledger (E-OFF-5) so encryption never precedes a confirmed server ack.

The pull-forward tasks (P5-T2, P5-T3, P8-T1, P8-T3, P8-T10) break the committed wave order; if the release process treats the 16 waves as immutable, the foundational epics they unblock (E-FEEDBACK-1, E-REACT-3, E-OFF-4, E-FLOW-2, E-RBAC-9) will block. The wave plan must explicitly absorb these five reorderings.

Milestone M8 concentrates four XL/high epics across three different pillars (reaction, flow, RBAC) in one phase. Even though their dependencies are satisfied, staffing them concurrently is unrealistic; M8 likely needs to be split into two or three delivery slices by pillar to avoid a stalled final milestone.
synthesis.vision_summary: QDesigner is roughly halfway to its 2026 four-pillar vision (pillars at 55/63/50/44/44), and the striking pattern is that the distance is mostly activation, not greenfield: the hardest engineering — a sub-millisecond precision clock, a seeded reproducible flow engine, a CAT/IRT engine, a full psychometric scoring stack, a two-tier RBAC model, a version-pinned offline cache — already exists but is dead code (CATSession, analytics/ScoreInterpreter, the Permission enum) or wired-but-broken (resume() only unpauses, loop blocks self-collapse, participantNumber never reaches the runtime, CompletionScreen mounts without scores, ProjectRole is write-only). The path therefore front-loads a correctness-and-security foundation (remediation P1+P2 in M1, then the resumable-session backbone plus test/type harness in M2), because a single task — P1-T1 variable persistence — unblocks eight epics and the HTTP harness (P3-T2) plus the typed runtime contract (P4-T4) gate the entire enterprise and reaction tracks. From there each pillar gets one foundational milestone that is largely wiring: reaction capture + provenance (M3), feedback resurrection (M4), flow-plumbing activation with a11y and Svelte-5 debt (M5), and bulletproof offline (M6). Governance and tenant isolation land in M7, and the genuinely new external-standard frontier — SSO/SAML, SCIM, adaptive CAT in fillout, longitudinal EMA, and the nine-paradigm library — is deliberately last in M8. This shape keeps risk low through the middle because most milestones activate proven capability, and concentrates it in two places: the XL/high resume linchpin that two pillars depend on, and the five external-standard/IRT frontier epics whose estimates have the widest variance. The committed 16-wave remediation order survives almost intact — only five perf/refactor tasks pull forward to unblock foundational epics, and E-RBAC-1 is reclassified as security rather than a deferrable feature. If the milestones land, the four pillars reach 95/95/95/90/90; the honest caveat is that CI-green never proved anything works here, so every participant-facing milestone lives or dies on real-browser offline QA.

tasks.title: Persist derived variables and quality report through the offline-first pipeline
tasks.scope: Delete FilloutRuntime's second, never-populated VariableEngine and read the real engine QuestionnaireRuntime computes into; route all variable saves through OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable (IndexedDB-first, D2) instead of the server-only api.sessions.upsertVariable; at completion persist the final variable snapshot plus session.metadata.qualityReport into the Dexie rows FilloutSyncEngine already ships; teach the server sync endpoint to merge session metadata into an EXISTING session row (today it only inserts); and guard the two redundant unconditional server round-trips (startSession, per-response updateSessionProgress) behind navigator.onLine. Persisting computed values also closes the F059 reproducibility concern (values are stored, never recomputed).
tasks.approach: QuestionnaireRuntime gets `public getVariableEngine(): VariableEngine { return this.variableEngine; }` — the engine populated by initializeVariables (:336-368) and updateQuestionVariables (:1078-1081). FilloutRuntime deletes its `variableEngine` field + `new VariableEngine()` (:54) and the @qdesigner/scripting-engine import; `getVariables()` delegates to `this.runtime.getVariableEngine().getAllVariables()` (keys are variable NAMES — same names {{}} interpolation sees). `updateProgress()` (:238-241) writes each name→value via `OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable(this.sessionId, name, value)` — Dexie PK `[sessionId+name]` (indexeddb.ts:181/:200) makes this an upsert that re-arms synced:0 — then fire-and-forget `syncEngine.syncNow()`. Rewrite `ResponsePersistenceService.saveVariable` to that offline-first pattern (mirror saveResponse :55-91) and delete the api.sessions.upsertVariable call; this single change activates the already-wired getUnsyncedVariables → payload.variables → markVariablesSynced chain in FilloutSyncEngine (:176,:202-205,:237) and the protectedVersionKeys unsynced-variable branch. In `handleComplete(session)` (:259-271), before completeSession(): persist every entry of `session.variables` (pushed by QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() :1202-1209) via OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable, and call new `OfflineSessionService.mergeMetadata(session.id, { qualityReport: session.metadata?.qualityReport, custom: session.metadata?.custom })` which does `db.filloutSessions.update(id, { metadata: {...existing, ...patch}, synced: 0 })` so FilloutSyncEngine.collectSessionsToSync picks the session up and its payload `session.metadata` (FilloutSyncEngine :220) carries the report. Server: sync_session today only INSERTs ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (:2884-2904), so metadata for online-created sessions is dropped — after ensure_session_participant_or_member (:2909) add: if `body.session.as_ref().and_then(|i| i.metadata.as_ref())` is Some and the session pre-existed, run `UPDATE sessions SET metadata = COALESCE(metadata,'{}'::jsonb) || $2 WHERE id = $1` inside the same tx (the 00021 dual UPDATE policy admits via the app.session_id GUC set by fillout_rls_context from the URL path, so anonymous sync works). F058: wrap SessionManagementService.startSession (FilloutRuntime :90) and the api.sessions.update inside updateSessionProgress in `if (navigator.onLine)` — FilloutSyncEngine materializes sessions idempotently at sync time so nothing is lost.
files: apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/QuestionnaireRuntime.ts (add public getVariableEngine() accessor near getSession/getProgress ~line 325; engine is the private field at :70/:123)
apps/web/src/lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime.ts (:4,:31,:54 remove duplicate engine; :88-94 startSession guard; :238-241 updateProgress; :259-271 handleComplete; :324-326 getVariables)
apps/server/src/api/sessions.rs (sync_session :2847-2909: metadata merge for existing sessions)
tasks.corrected_refs: FilloutRuntime.ts: duplicate `variableEngine` field at :31 + `new VariableEngine()` at :54 + import at :4 (all accurate); startSession :88-94; updateProgress :213-242 (variable loop :238-241); handleComplete :259-271; getVariables :324-326 — ALL accurate. ResponsePersistenceService.saveVariable :169-180 (current signature `saveVariable(name, value, type='string')`, called at FilloutRuntime:240 as `saveVariable(name, value, typeof value)`); updateSessionProgress :129-144 — accurate. OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable signature is `(sessionId, name, value)` :96-105 — matches plan's call form. IMPORTANT CORRECTION: QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() pushes entries shaped `{ variableId, value, timestamp }` (NOT `{name,...}`) at :1204-1208 — so handleComplete must read `entry.variableId` as the variable name. QuestionnaireRuntime private field is `variableEngine` at :70/:123; nearest public accessor to co-locate getVariableEngine() is getProgress() at :325 (there is NO public getSession — FilloutRuntime reaches session via an unsafe cast). step_by_step: QuestionnaireRuntime.ts: add `public getVariableEngine(): VariableEngine { return this.variableEngine; }` immediately after getProgress() (~:333). The engine (:70/:123) is the one populated by initializeVariables (:336-402) and updateQuestionVariables (:1078-1081).

FilloutRuntime.ts: delete the `variableEngine` field (:31), the `new VariableEngine()` construction (:54), and the `import { VariableEngine } from '@qdesigner/scripting-engine'` (:4). Rewrite getVariables() (:324-326) to `return this.runtime.getVariableEngine().getAllVariables();` (keys are the same variable names {{}}/${} interpolation resolves).

FilloutRuntime.ts updateProgress(): keep the existing `for (const [name, value] of Object.entries(variables))` loop (:238-241) but have it call the offline-first path. ResponsePersistenceService.ts: rewrite saveVariable (:169-180), dropping the `api.sessions.upsertVariable` call, to `await OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable(this.sessionId, name, value)` then `if (navigator.onLine) void this.syncEngine.syncNow();` (mirror saveResponse :55-91). The Dexie `[sessionId+name]` PK makes the put() an upsert that re-arms synced:0. FilloutRuntime.ts F058 guards: wrap the body of startSession() (:89-93 SessionManagementService.startSession) in `if (navigator.onLine)`. In ResponsePersistenceService.updateSessionProgress (:135-143) wrap the `api.sessions.update` in `if (navigator.onLine)`. FilloutSyncEngine materializes sessions idempotently at sync time so nothing is lost offline.

OfflineSessionService.ts: add `static async mergeMetadata(sessionId: string, patch: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>` that reads the row (`const s = await db.filloutSessions.get(sessionId); if (!s) return;`), then `await db.filloutSessions.update(sessionId, { metadata: { ...(s.metadata ?? {}), ...patch }, synced: 0 })` (mirror updateProgress :99-107). synced:0 makes collectSessionsToSync pick it up.

FilloutRuntime.ts handleComplete() (:259-271): BEFORE `await this.persistenceService.completeSession()`, persist the final snapshot: `for (const v of session.variables) { await OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable(this.sessionId, v.variableId, v.value); }` (note field is `variableId`), then `await OfflineSessionService.mergeMetadata(session.id, { qualityReport: session.metadata?.qualityReport, custom: session.metadata?.custom });`. Import OfflineResponsePersistence + OfflineSessionService into FilloutRuntime. This must run before config.onComplete (:265) so metadata is set before the page's syncNow() fires.

Server sessions.rs sync_session: after ensure_session_participant_or_member (:2909), add a metadata-merge for the pre-existing case: `if let Some(init) = &body.session { if let Some(meta) = &init.metadata { sqlx::query("UPDATE sessions SET metadata = COALESCE(metadata,'{}'::jsonb) || $2 WHERE id = $1").bind(session_id).bind(meta).execute(&mut **tx).await?; } }`. This runs under the fillout GUC (app.session_id from URL path) so the 00021 dual UPDATE policy admits anonymous sync. Runs unconditionally (both the freshly-inserted and pre-existing paths) — the `|| $2` merge is idempotent.
Extend apps/web offline-roundtrip.test.ts + OfflineSessionService.test.ts and apps/server sync_session_dedup.rs per verification.

Extend OfflineSessionService.test.ts: mergeMetadata merges into existing metadata (preserves prior keys) and resets synced→0; no-op when the row is absent.

Repeat with DevTools offline through completion, then go online → verify FilloutSyncEngine lands both the variables and the merged metadata.
tasks.precision_notes: Field-name gotcha: session.variables entries are `{variableId, value, timestamp}` — use variableId, not name. The two-engine bug is real: FilloutRuntime's own VariableEngine is never fed (zero setVariable callers), so getVariables() returns {} today and the updateProgress loop never iterates — delegating to runtime.getVariableEngine() fixes the root cause. For an ONLINE session, `body.session` init IS present in the sync payload (the local pin row from recordServerSession at +page.svelte:386 carries questionnaireId+metadata), and `already`==true so the INSERT branch is skipped — hence the metadata merge must be OUTSIDE/AFTER that INSERT branch. mergeMetadata works for both online (row exists via recordServerSession) and offline (row exists via createSession) sessions. Watch the ordering hazard already documented at FilloutRuntime:218-225 — do not re-assert status here.

tasks.title: Fix broken HSL-triplet CSS tokens on participant fillout screens
tasks.scope: All participant-facing fillout chrome currently feeds bare HSL triplets (e.g. `background: var(--muted)` → `background: 210 40% 96.1%`, an invalid declaration that is dropped) into scoped component CSS: the welcome instructions box, consent muted surfaces/signature input border/destructive text, language-picker pill, the loading progress bar, and the .form-card container that hosts every form question renders with no background over the canvas. Wrap every raw color token in hsl(var(--token)) and remove the never-engaging hex fallbacks. Fillout participant surfaces ONLY — the design-system-wide token sweep belongs to P8.
tasks.approach: Tokens are defined as bare triplets in lib/styles/themes/variables.css (`--muted: 210 40% 96.1%`) and only become colors via hsl() (app.css @theme inline confirms). Delete the dead fallbacks `var(--card, #ffffff)` / `var(--card-foreground, #0f172a)` (:856-857) and `var(--primary, #2563eb)` / `var(--primary-foreground, #ffffff)` (:873-874) — the fallback branch never engages because the custom property IS defined (as a triplet). Then grep the participant tree (`lib/fillout`, `routes/(fillout)`, `lib/runtime/timing/components`) for remaining raw `var(--` color usages and fix stragglers (skip non-color tokens like --radius). Do not touch designer/app/module surfaces.

tasks.title: Resolve question-id flow-control targets to their containing page
tasks.scope: FlowControlManager offers question ids as skip/branch/loop targets (labelled 'Q: …'), but QuestionnaireRuntime.handleFlowControl resolves targets exclusively via pages.findIndex(page.id === target), so a question target silently no-ops into linear next-page navigation. Add a target resolver that maps a question id to the index of the page containing it, use it in all three rule branches, and warn loudly when a target resolves nowhere instead of falling through silently.
tasks.approach: New exported pure function `resolveFlowTargetPageIndex(pages: Page[], target: string): number` in flowTarget.ts: (1) `pages.findIndex(p => p.id === target)`; (2) else find the page whose `questions` array contains target OR whose `blocks` contain it — the exact containment predicate FlowControlManager.svelte:44-49 already uses to label targets (`p.questions?.includes(id) || p.blocks?.some(b => b.questions.includes(id))`); (3) else -1. Pure module (no runtime construction needed) because QuestionnaireRuntime's constructor requires canvas/WebGL and has no existing test harness. In handleFlowControl replace both inline `this.config.questionnaire.pages.findIndex(...)` calls with the helper; when a rule has a target and the helper returns -1, `console.warn('[flow] rule %s target %s matches no page or question — falling through to next page', matchingRule.id, matchingRule.target)` before the default `navigateToPage(currentPageIndex + 1)`. No designer change — its question targets become honored.
tasks.corrected_refs: flowTarget.ts confirmed ABSENT (new file). FlowControlManager.svelte containment predicate is at :47-48 (plan said :44-49): `p.questions?.includes(question.id) || p.blocks?.some((b) => b.questions.includes(question.id))`; question targets pushed with type:'question' (:54) and label `Q: …` (:53). QuestionnaireRuntime.handleFlowControl at :1128-1169 — loop branch findIndex at :1144-1146 (guarded :1141-1151), skip/branch findIndex at :1157-1159 (guarded :1155-1165), silent fall-through default `navigateToPage(currentPageIndex + 1)` at :1168 — ALL accurate.
step_by_step: Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/flowTarget.ts exporting a pure `resolveFlowTargetPageIndex(pages: Page[], target: string): number`: (1) `const byPage = pages.findIndex(p => p.id === target); if (byPage >= 0) return byPage;` (2) else `return pages.findIndex(p => p.questions?.includes(target) || p.blocks?.some(b => b.questions.includes(target)));` (returns -1 if none). Import the Page type from $lib/shared. Page-id match wins over an identically-named question because it is checked first.

handleFlowControl loop branch (:1143-1151): replace the inline `this.config.questionnaire.pages.findIndex(page => page.id === matchingRule.target)` with `resolveFlowTargetPageIndex(this.config.questionnaire.pages, matchingRule.target)`, keep the `>= 0` guard + navigateToPage(targetIndex).

tasks.title: Real per-cell quota counting server-side; enforce offline; stop failing open
tasks.scope: Two server defects: (a) quota_status pushes total_completed as every cell's count, ignoring quota conditions; (b) WORSE — discovered during planning: quota_status counts sessions on the bare app pool (&state.pool) with no RLS GUC, and the 00021 dual-path SELECT policy hides every row from qdesigner_app there, so total_completed is ALWAYS 0 in production and quotas are completely inert. Fix with aggregate-only SECURITY DEFINER counting functions plus per-quota condition evaluation against completed sessions' urlParams/session-variables. Client side: cache the last good quota snapshot so the offline start path can enforce quotas, and stop swallowing quota-check failures as silent allows.
tasks.approach: Migration 00027 creates two functions owned by the migration role `qdesigner` (table owner; `sessions` is not FORCE'd, so owner-definer bypasses RLS — the same posture reasoning as ADR 0013/0022): `fillout_completed_count(qid uuid) RETURNS bigint` and `fillout_quota_condition_count(qid uuid, var_name text, op text, cmp_value text) RETURNS bigint`, both `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp`, `REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app`. The condition variant counts completed sessions where `val := COALESCE(s.metadata->'urlParams'->>var_name, (SELECT sv.variable_value #>> '{}' FROM session_variables sv WHERE sv.session_id = s.id AND sv.variable_name = var_name))` satisfies op: ==/!= as text equality, >,<,>=,<= as numeric compare guarded by a numeric-regex check so bad data counts as non-match. Aggregate-only return (a count) so nothing per-session leaks through the definer. quota_status: keep the settings load, replace the loop body — parse each enabled quota's `condition` with the SAME grammar the client uses (QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition :99-157: trim; 'true'/'1' catch-all → completed_count; regex ^(\w+)\s*(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)\s*(.+)$ with quoted-string stripping) and call fillout_quota_condition_count per quota; unparseable condition → fall back to fillout_completed_count (mirrors client allow-by-default) with a tracing::warn. total_completed also switches to the definer fn (fixing the always-0 bug). Client: fetchQuotaStatus caches every successful snapshot to localStorage key `qd-quota-status:{questionnaireId}` ({fetchedAt, statuses}) and THROWS on !res.ok/network error instead of returning []; checkQuotas catches, falls back to the cached snapshot, and when neither exists returns `{allowed: true, unchecked: true, fullQuotas: []}` (add `unchecked?: boolean` to QuotaCheckResult). +page.svelte :417: drop the `navigator.onLine &&` gate so offline starts evaluate against the cached snapshot; while online retry the check once on failure; when the result is `unchecked`, record `quotaUnchecked: true` into the session metadata (online create payload :341-352 / offline createSession metadata arg :397-405) instead of silently proceeding, so researchers can filter unverified completions.
tasks.corrected_refs: quota_status at :2651-2723 (accurate); confirmed BOTH defects — the per-quota loop pushes `current: total_completed` for every enabled quota (:2706-2715) ignoring each quota's condition, AND both counts run on `&state.pool` (:2660,:2669) with NO Tx/GUC, so the 00021 dual-path SELECT policy hides all sessions from qdesigner_app → total_completed is ALWAYS 0 in production. Quota route mounted in questionnaire_routes at mod.rs:175 (`/{id}/quota-status`), which uses the set_rls_context layer (:185) and is anonymous-reachable (sits beside the public by-code + condition-counts routes). Highest existing migration is 00026_session_fingerprint.sql, so 00027 is the correct next number. QuotaService.ts fetchQuotaStatus :33-51, checkQuotas :53-97, QuotaCheckResult :3-9, evaluateQuotaCondition :99-157 — trust plan's line refs (not re-read this pass). +page.svelte quota block :415-438, online create metadata :341-352, offline createSession metadata arg :397-405.
step_by_step: Create apps/server/migrations/00027_quota_counting.sql (owner = qdesigner migration role): `fillout_completed_count(qid uuid) RETURNS bigint` and `fillout_quota_condition_count(qid uuid, var_name text, op text, cmp_value text) RETURNS bigint`, both `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp`; then `REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION … FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE … TO qdesigner_app`. Owner-definer bypasses RLS because `sessions` is not FORCE'd (same posture as ADR 0013/0022). Aggregate-only returns (a bigint count) so no per-session row crosses the definer boundary.

In fillout_quota_condition_count, resolve the value per completed session as `val := COALESCE(s.metadata->'urlParams'->>var_name, (SELECT sv.variable_value #>> '{}' FROM session_variables sv WHERE sv.session_id = s.id AND sv.variable_name = var_name))`; apply op: ==/!= as text equality; >,<,>=,<= as numeric compare guarded by a numeric-regex (`val ~ '^-?[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$'` AND cmp numeric) so non-numeric data counts as non-match. COUNT over `status='completed' AND questionnaire_id=qid`.

sessions.rs quota_status: keep the settings load (:2656-2662) and the quota-group extraction (:2673-2686). Replace the per-quota loop body (:2706-2715): for each enabled quota parse its `condition` with the SAME grammar as the client (QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition :99-157 — trim; 'true'/'1' catch-all; regex `^(\w+)\s*(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)\s*(.+)$` with quoted-string stripping) and call fillout_quota_condition_count(qid, name, op, value); an unparseable/catch-all condition falls back to fillout_completed_count(qid) with a tracing::warn.

sessions.rs: switch total_completed (:2665-2670) to `SELECT fillout_completed_count($1)` (fixes the always-0 bug). quota_status can stay `State`-only (the definer functions provide visibility on the bare pool) — no Tx signature change required.

QuotaService.ts fetchQuotaStatus: on success cache the snapshot to localStorage key `qd-quota-status:{questionnaireId}` as `{fetchedAt, statuses}`; on !res.ok or network error THROW (stop returning []).

QuotaService.ts checkQuotas: wrap the fetch; on throw fall back to the cached snapshot; when neither live nor cached exists return `{allowed: true, unchecked: true, fullQuotas: []}`. Add `unchecked?: boolean` to QuotaCheckResult (:3-9).

+page.svelte quota block (:415-438): drop the `navigator.onLine &&` gate at :417 so offline starts evaluate against the cached snapshot; while online, retry the check once on failure; when the result carries `unchecked`, write `quotaUnchecked: true` into session metadata (online create payload metadata at :341-352, offline createSession metadata arg at :397-405) instead of silently proceeding.
Create apps/server/tests/quota_counting.rs and apps/web QuotaService.test.ts per verification.
verification: cargo test --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml quota_counting -- --include-ignored (fixture pool via DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS per tests/rbac_integration.rs): seed a published questionnaire with settings.quotas (gender==male target 2, gender==female target 2) + 3 completed sessions metadata.urlParams.gender male/male/female + 1 in-progress → per-cell counts 2/1, total 3. Then SET LOCAL ROLE to a fresh non-owner test role and assert EXECUTE on the fn succeeds while `SELECT count(*) FROM sessions` returns 0 (proves the definer is the only aggregate window).

pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test QuotaService.test.ts (mock fetch + localStorage): per-cell isFull only blocks the matching condition; fetch failure → cached snapshot used; no cache → unchecked flag set.
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && build; cargo check + cargo build --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml.
Live QA (required): publish with a male-cell target of 1; complete one ?gender=male session; a second ?gender=male visit hits the over-quota screen while ?gender=female proceeds.
tasks.precision_notes: The always-0 GUC bug is the more severe of the two and is confirmed by direct reading (bare &state.pool + no set_config). Keep the client/server grammar in lockstep — divergence would let the client believe a cell is full while the server counts it differently. Migration number 00027 is correct as of now but if a parallel phase lands a migration first, renumber to the next free slot. depends_on P1-T2 is cosmetic-only (the over-quota screen uses fillout tokens fixed in T2) — no code coupling; depends_on P1-T1 is unrelated to files here (likely sequencing only). Consider a numeric-safe comparison helper shared with T5's stats function since both parse session_variables/urlParams values.

tasks.title: Anonymous-safe cohort feedback path (public aggregate stats endpoint)
tasks.scope: Cohort and participant-vs-cohort statistical-feedback modes are dead for participants: /api/sessions/aggregate|compare take the AuthenticatedUser extractor (401 before RLS) and verify_questionnaire_access, so the engine's fetch fails and the panel renders a null 'No data' point. Add a public, aggregate-only cohort-stats endpoint for published questionnaires (min-N floored), rewire the feedback engine's cohort modes onto it using the participant's own local variable value for the participant point, and explicitly gate participant-vs-participant as researcher-only with a designer warning and an honest runtime message.
tasks.approach: Migration 00028 adds `fillout_cohort_stats(qid uuid, source text, key text) RETURNS TABLE(n bigint, mean float8, std_dev float8, min float8, max float8, median float8, p90 float8, p95 float8, p99 float8)` — SECURITY DEFINER owned by qdesigner (same grants/search_path posture as P1-T4's functions), computing percentile_cont/avg/stddev over COMPLETED sessions' numeric values, mirroring load_numeric_samples' source resolution (sessions.rs :2191-2237: source='variable' → session_variable_index.numeric_value with session_variables fallback; source='response' → the responses branch). Aggregate-only in SQL so per-session values never cross the definer boundary. Handler `public_cohort_stats(State, Path(id), Query{source, key})`: verify the questionnaire exists AND status='published' (mirror sync_session's gate :2853-2859) else 404; call the fn; apply a min-N floor — n < 5 returns n plus null stats (documented in the utoipa annotation) to prevent small-cohort deanonymization; respond with the SessionAggregateResponse-compatible stats shape the client already parses. Mount in questionnaire_routes next to quota-status (public, set_rls_context layer is fine since the definer fn provides visibility). Client: sessionAnalytics gains `aggregatePublic(questionnaireId, source, key)` using raw fetch against `${VITE_API_URL}/api/questionnaires/{id}/cohort-stats` (same pattern as QuotaService — avoids an OpenAPI contract regen); engine.ts 'cohort' mode calls it in runtime mode; 'participant-vs-cohort' builds the participant point from the LOCAL runtime `variables` (resolveVariableValue on dataSource.currentVariable || key — the participant's own computed score is already client-side, and anonymous callers could never read it back via /aggregate anyway) plus cohort stats from aggregatePublic, computing zScore client-side exactly as buildParticipantVsCohortSeries does (:113-121). 'participant-vs-participant' keeps sessionAnalyticsService.compare but throws a clear 'Participant comparison requires a signed-in researcher and is not shown to participants' so StatisticalFeedback.svelte's loadError (:45-62) surfaces an honest message instead of 'No data'. Designer: warning paragraph rendered when sourceMode === 'participant-vs-participant' stating it will not render for anonymous participants.
files: apps/server/src/api/sessions.rs (new public_cohort_stats handler beside quota_status; reuse NumericStatsSummary/compute_numeric_stats shapes)

tasks.title: Unify ${} and {{}} variable interpolation on the live runtime path
tasks.scope: TextDisplay runs a second ${var} interpolation pass against the scriptingEngine singleton whose variable store nothing ever populates (zero setVariable callers in runtime/fillout), while ModularRenderer already interpolates {{var}} with the real runtime variables — and the designer help text teaches the broken ${} syntax. Unify on the shared variableInterpolation service: support both syntaxes there, feed TextDisplay/TextInstruction from the variables prop ModularRenderer already passes, delete the dead singleton passes, and fix the designer help text.
tasks.approach: variableInterpolation.interpolateVariables gains a second replace pass over /\$\{([^}]+)\}/g routed through the identical lookup: `expression in variables` → formatValue, else evaluateExpression via FormulaParser/ASTEvaluator, else leave the original text (extract the shared substitution callback so both regexes use one implementation). hasInterpolation matches either syntax; extractVariables scans both. Because ModularRenderer.processedItem already calls this function on text/instruction/config strings (:122-134) with the live runtime variables (activePresentation.variables = QuestionnaireRuntime's engine.getAllVariables()), extending the shared function makes ${totalScore} work everywhere {{totalScore}} already does. TextDisplay: destructure the `variables = {}` prop (ModularRenderer passes it in base componentProps :172-176; add it to InstructionProps), replace `scriptingEngine.interpolateVariables(content)` (:56) with the shared `interpolateVariables(content, variables)` and drop the scriptingEngine import — the pass stays so directly-mounted previews still interpolate, and re-running on already-substituted text is a no-op for plain values. TextInstruction: replace `scriptingEngine.getAllVariables()` (:109) with its `variables` prop.
