tasks.title: Harden main-thread + worker script sandboxes against bracket-notation constructor escapes
tasks.scope: Broaden the static escape guard to catch computed/quoted constructor & prototype access in all three sandbox implementations, resolve the inert strict-mode guard in the worker, and document the residual main-thread limits (full worker migration is blocked by the synchronous hook contract).
tasks.approach: (1) Broaden BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS (ScriptExecutor.ts:11-12) and BANNED_PATTERN (ScriptWorker.ts:45, ScriptEngine.ts:217) to also match computed/quoted access, e.g. /(?:\.\s*|\[\s*['"])(constructor|prototype|__proto__|__define[GS]etter__|__lookup[GS]etter__)\b/ , which rejects the confirmed PoC `[]['constructor']['constructor']('return this')()` and `["__proto__"]`. (2) Resolve F004 honestly: `"use strict"` is illegal together with `with(sandbox)`, so the current placement inside the with-body (ScriptWorker.ts:172-173) is inert by design. Either remove it with an explanatory comment (keeping the Proxy `with` free-variable trap) OR refactor ScriptWorker/ScriptEngine to pass SAFE_GLOBALS as named `new Function` parameters plus a real `"use strict"` prologue and drop `with` (recommended — they only need the known globals). (3) Add a worker-egress hardening note recommending a `connect-src 'none'` CSP since the worker escape's stated impact is exfiltrating the passed `context`. Update ScriptExecutor.security.test.ts (the comments at :29-30/:79-81 currently DOCUMENT the gap) to assert the bracket-notation PoC now returns {} / is rejected; add an equivalent ScriptWorker sandbox test. Add a code comment + finding note that fully isolating ScriptExecutor would require moving hook execution into the worker, which is blocked by the synchronous hook API consumed at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:748/942/995 (executeOnMount/executeOnValidate results used synchronously) — recommend as a follow-up async refactor, out of scope here.
tasks.corrected_refs: ScriptExecutor.ts: BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS dot-only regex at :11-12; new Function with(sandbox) at :224; `"use strict";` is INSIDE the with-body at :219 so it is inert HERE too, not only the worker. ScriptWorker.ts: BANNED_PATTERN at :45; inert `'"use strict";'+script` inside with(sandbox) at :172-173. CORRECTION: plan's 'ScriptEngine.ts:217' is NOT a BANNED_PATTERN — :217 is the validate()-only `new Function('sandbox','with(sandbox){"use strict";...}')` that is constructed for SYNTAX validation and NEVER invoked; ScriptEngine.execute() delegates to ScriptWorker (:96). So there are TWO live sandboxes (ScriptExecutor main-thread hooks + ScriptWorker), not three. Hook callsites confirmed: QuestionnaireRuntime.ts executeOnMount :748 & :789, executeOnValidate :942 & :995, executeOnResponse :975 & :1036, executeOnNavigate :523 — all synchronous.
step_by_step: Broaden BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS (ScriptExecutor.ts:11-12) to match computed/quoted access, e.g. `/(?:\.\s*|\[\s*["'])(constructor|prototype|__proto__|__define[GS]etter__|__lookup[GS]etter__)\b/`, keeping the dotted form.
Apply the same broadened regex to BANNED_PATTERN in the worker source string ScriptWorker.ts:45 (it is a JS string with doubled backslashes — escape accordingly).

Resolve F004 in ScriptWorker.ts: recommended — drop `with(sandbox)`, pass SAFE_GLOBALS keys + context keys as named Function params with a REAL `"use strict";` prologue; minimal — remove the inert prefix at :172 with a comment that strict mode is illegal with `with` and the Proxy has-trap is the real guard.
Apply the same F004 treatment to ScriptExecutor.ts:218-222 (its :219 `"use strict"` is equally inert).
Optionally add the broadened check to ScriptEngine.ts validate() (:198-221) for consistency, documenting it is syntax-only and not an execution sink.

Add a code comment (ScriptExecutor + finding writeup) that isolating main-thread hooks requires moving execution into the worker, blocked by the synchronous hook contract at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:748/789/942/975/995/1036 — recommend async refactor, out of scope.
Add a worker-egress note recommending `connect-src 'none'` CSP (none exists today) since the worker escape's impact is exfiltrating context via fetch/XHR/importScripts.

Update ScriptExecutor.security.test.ts: the :29-30 and :79-81 comments currently DOCUMENT the gap — change bracket-notation cases to assert rejection; add a test that `[]['constructor']['constructor']('return this')()` yields {} (setVariable never called); add an equivalent ScriptWorker sandbox test in scripting-engine asserting the same PoC is rejected.
Run pnpm --filter @qdesigner/scripting-engine test && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test && check && build.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/scripting-engine test (new ScriptWorker sandbox test rejecting the bracket-notation PoC)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (updated ScriptExecutor.security.test.ts asserting `[]['constructor']['constructor']('return this')()` yields {} / no setVariable)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && build
PR body: residual risk — string-concat obfuscation (`['con'+'structor']`) still defeats a static regex on the main thread; only the worker has a hard terminate() timeout
tasks.precision_notes: The plan's third target (ScriptEngine.ts:217) is a validate-only Function never called, so hardening it changes no runtime behavior; real sinks are ScriptExecutor.ts:224 and ScriptWorker.ts:173. The inert `"use strict"` (F004) exists in BOTH ScriptExecutor.ts:219 and ScriptWorker.ts:172 — fix/document both. The worker source is an embedded template literal with escaped backslashes; broadening BANNED_PATTERN requires correct double-escaping or the regex won't compile as intended. Regex broadening is partial (concatenation bypass remains) — keep the honest residual-risk note.

tasks.title: Purge sensitive fillout data from IndexedDB after sync + shared-device clear
tasks.scope: Purge participant response/variable/event data from IndexedDB after a successful final sync and via an explicit shared-device 'end session / clear local data' action, without ever dropping unsynced records.
tasks.approach: Add `db.purgeSyncedSessionData(sessionId)` deleting only rows with `synced === 1` across filloutSessions/filloutResponses/filloutEvents/filloutVariables for that session, and `db.clearAllFilloutData()` for the shared-device action. Call purgeSyncedSessionData from FilloutSyncEngine after the server acks the session+responses+events+variables (post-markSynced). Add a visible 'End session / clear this device' control on the completion screen in (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte wired to clearAllFilloutData, with an offline/unsynced warning. COORDINATION: this is adjacent to the offline-persistence findings F055–F057 (owned by another phase) that make variables persist+sync — purge MUST run strictly after sync ack and only on synced rows so it does not undermine those fixes; land after or in coordination with that work.
tasks.corrected_refs: Mostly accurate. indexeddb.ts: fillout tables at :154-157; schema is at version(3) (:190), not v2 as CLAUDE.md states — no bump needed to add methods. Indexes support efficient purge: filloutResponses/filloutEvents have `[sessionId+synced]` (:198-199), filloutVariables has `sessionId, synced` (:200); synced:0|1 at :111/:124/:135/:142. An existing `clearAllData()` at :436 (designer tables) — name the new one clearAllFilloutData(). FilloutSyncEngine.syncSession marks synced at :235-240 (markResponsesSynced/markEventsSynced/markVariablesSynced then OfflineSessionService.markSynced) — correct post-ack purge point is right after :240.
step_by_step: In indexeddb.ts add `async purgeSyncedSessionData(sessionId)`: in a rw transaction over the 4 fillout tables, delete only synced===1 rows for that sessionId using `.where('[sessionId+synced]').equals([sessionId,1]).delete()` for responses/events, the sessionId+synced filter for variables, and delete the filloutSessions row only if its synced===1.

Add `async clearAllFilloutData()` clearing filloutSessions/filloutResponses/filloutEvents/filloutVariables (decide whether to also clear filloutQuestionnaires cache; document). Follow the clearAllData() pattern at :436.

In FilloutSyncEngine.syncSession, after markSynced (:240), call `await db.purgeSyncedSessionData(session.id)` GATED on `session.status === 'completed'` so in-progress/resumable sessions keep synced responses locally (see precision note).

In (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte add a visible 'End session / clear this device' control on the completion screen wired to db.clearAllFilloutData(); before clearing, probe for unsynced rows and show an offline/unsynced warning that blocks or confirms.
Add a vitest around purgeSyncedSessionData: seed mixed synced/unsynced rows across two session ids; assert unsynced survive, only synced rows of the target session removed, other session untouched.
Run pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && test && build.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && test (new purge test green) && build
Live QA online: complete a session, DevTools IndexedDB, confirm that session's rows cleared after sync ack
Live QA offline: complete offline, confirm rows retained; restore connectivity, confirm sync then purge
Live QA shared-device: 'End session / clear this device' clears all fillout tables and warns when queued data exists
tasks.precision_notes: COORDINATION with F055–F057 (offline variable persist+sync, other phase): purge must run strictly after sync ack and only on synced rows. CRITICAL edge the plan understates: purging synced responses of an in-progress resumable session can break resume/carry-forward, because the runtime rehydrates prior answers from IndexedDB on resume — so gate purge on session.status==='completed' (or invoke only from the completion path), NOT on every syncSession. Use the [sessionId+synced] compound index for efficient deletes. Schema is at version(3); adding methods needs no version bump.

tasks.title: Auth endpoint hardening: peer-IP rate key, verify-code lockout + send cap, dummy-Argon2, max password, 23505→409
tasks.scope: Rekey the auth rate limiter off the socket peer IP; add per-email verify-attempt lockout, send/resend cap, and a user-existence gate; run a dummy Argon2 verify on login misses to kill the timing oracle; bound password length; and map unique-violation (23505) races to 409 instead of 500.
tasks.approach: F006: in rate_limit_middleware (rate_limit.rs:81-104) replace the raw `x-forwarded-for` fallback with the socket peer IP read from `request.extensions().get::<axum::extract::ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>>()`; wire it by changing main.rs:206 to `axum::serve(listener, app.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>())`. Optionally add a trusted-proxy-hops config that indexes from the right of XFF, defaulting to peer IP. F009: add two RateLimiter instances to AppState (state.rs) constructed in main.rs — a send cap (~3/15min, key `authsend:{email}`) and a verify-attempt limiter (~5/15min, key `authverify:{email}`), reusing the existing Redis+in-memory RateLimiter so the in-memory fallback still enforces when Redis is down. tasks.title: Validate anonymous session media uploads (size, MIME allowlist, magic bytes, filename sanitize, body limit)
tasks.scope: Enforce a size cap, a MIME allowlist verified against magic bytes, drop the client filename from the S3 key, and bound the request body on the anonymous session media upload (and the shared upload_media path).
tasks.approach: Add `infer` (magic-byte sniffing) to Cargo.toml. Factor a shared `validate_upload(bytes, declared_content_type) -> Result<(mime, ext)>` and use it in upload_session_media (media.rs:526-600) and upload_media (:135-179). It must: reject over a max size (~25 MB) with 413/400; call `infer::get(&bytes)` and require the sniffed type be in an image/audio/video allowlist AND consistent with the declared content_type; return the canonical mime+extension. Replace the S3 key's raw filename component — build `sessions/{session_id}/media/{file_id}.{ext}` instead of `..._{filename}` (media.rs:571) so the untrusted client filename never lands in the key. Add a per-route `DefaultBodyLimit::max(...)` (or RequestBodyLimitLayer) on the `/{id}/media` route in mod.rs:221 so the multipart is bounded before `field.bytes()` buffers it.

No DefaultBodyLimit override; the /{id}/media route is mod.rs:221. Cargo.toml has no `infer` dependency.

In upload_session_media call validate_upload after assembling (filename,bytes,content_type) at :566-567, before S3 upload; use the returned ext for the key.

tasks.title: Router/transport security: WS token off URL, CatchPanic on auth+ws, dev router out of release, verify_domain DNS challenge
tasks.scope: Move the WS JWT out of the URL query into the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol handshake, add CatchPanicLayer to auth_routes and ws_route, compile the dev bootstrap router out of release builds, and replace verify_domain's unconditional auto-verify with a DNS TXT ownership challenge.
tasks.approach: F008: change ws_upgrade (handler.rs:25-34) to read the token from the `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header instead of `Query<WsQuery>` (delete WsQuery:18-22). Adopt a subprotocol scheme (e.g. two tokens `['qde-auth', <jwt>]`), parse the jwt, verify_access_token, and echo the accepted subprotocol back on the WebSocketUpgrade so the handshake completes. Update the two FE clients to pass the token via `new WebSocket(url, [...])` and drop `?token=`: ws.ts:85-86 and YjsProvider.ts:57-119. F085: add `.layer(CatchPanicLayer::new())` to auth_routes (mod.rs:43-46, after the rate_limit layer) and to ws_route (mod.rs:250) — keep this in mod.rs, NOT the base router in main.rs, to stay disjoint from P2-T5's main.rs edits. F012: change the dev-router mount (mod.rs:270-292) to `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` only and drop the DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED env escape; mirror in dev.rs dev_helpers_enabled() (:44-54). Replace verify_domain (organizations.rs:1495-1528) unconditional `UPDATE organization_domains SET verified_at=NOW()` with a real ownership proof: read/generate a per-domain challenge token, resolve TXT at `_qdesigner-verify.<domain>`, and set verified_at only when it matches (else 422/409). If adding a DNS resolver dependency is undesirable within this task, gate the resolver behind a documented feature and have the endpoint return 'pending' rather than auto-verify.
tasks.corrected_refs: Accurate. handler.rs: WsQuery{token} :18-22, ws_upgrade reads Query<WsQuery> + verify_access_token(&query.token) :25-33 (upgrade via ws.on_upgrade :33 — subprotocol must be echoed). mod.rs: auth_routes rate_limit layer :43-46 (add CatchPanicLayer after it), ws_route :250 (bare), dev-router gate :270-292 (cfg!(debug_assertions) || DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED). dev.rs dev_helpers_enabled() :44-54. organizations.rs verify_domain :1495-1528: has_org_role Admin :1505-1511, unconditional UPDATE ... verified_at=NOW() :1514-1521 ('In development, auto-verify' comment :1513). FE: ws.ts:86 `new WebSocket(\`${wsUrl}?token=...\`)`; YjsProvider.ts:119-120 builds `?token=` URL then new WebSocket(url) (wsUrl default :54-58).
step_by_step: F008 server: in handler.rs read the JWT from the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header instead of Query<WsQuery>; delete WsQuery (:18-22) and the Query import. Client sends `['qde-auth', <jwt>]`; parse the second protocol token, verify_access_token, and echo the accepted subprotocol back on the upgrade (ws.protocols(['qde-auth'])) so the handshake completes.

F008 clients: ws.ts:86 → `new WebSocket(wsUrl, ['qde-auth', this.token])` drop `?token=`; YjsProvider.ts:119-120 → `new WebSocket(this.wsUrl, ['qde-auth', this.token])` drop `?token=` (keep binaryType='arraybuffer' :121).
F085: in mod.rs add `.layer(CatchPanicLayer::new())` to auth_routes after the rate_limit layer (:46) and to ws_route (:250) — keep in mod.rs (not main.rs) to stay disjoint from P2-T5.
F012 dev-router: change the mod.rs gate (:270-278) to `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-only so the /api/dev nest compiles out of release; drop the DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED branch.
F012 dev.rs: mirror — dev_helpers_enabled() (:44-54) → cfg!(debug_assertions) only, or gate the module behind #[cfg(debug_assertions)].

F012 verify_domain: replace the unconditional UPDATE (:1514-1521) with a DNS TXT ownership proof at _qdesigner-verify.<domain>, setting verified_at only on match (else 422/409). If adding a resolver (hickory-resolver) is undesirable here, gate it behind a documented cargo feature and return 'pending' instead of auto-verifying — the key requirement is no unconditional auto-verify.
Run cargo check + test --include-ignored + `cargo build --release`; confirm /api/dev/bootstrap-personas is 404 in release.
Live QA (mandatory): designer Yjs connects with NO ?token= in the WS URL (subprotocol handshake) and reconnects after a drop; verify_domain not-verified without the TXT record, verified with it.
verification: cargo check + cargo test --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml -- --include-ignored
cargo build --release then curl release /api/dev/bootstrap-personas → 404 (compiled out)
Live QA: designer Yjs connects with no ?token= in the WS URL and reconnects after a forced drop
Live QA: verify_domain returns not-verified/pending without the _qdesigner-verify TXT record; verified with it present
tasks.precision_notes: Sec-WebSocket-Protocol requires the server to ECHO an accepted subprotocol or browsers fail the connection — don't forget the echo. Both FE clients (ws.ts AND YjsProvider.ts, the live designer path) must change together. CatchPanicLayer on ws_route catches only the synchronous upgrade handler; panics inside spawned handle_socket tasks (:47+) are already isolated by tokio::spawn — defense-in-depth on the upgrade only. #[cfg(debug_assertions)] compile-out is stronger than the plan's env-drop and removes DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED entirely. verify_domain runs inside the RLS tx (Tx extractor) — a DNS lookup inside the guard.lock holds the DB connection during network I/O; resolve DNS outside the guard scope or be mindful of tx duration.

tasks.title: Constrain anonymous sessions_insert_dual so user_id cannot be forged
tasks.scope: Add WITH CHECK constraints so the anonymous-admitting branches of sessions_insert_dual require sessions.user_id IS NULL, and the authenticated branch requires user_id = current_app_user_id(), closing the defense-in-depth gap where RLS would not stop an anonymous INSERT that set a victim's user_id.
tasks.approach: New migration 00027 (00026 is latest) drops and recreates the sessions_insert_dual policy from 00021:111-124: keep the authenticated branch as `current_app_user_id() IS NOT NULL AND sessions.user_id = current_app_user_id()`; add `AND sessions.user_id IS NULL` to BOTH anonymous branches — the session-GUC branch (currently :116-117) and the bootstrap branch (currently :122-123). This is compatible with the live handler which writes user_id=NULL for anonymous callers (sessions.rs:409). Add an rls_enforcement.rs assertion: an anonymous INSERT with a non-null user_id is denied, a NULL-user_id anonymous INSERT is admitted.
tasks.corrected_refs: Accurate. Latest migration is 00026_session_fingerprint.sql, so the new file is 00027 as planned. 00021 sessions_insert_dual at :111-124: authenticated branch :114-115, session-GUC anonymous branch :116-117, bootstrap anonymous branch :122-123. tasks.title: Move refresh token to httpOnly cookie; keep only in-memory access token
tasks.scope: Store the refresh token in an httpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie set by the server; keep only the short-lived access token in memory on the client; stop persisting either token in localStorage. Depends on P2-T5 because both edit auth.rs and models.rs.
tasks.approach: Backend: enable the axum-extra `cookie` feature (Cargo.toml). In login (auth.rs:175-188), register (:81-93) and refresh (:201-262) return `(CookieJar, Json<AuthResponse>)` setting a refresh_token cookie `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/api/auth; Max-Age=<refresh exp>`; refresh reads the token from the cookie (fall back to RefreshRequest body during transition — make it Optional in models.rs:82-84), rotates it via the existing session::revoke_refresh_token/store_refresh_token logic, sets the new cookie, and drops refresh_token from the JSON body (or leaves it empty) so JS never receives it. logout clears the cookie. Frontend: set `credentials: 'include'` on authApiClient (auth.ts:13-20) and generatedClient (runtime.ts:6-16) so the cookie flows. In auth.ts stop persisting tokens: storeSession (:322-334) writes only non-secret user/expiry (or nothing), getStoredSession (:336-345)/init (:110-125) no longer restore tokens and instead bootstrap via a silent refresh call; refreshSession (:260-277) calls the endpoint with no body relying on the cookie; keep accessToken only in the in-memory `session`; clearSession already removes the localStorage key. CSRF stays covered by the global X-Requested-With gate (csrf.rs) + SameSite; CORS already sets allow_credentials(true). Note the Secure-on-http-localhost browser exception; if non-localhost http dev is needed, gate Secure behind a config flag. Regenerate OpenAPI/contract types after the AuthResponse change. FALLBACK if cross-origin cookies prove infeasible within the task: at minimum set the refresh token as an httpOnly cookie and keep the access token in memory, even if the response body still returns tokens.
tasks.corrected_refs: Accurate. auth.rs: login returns Json(AuthResponse) :175-187, register :81-93, refresh :258-270; refresh reads body.refresh_token :207; logout :285-296. models.rs: AuthResponse :54-61, RefreshRequest :81-84. Cargo.toml axum-extra features=["typed-header"] only (:9) — must add "cookie". auth.ts: STORAGE_KEY :11, authApiClient createClient :13-20, init :110-125, refreshSession :260-277 (sends body {refresh_token}), createSession :279-286, storeSession writes both tokens :322-334, getStoredSession :336-345, clearSession :295-305, signOut :184-200. runtime.ts setConfig :6-17 (no credentials:'include'). FE uses a generated SDK (sdk.gen refresh/login/register).
step_by_step: Land AFTER P2-T5 (shared auth.rs/models.rs). Add "cookie" to axum-extra features in Cargo.toml (:9).

Change login (:108/:175), register (:27/:81), refresh (:201/:258) return types to `Result<(CookieJar, Json<AuthResponse>), ApiError>` (import axum_extra::extract::cookie::{CookieJar,Cookie}); build a refresh cookie `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/api/auth; Max-Age=<refresh exp secs>` and return (jar.add(cookie), Json(resp)).

In refresh (:201-207) read the refresh token from the CookieJar first, falling back to body.refresh_token during transition — make RefreshRequest.refresh_token Optional in models.rs:82-84. Rotate via existing revoke/store logic (:210-256), set the new cookie, and drop refresh_token from the JSON body (empty string for schema stability, or remove and regen contracts).
In logout (:285-296) return a CookieJar removing the refresh cookie (matching Path).
FE: runtime.ts setConfig (:6-17) add `credentials: 'include'`; auth.ts authApiClient createClient (:13-20) add `credentials: 'include'` so the cookie flows cross-origin (5173→4100).

auth.ts stop persisting tokens: storeSession (:322-334) writes only non-secret user/expiresAt (or nothing); getStoredSession (:336-345) + init (:110-125) no longer restore tokens — bootstrap via a silent refresh on init; refreshSession (:260-277) calls the endpoint with NO body; keep accessToken only in-memory. clearSession already removes the key.

tasks.precision_notes: Cross-origin dev 5173→4100: fetch with credentials needs CORS allow_credentials(true) AND a specific (non-wildcard) Access-Control-Allow-Origin — verify middleware/cors.rs sets both (wildcard+credentials is browser-rejected) before relying on cookies. SameSite=Strict + the global X-Requested-With CSRF gate protects the cookie; Path=/api/auth keeps it off non-auth routes. Secure is dropped by browsers only on http://localhost (dev exception); gate Secure behind a config flag for non-localhost http dev. FE uses the generated SDK; refreshRequest currently sends body {refresh_token} (:267) — make the body optional/empty and rely on credentials:'include'. Depends on P2-T5 (both edit auth.rs+models.rs) — sequence T5 then T9. tasks.title: Shared test-DB bootstrap: self-provisioned migrations, REQUIRE_DB hard-fail, qdesigner_app DSN in CI
tasks.scope: Replace the 6 copy-pasted `get_test_pool() -> Option<PgPool>` helpers with one shared `tests/common/mod.rs` that (a) runs `sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")` against the migration DSN before handing out any pool, so `cargo test` works on a bare Postgres (local fresh stack and CI alike), (b) panics with a clear message instead of returning None when the env var REQUIRE_DB is truthy and the DB is unreachable — eliminating the false-green skip-pass — while preserving the local skip behavior when REQUIRE_DB is unset, and (c) exposes two entry points: `fixture_pool()` (prefers DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS, superuser, for RLS-bound fixture seeding) and `app_pool()` (DATABASE_URL, qdesigner_app). Update ci.yml backend job: add REQUIRE_DB="1" and DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS (superuser DSN), and repoint DATABASE_URL to postgresql://qdesigner_app:qdesigner_app@localhost:5432/qdesigner so tests reading it run as the non-BYPASSRLS production role (the role + its 'qdesigner_app' dev password are created by migration 00018 during the provisioning step, resolving the chicken-egg).
tasks.approach: tests/common/mod.rs keeps the existing `.env.development` discovery (CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR .parent().parent() per the P3.4a note in CLAUDE.md) then adds a `provision()` guarded by a `tokio::sync::OnceCell<()>`: resolve DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS || DATABASE_URL, open a 1-connection pool, run `sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")` (idempotent; sqlx takes an advisory lock, and cargo runs integration-test binaries sequentially, so cross-binary races are safe), close it. `fn require_db() -> bool` parses REQUIRE_DB in {1,true,yes,on}. `fixture_pool()`/`app_pool()` return Option<PgPool>: on any connect/migrate error, `if require_db() { panic!("REQUIRE_DB=1 but <dsn kind> unreachable: {e}") } else { None }`. The 25 `let Some(pool) = ... else { eprintln!("skipping"); return; }` guard sites stay verbatim — only the helper import changes, so the diff per test file is ~20 deleted lines + 2 added. Before mapping each file to fixture_pool vs app_pool, read its current get_test_pool body (rls_context reads DATABASE_URL only; rls_enforcement/rbac prefer DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS) and preserve that choice exactly — do NOT flip bump_version/sync_session onto the app pool in this task, since their fixture INSERTs into RLS-bound tables would be denied under qdesigner_app without a GUC. ci.yml gets the three env lines; the Test step also gains `-- --include-ignored` to match the documented local gate.

apps/server/tests/bump_version_semver.rs (:18-.., map to whichever DSN it reads today)

Guard-site counts exact: 25 total `let Some(pool)` sites = rls_enforcement 7, rbac 8, bump 5, sync 3, rls_context 1, revoked 1; auth_flows 0 (pure crypto). ci.yml refs exact: env block :9-21 (DATABASE_URL superuser DSN on :11, port 5432), Test step :82-84 (`cargo test`, no `--include-ignored`, no migrate step). docker-compose.test.yml postgres is on host port 15435 (not 15434), superuser DSN qdesigner/qdesigner, DB qdesigner_test.

Add the `.env.development` discovery block copied verbatim from rbac_integration.rs:31-53 (CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR .parent().parent().join('.env.development'), parsing BOTH DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS= and DATABASE_URL= into std::env). Add `fn require_db() -> bool` that reads REQUIRE_DB and returns true when its lowercased value is in {"1","true","yes","on"}.

Add a `static PROVISIONED: tokio::sync::OnceCell<()> = OnceCell::const_new();` and `async fn provision()`: call load_env(); resolve url = DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS.or(DATABASE_URL); open a 1-connection PgPoolOptions pool; run `sqlx::migrate!("./migrations").run(&pool).await`; close the pool. On error, `if require_db() { panic!("REQUIRE_DB set but migration DSN unreachable / migrate failed: {e}") }` else return (skip). Guard the whole body via PROVISIONED.get_or_init.

Add `pub async fn fixture_pool() -> Option<PgPool>`: call provision().await; load_env(); url = DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS.or(DATABASE_URL); `PgPool::connect(&url).await` mapping error to `if require_db(){panic!("REQUIRE_DB=1 but migration/fixture DSN unreachable: {e}")} else {None}`.
Add `pub async fn app_pool() -> Option<PgPool>`: same shape but url = DATABASE_URL only (the qdesigner_app role in CI/prod posture); same panic-or-None semantics.

Add `mod common;` to the five files that currently prefer DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS (rls_enforcement, rbac_integration, bump_version_semver, sync_session_dedup) and replace each local `get_test_pool` with `use common::fixture_pool` and swap the call at every guard site (`get_test_pool()` → `fixture_pool()`). Delete the local fn bodies. Leave the `let Some(pool) = ... else { eprintln!(...); return; }` lines otherwise verbatim.

For rls_context.rs (:21-40) and revoked_tokens_purge.rs (:15-31): add `mod common;`, delete the local get_test_pool, and repoint their call sites to `common::app_pool()` (both currently read DATABASE_URL only — preserve that as the app pool).

Do NOT flip bump_version_semver / sync_session_dedup / rbac / rls_enforcement onto app_pool — their fixture INSERTs into RLS-bound tables (organization_members, projects, sessions) need the superuser fixture_pool; qdesigner_app would be denied without a matching GUC.

tasks.precision_notes: The skip message is NOT uniform across files: rls_context.rs/rls_enforcement.rs print 'skipping...', while bump/sync/rbac/revoked print 'Skipping: DATABASE_URL not set'. The plan's `grep -c skipping` verification undercounts — use `grep -ci`. sqlx::migrate! takes an advisory lock and cargo runs integration-test binaries sequentially by default, so the OnceCell-guarded provision() is race-safe cross-binary. revoked_tokens_purge.rs reads DATABASE_URL only today (plan left its DSN unmapped) → app_pool is the behavior-preserving choice, and revoked_tokens is not among the 8 RLS-bound tables so qdesigner_app seeding succeeds.

tasks.title: tower/axum HTTP handler harness: auth + fillout-session round-trips and explicit api/access cross-tenant mutation-DENIAL suite
tasks.scope: Add the first automatically-run HTTP round-trip coverage: a test-only AppState builder + `test_app()` helper that wraps `api::router(state)` with the same csrf_middleware layering as main.rs:191-194, driven via tower::ServiceExt::oneshot. Three new integration test files cover (1) auth register→login→me→refresh→logout plus 401 on bad password and 403 on CSRF-missing POST, (2) the ADR-0013 authorization contract — since admin-table mutation RLS is permissive WITH CHECK(true), api/access::verify_project_write_access / verify_org_membership / verify_questionnaire_access are THE gate, and this suite asserts cross-tenant mutations are DENIED end-to-end, (3) anonymous fillout session lifecycle through middleware/fillout_rls_context (create → submit response → read own rows; a different anonymous session cannot read them). Requires a probe-free S3StorageService constructor so the harness runs with only Postgres up.
tasks.approach: build_test_state(): pool = common::app_pool() (qdesigner_app — mirrors production posture, extending F078's fix to the handler layer); JwtManager::new(test secrets, 900s/7d); RbacManager::new(); Arc::new(S3StorageService::new_unchecked(env MINIO_* or dummies)); Arc::new(WebSocketState::new()); YjsStore::new(); redis: None; RateLimiter::new(10_000, 60, None) — deliberately high so the 10/60s auth limiter can't flake harness tests; Config constructed literally (all fields pub — avoids Config::from_env's env_required panics). test_app = api::router(state).layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(csrf_middleware)). Requests via `app.clone().oneshot(Request::builder()...)` with `X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest` + `Authorization: Bearer` headers, bodies collected via http_body_util::BodyExt. Users/tenants are provisioned THROUGH the API (POST /api/auth/register then /api/auth/login for tokens); read apps/server/src/api/auth.rs register/login first — if login requires email_verified, flip users.email_verified=true via common::fixture_pool() between register and login. Denial matrix (user B = owner of org B only, resources under org A): PATCH /api/projects/{a_proj}, DELETE /api/projects/{a_proj}, POST /api/projects/{a_proj}/questionnaires, PATCH + DELETE /api/projects/{a_proj}/questionnaires/{qid}, POST .../bump-version, POST /api/organizations/{a_org}/members — assert the status api/access::* actually maps to (read access.rs:19-121 + error.rs; expect 403 or 404, assert exact) and pair each with the user-A positive control. Fillout: POST /api/sessions anonymous (handler-generated id per fillout_rls_context contract), POST /{id}/responses with a client_id UUID, GET /{id}/responses returns it; GET with a different session id in the path (fresh anonymous context) must NOT return session 1's rows — the dual-path 00021 policy asserted at HTTP level rather than raw SQL.
files: apps/server/src/storage/s3.rs (add `pub fn new_unchecked(endpoint, access_key, secret_key, bucket) -> Self` next to new() at :20 — builds Client::from_conf without the head_bucket/create_bucket network probe; doc-comment it as test-support)
tasks.corrected_refs: access.rs helpers confirmed at exact lines: get_project_org_id:19, verify_org_membership:33, verify_project_access:58, verify_project_write_access:88, verify_questionnaire_access:121 — all return ApiError::Forbidden (String) or ApiError::NotFound; implementer must read error.rs to confirm the HTTP status each maps to (assert the exact code). s3.rs::new is `pub async fn new(endpoint,access_key,secret_key,bucket) -> Result<Self,ApiError>` at :20; it builds `Client::from_conf(config)` at :36 then does head_bucket/create_bucket at :39/:43 — new_unchecked should be a NON-async `pub fn new_unchecked(...) -> Self` that stops after Client::from_conf (no network probe). main.rs csrf layering confirmed at :192 (`.layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(csrf_middleware))`). api::router(state) confirmed at api/mod.rs:27; session_routes at :187 carry set_fillout_rls_context (:225). Config: ALL fields pub (config.rs:5-38) — literal construction is viable, no from_env panic. AppState: all fields pub (state.rs:16-25); redis is Option<Arc<redis::Client>>, rate_limiter is RateLimiter (owned, not Arc). RateLimiter::new(max_requests:u64, window_secs:i64, redis:Option<Arc<redis::Client>>) confirmed at rate_limit.rs:27 — plan's `RateLimiter::new(10_000, 60, None)` typechecks. verify-email/verify handler is `auth::verify_code` (mod.rs:36), verify_code UPDATEs users.email_verified by EMAIL (auth.rs:526), not by token.
step_by_step: Add dev-dependencies to apps/server/Cargo.toml: `tower = { version = "0.5", features = ["util"] }` and `http-body-util = "0.1"` (axum 0.7/0.8 already provides Request/Response; confirm axum version to pick matching tower).

In apps/server/src/storage/s3.rs, add `pub fn new_unchecked(endpoint:&str, access_key:&str, secret_key:&str, bucket:&str) -> Self` next to new() (:20): duplicate the Credentials/S3ConfigBuilder/Client::from_conf block (lines 26-36), return `Self { client, bucket: bucket.to_string() }`, skip head_bucket/create_bucket. Doc-comment it `/// Test-support: constructs the client without the network bucket probe.`

Extend tests/common/mod.rs with `pub async fn build_test_state() -> Option<AppState>`: pool = app_pool().await? (qdesigner_app posture); JwtManager::new(test access+refresh secrets, Duration::seconds(900), Duration::days(7)) — read auth/session.rs for the exact JwtManager::new signature first; RbacManager::new(); storage = Arc::new(S3StorageService::new_unchecked(dummy endpoint/keys/bucket)); websocket_state = Arc::new(WebSocketState::new()); yjs_store = YjsStore::new(); redis: None; rate_limiter = RateLimiter::new(10_000, 60, None); config = Arc::new(Config{ ...literal all pub fields... }). Return Some(AppState{...}).

Add a request helper in common: build Request with method/uri/JSON body, headers `X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest` (CSRF) and optional `Authorization: Bearer <tok>`, drive via `app.clone().oneshot(req)`, collect body via http_body_util::BodyExt::collect. Return (StatusCode, serde_json::Value).

Create tests/http_auth_flows.rs: read auth.rs register(:27)/login(:108) first — register INSERTs email_verified (:54); if login (:108) requires email_verified=true, flip it via common::fixture_pool() between register and login. Create tests/http_access_denial.rs: provision user B (owner of org B) and user A (owner of org A + a project/questionnaire under org A) THROUGH the API. Assert cross-tenant DENIAL for B against A's resources: PATCH & DELETE /api/projects/{a_proj}, POST /api/projects/{a_proj}/questionnaires, PATCH & DELETE .../questionnaires/{qid}, POST .../bump-version, POST /api/organizations/{a_org}/members. Read access.rs:88-143 + error.rs to assert the EXACT status (Forbidden→403 or the NotFound→404 that get_project_org_id yields when the project scope is hidden). Pair each with a user-A positive control (expect 2xx).

tasks.precision_notes: Config MUST be built as a struct literal (all fields pub, confirmed) — do NOT call Config::from_env (it env_requires JWT secrets and would panic in-harness). storage uses new_unchecked so no MinIO needed for T2. rate_limiter is an owned RateLimiter value in AppState (not Arc). The bootstrap-anonymous session id is generated in the handler (POST /api/sessions), so the GET-with-different-id isolation test must open a genuinely fresh request (new oneshot) so fillout_rls_context sets app.session_id from the URL path, not from session 1. Depends on P3-T1 (needs common::app_pool/fixture_pool).

tasks.title: MinIO storage + media-proxy Range + email-verification integration tests; provision minio/mailpit in the CI backend job
tasks.scope: Close the zero-coverage gap on the storage and email paths. New tests: (1) storage-client tests against a real MinIO — upload/get_object (full + Range slice)/presigned_url/delete via S3StorageService::new (which creates the bucket, so no mc-init needed); (2) harness-driven media tests — authenticated multipart POST /api/media then GET /api/media/{id}/content asserting the ADR-0023 D1 contract headers (Accept-Ranges: bytes, Cache-Control immutable, stable cache key) and Range: bytes=0-3 → 206 + correct Content-Range/body slice; (3) email-verification round-trip — POST /api/auth/verify-email/send delivers through the real lettre SMTP path to MailPit, code row lands in email_verification_codes, POST /api/auth/verify-email/verify flips users.email_verified, and MailPit's HTTP API confirms the message actually arrived. Add minio + mailpit service containers to the ci.yml backend job so these run in CI (env MINIO_*/SMTP_* at :15-20 already point at localhost:9000/1025 but the services were never provisioned).
tasks.approach: storage_minio.rs reads MINIO_ENDPOINT/MINIO_ACCESS_KEY/MINIO_SECRET_KEY from env (with the same .env.development fallback), uses a dedicated bucket name (e.g. qdesigner-media-test) so ::new's create-if-missing path is itself exercised; on unreachable MinIO apply the T1 semantics — panic under REQUIRE_DB, skip otherwise. Range test: upload known 16 bytes, get_object(key, Some("bytes=2-5")) → exactly bytes 2..=5 (read s3.rs get_object signature first — media.rs:369 shows it takes Option<&str> range). http_media_email.rs: build AppState with the REAL S3StorageService::new so upload_media → stream_media_content round-trips through MinIO; multipart body built by hand (multipart/form-data boundary string) or via a tiny helper; assert media.rs:298-380 documented behavior — 200 full body, 206 + Content-Range on single Range, Accept-Ranges/Cache-Control headers. Email: send_verification_code (auth.rs:388-473) uses SmtpTransport::builder_dangerous(smtp_host):smtp_port — with mailpit up it must return 200 (this also pins the current 'send fails → handler errors?' behavior; assert whatever auth.rs actually does after reading :446-473); fetch code via fixture_pool from email_verification_codes; verify via POST /api/auth/verify-email/verify; then GET http://localhost:8025/api/v1/search?query=to:<addr> (MailPit HTTP API) and assert 1 message containing the code. CI: minio as `bitnami/minio` (env MINIO_ROOT_USER/MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=minioadmin, port 9000:9000, health-cmd curl -f http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live) — the official minio/minio image can't be a GH service container because services can't pass a command; mailpit as `axllent/mailpit` (ports 1025:1025, 8025:8025 — its default entrypoint needs no args). Do NOT use docker-compose.test.yml here; the backend job's env already targets 9000/1025.

Risk stays high purely from ripple breadth; the change itself is mechanical. moduleConfigAdapter (T4) and PropertiesPanel (T5) additionally read off-union legacy fields (q.config/q.text/q.instruction/q.display) that T3 does NOT put on BaseQuestion — those are handled in their own tasks via typed views/guards, not here.

tasks.title: Align SessionManagementService with the real session-update wire contract
tasks.scope: Remove the five `as any` update payloads and two `as unknown as Session` casts by coding to the verified server contract: UpdateSessionRequest (sessions.rs:67) accepts ONLY `{status?, metadata?}`; normalize_session_status (sessions.rs:2374) folds not_started/in_progress/paused into 'active'; the server always stamps last_activity_at = NOW() itself and stamps completed_at on status='completed' (update_session :1416-1431). The `last_activity_at/started_at/completed_at/state_snapshot` fields this service sends today are silently dropped by serde — deleting them is behavior-preserving. Reconcile the two session shapes: build the fillout `Session` (shared/types/session.ts) explicitly from api.sessions results instead of blind-casting.
tasks.approach: Type `api.sessions.update` calls against the post-T1 body type (`{ status?: string; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> }` mirroring GeneratedUpdateSessionRequest): startSession sends `{ status: 'in_progress' }`; resumeSession sends `{ status: session.status === 'not_started' ? 'in_progress' : session.status }`; completeSession sends `{ status: 'completed' }`; abandonSession sends `{ status: 'abandoned', metadata: { abandon_reason } }`; pauseSession sends `{ status: 'paused', metadata: currentState !== undefined ? { state_snapshot: currentState } : undefined }` — folding the previously-dropped snapshot into metadata is the one deliberate behavior improvement; flag it in the commit message (server merges metadata via the $N bind, verify against update_session's metadata branch before keeping it, otherwise drop the param). Replace `data as unknown as Session` (:70, :90) with an explicit `toFilloutSession(data: SessionData): Session` conversion (questionnaire_id from questionnaireId, status pass-through with the wider fillout union, metadata ?? {}). Type pauseSession's currentState as `unknown`, and `getLocalSession(): { id: string; questionnaire_id: string; access_code?: string; status: string; saved_at: number } | null` matching exactly what saveLocalSession writes (:264-270).
files: apps/web/src/lib/shared/types/session.ts (Session doc/shape notes only if needed)
tasks.corrected_refs: Casts confirmed: `data as unknown as Session` at :70 and :90; `} as any` update payloads at resumeSession :98-102, startSession :118-123, pauseSession :131-136, completeSession :143-147, abandonSession :157-164; `pauseSession(sessionId, currentState?: any)` at :130; `getLocalSession(): any` at :277 (saveLocalSession writes {id, questionnaire_id, access_code, status, saved_at} at :264-270). normalize_session_status (sessions.rs:2374) accepts not_started|in_progress|paused|active->active, completed, abandoned, expired — all T2 status literals are valid.
step_by_step: Depends on P4-T1 changing api.sessions.update to accept `{ status?: string; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> }`. Type all five update calls against that shape and delete the `} as any`.
startSession -> `{ status: 'in_progress' }` (drop the serde-dropped started_at/last_activity_at — server stamps last_activity_at=NOW() always and completed_at on completion).
resumeSession (:98) -> `{ status: session.status === 'not_started' ? 'in_progress' : session.status }` (drop last_activity_at).
completeSession -> `{ status: 'completed' }`.

abandonSession -> `{ status: 'abandoned', metadata: { abandon_reason: reason || 'user_exit' } }` (preserves current behavior; note the server REPLACES metadata, but abandon is terminal so clobbering create-time metadata matches today's behavior).

pauseSession -> send ONLY `{ status: 'paused' }`. DO NOT fold currentState into metadata: update_session (sessions.rs:1441) does `metadata = $N` (full replace, NOT a merge), so a metadata snapshot would clobber the create-time metadata (access_code/device_info/browser_info). The old top-level state_snapshot field was already serde-dropped, so omitting it is behavior-preserving. Keep `currentState?: unknown` in the signature but drop it from the request; leave a comment that persisting a resume snapshot needs a server-side metadata-merge or a dedicated column first.

Replace `data as unknown as Session` at :70 and :90 with an explicit `toFilloutSession(data: SessionData): Session` helper: questionnaire_id from data.questionnaireId, status pass-through into the wider fillout Session union, metadata ?? {}, plus whatever else the fillout Session shape (shared/types/session.ts) requires. Use it in createSession and resumeSession.
Type getLocalSession(): `{ id: string; questionnaire_id: string; access_code?: string; status: string; saved_at: number } | null` matching saveLocalSession's written shape; drop its eslint-disable.
Run the verification gate.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
grep -c 'as any' apps/web/src/lib/fillout/services/SessionManagementService.ts == 0

Live QA: start a fillout session, answer a question, reload with ?sid= to resume, then complete; in network tab confirm PUT /api/sessions/{id} bodies carry only {status[,metadata]} and the row goes active->completed with no 400s from normalize_session_status
tasks.precision_notes: CORRECTION to the plan's 'deliberate behavior improvement': the server does NOT merge metadata (update_session sessions.rs:1441 is `metadata = $N`, a full replace), so folding pauseSession's state_snapshot into metadata would DESTROY the session's create-time metadata (access_code/device_info/browser_info). The plan's own guard clause anticipated this — take the 'otherwise drop the param' branch. Net: pause sends status only; no behavior regression (state_snapshot was never persisted). This makes the task purely subtractive and keeps risk low. Reconcile Session vs SessionData via the explicit converter, not a cast.

tasks.title: Rust structure: Tx guard helper kills the 73-copy unwrap boilerplate; split sessions.rs (3,585 lines) by concern
tasks.scope: (1) F081: add a TxHandle guard to middleware/tx.rs — `impl Tx { pub async fn tx(&self) -> Result<TxHandle<'_>, ApiError> }` where TxHandle wraps the MutexGuard, errors with ApiError::Internal when the slot is None (route wired without rls_context), and implements Deref/DerefMut to Transaction<'static, Postgres>. Replace all 73 copies of the two-line `let mut guard = tx.lock().await; let tx = guard.as_mut().expect("rls_context middleware placed a transaction");` prelude (grep-counted this session: organizations.rs 20, sessions.rs 19, projects.rs 9, questionnaires.rs 9, media.rs 5, templates.rs 5, comments.rs 4, users.rs 2) with `let mut tx = tx.tx().await?;` — every existing `&mut **tx` call site compiles unchanged because the deref chain still lands on PgConnection. (2) F080: split api/sessions.rs into api/sessions/ — models.rs (the ~30 DTO/FromRow structs at :22-357 plus :1708-1755,:2568,:2621), crud.rs (create_session:361, check_duplicate:512, list_sessions:573, get_session:1206, get_responses:1252, get_events:1321, get_variables:1363, update_session:1403 + ensure_session_active/exists/access/participant_or_member helpers :2390-2511), ingestion.rs (submit_response:1507, submit_events:1594, upsert_variable:1664 + fetch_session_variable_context/load_variable_definition_map/persist_session_variable/refresh_session_variable_projection/insert_response :1790-2488), analytics.rs (aggregate_sessions:652, compare_sessions:711, dashboard_summary:810, cross_project_analytics:948, load_numeric_samples:2191, timeseries:3478), quotas.rs (condition_counts:2589, quota_status:2651), sync.rs (sync_session:2827), filter.rs (filter_sessions:3144 + is_safe_filter_key). mod.rs does `pub use` globs so api/mod.rs:190-222 route wiring and openapi.rs:140-159 `api::sessions::*` path references compile unchanged.
tasks.approach: Do the Tx sweep first (mechanical, compiler-verified), then the file split as pure code motion — no query or signature changes, shared structs live in sessions/models.rs with pub(crate) visibility re-exported from sessions/mod.rs. utoipa's #[utoipa::path] generates a pub __path_<fn> item next to each pub handler, so `pub use crud::*;` in mod.rs re-exports it and openapi.rs's `api::sessions::create_session` path references keep resolving; fallback if a glob misses: point openapi.rs at the concrete submodule paths. Watch for handlers that drop the guard mid-function to call `impl PgExecutor` helpers — TxHandle supports the same pattern (pass `&mut **tx`). The 19 sessions.rs prelude sites get replaced during the move.
tasks.corrected_refs: Boilerplate count confirmed EXACTLY 73: organizations.rs 20, sessions.rs 19, projects.rs 9, questionnaires.rs 9, media.rs 5, templates.rs 5, comments.rs 4, users.rs 2. Total .expect() in api/ = 85. sessions.rs confirmed 3585 lines. tx.rs currently exposes only Tx::lock() returning MutexGuard<Option<Transaction>> + FromRequestParts (errors ApiError::Internal if SharedTx absent). openapi.rs:140-159 references all 20 `api::sessions::*` handler paths — must remain resolvable via glob re-export from sessions/mod.rs. api/mod.rs:256-264 nests session_routes at /api/sessions.
step_by_step: middleware/tx.rs: add `pub struct TxHandle<'a>(tokio::sync::MutexGuard<'a, Option<Transaction<'static, Postgres>>>);` with Deref<Target=Transaction<'static,Postgres>> (`self.0.as_ref().expect(...)`) + DerefMut (`self.0.as_mut().expect(...)`); the expect is unreachable because tx() checks Some.

Add `impl Tx { pub async fn tx(&self) -> Result<TxHandle<'_>, ApiError> { let guard = self.0.lock().await; if guard.is_none() { return Err(ApiError::Internal("rls_context middleware did not place a transaction".into())); } Ok(TxHandle(guard)) } }`.

Sweep all 73 sites across the 8 handler files: replace the two-line prelude `let mut guard = tx.lock().await; let tx = guard.as_mut().expect("rls_context middleware placed a transaction");` with `let mut tx = tx.tx().await?;`. Existing `&mut **tx` call sites compile unchanged (TxHandle DerefMut → Transaction → Deref → PgConnection).

Verify the shadowing pattern: `let mut tx = tx.tx().await?;` borrows the original Tx which stays alive (shadowed) to end of scope — valid Rust. Handlers that release the lock mid-fn to call impl PgExecutor helpers keep passing `&mut **tx`; if a non-tx await intervenes, drop(tx) explicitly first.

Create apps/server/src/api/sessions/ and split sessions.rs by pure code motion: models.rs (DTO/FromRow structs :22-357,:1708-1755,:2568,:2621 pub(crate)), crud.rs (create_session:361, check_duplicate:512, list_sessions:573, get_session:1206, get_responses:1252, get_events:1321, get_variables:1363, update_session:1403 + ensure_session_active/exists/access/participant_or_member :2390-2511), ingestion.rs (submit_response:1507, submit_events:1594, upsert_variable:1664 + fetch_session_variable_context/load_variable_definition_map/persist_session_variable/refresh_session_variable_projection/insert_response :1790-2488), analytics.rs (aggregate_sessions:652, compare_sessions:711, dashboard_summary:810, cross_project_analytics:948, load_numeric_samples:2191, timeseries:3478), quotas.rs (condition_counts:2589, quota_status:2651), sync.rs (sync_session:2827), filter.rs (filter_sessions:3144 + is_safe_filter_key).

Create sessions/mod.rs: `mod models; mod crud; mod ingestion; mod analytics; mod quotas; mod sync; mod filter; pub use crud::*; pub use ingestion::*; pub use analytics::*; pub use quotas::*; pub use sync::*; pub use filter::*; pub(crate) use models::*;` — the pub use globs re-export both each `pub async fn` handler AND utoipa's generated `pub __path_<fn>` item, keeping openapi.rs's `api::sessions::create_session` refs resolvable.
Delete the original sessions.rs after the move.
Build; if any glob misses a __path_ item, point openapi.rs at the concrete submodule path (e.g. api::sessions::crud::create_session).

tasks.precision_notes: `let mut tx = tx.tx().await?;` shadowing works because Rust keeps the shadowed Tx binding alive to end of scope, so TxHandle's borrow is valid. TxHandle must be lifetime-parameterized (holds the MutexGuard borrowing the Arc<Mutex> owned by Tx). The utoipa __path_ glob re-export is the load-bearing detail for keeping openapi.rs unchanged; verify all 20 sessions paths resolve after the split. Do the Tx sweep first (mechanical, compiler-verified) then the file split (pure motion) to isolate failures.

tasks.title: Rust query/error safety: adopt sqlx compile-time macros on the highest-churn sessions queries + error-mapping/hygiene cleanup
tasks.scope: (1) F079 (incremental, sessions/ modules only): convert the static-SQL, highest-churn queries to query!/query_as!/query_scalar! — crud.rs (create_session INSERT..RETURNING as query_as!(Session,..), get_session/get_responses/get_events/get_variables SELECTs, ensure_session_active/exists probes), ingestion.rs (insert_response, submit_events INSERT, persist_session_variable), sync.rs (the per-row INSERT..ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING trio). Dynamic SQL (filter_sessions, any COALESCE-based dynamic UPDATE) explicitly stays on the runtime API. Infra: add pkgs.sqlx-cli to flake.nix buildInputs; generate offline data with the schema-bearing role (`cd apps/server && DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS cargo sqlx prepare -- --tests`) and commit the .sqlx/ directory; add SQLX_OFFLINE: "true" to the ci.yml backend job env (CI's DATABASE_URL points at a bare postgres and must not be consulted at macro-expansion time); record the decision + regen workflow in a short ADR (docs/decisions/0024-sqlx-offline-macros.md). (2) F084: in sessions/filter.rs, stop binding every value as text — introduce enum FilterParam { Text(String), BigInt(i64), Timestamp(DateTime<Utc>) }, choose the target type from the field allowlist (reaction_time_us -> i64; started_at/completed_at -> RFC3339 timestamptz; status/participant_id/metadata.*/variable.* -> text), return ApiError::Validation (422) on parse failure instead of a Postgres execution error 500, and collapse the six duplicated operator match arms (:3224-3255) into an operator->SQL-token lookup with a single param-push path. (3) F085: mount .layer(CatchPanicLayer::new()) on auth_routes (mod.rs:44-46) and ws_route (mod.rs:250). (4) F086: remove the blanket #[allow(dead_code)] from the ApiError enum (error.rs:6) and AppState (state.rs:15), fixing/target-allowing what the compiler then flags; replace the two `.unwrap_or_default()` role-lookup swallows in auth.rs (:147 login, :236 refresh) with `?` so a DB failure is a 500 rather than silently minting a token with the defaulted 'user' role. DO NOT touch the 23505->409 unique-violation mapping (P2's charter).
tasks.approach: sqlx 0.8's macros auto-load .sqlx offline data when SQLX_OFFLINE=true or DATABASE_URL is unset, so dev `cargo check` keeps working with the DB down once .sqlx is committed. Conversion pattern: `sqlx::query_as!(Session, r#"SELECT ... "#, id).fetch_optional(&mut **tx)` — expect to need `as "col!"`/`as "col?"` nullability overrides on LEFT JOIN / RETURNING columns; the FromRow derives on Session/ResponseRecord stay for the remaining runtime-API queries. Behavior notes to verify in tests: F084's typed timestamps change bad-input responses from 500 to 422 (add a filter_sessions integration case posting a non-numeric reaction_time_us and asserting 422); auth propagation changes a degraded-DB login from silent role-downgrade to explicit 500 (acceptable per audit rec — role claims feed authorization). Runs strictly after P5-T8 since it edits the modules that task creates.
files: apps/server/Cargo.toml (sqlx macros feature check; 0.8 already has them)
tasks.corrected_refs: Confirmed: 0 query!/query_as!/query_scalar! macros; 185 runtime sqlx calls in api/. No .sqlx/ dir. sqlx 0.8 with default features (macros available — Cargo.toml:13 does not set default-features=false). filter_sessions field_expr match ~3180-3213, value_str always-stringified ~3215-3220, operator arms duplicated (eq/neq/gt/lt/gte/lte each push+format+increment) :3224-3255+. auth.rs unwrap_or_default at :147 (login) and :236 (refresh), both fall back to vec!["user"]. error.rs:6 #[allow(dead_code)] on ApiError enum + state.rs:15 #[allow(dead_code)] on AppState confirmed. flake.nix buildInputs at :18. .github/workflows/ci.yml exists. MAJOR CORRECTION on CatchPanicLayer: auth_routes (mod.rs:28-46) ends with a rate_limit_middleware layer at :43-46 and has NO CatchPanicLayer (the :50 CatchPanicLayer belongs to user_routes); ws_route at mod.rs:250 is `Router::new().route("/ws",...)` with NO layers. tasks.title: Fix memoization-defeating $derived callables, effect-as-derived, and config-default $effects
tasks.scope: Convert all 14 callable-pattern `const x = $derived(() => …)` declarations to `$derived.by(() => …)` and drop the call parens at every use site; replace the two effect-writes-state-a-derived-should-own sites (AppLoader.currentStage, PropertiesPanel.bulkOptionDraft reset) with $derived / identity-keyed effects; replace the three config-default-seeding $effects (MediaResponseDesigner, BarChart, DrawingDesigner) with one-time init normalization or derive-with-fallback. Pure idiom fixes — no behavior change.
tasks.approach: F027: mechanical `$derived(() => body)` → `$derived.by(() => body)`, then grep each file for `<name>(` and strip parens at consumers (e.g. IRTPanel tifPath reads `tifData` not `tifData()`; TextDisplay `style={contentStyle}`; CompletionScreen `effectiveRedirectUrl` becomes `$derived(redirectUrl || panelRedirectUrl)`). Note ResponseMappingEditor:10 and fillout +page:130 are plain-expression deriveds — leave them. F031 AppLoader: replace the $effect with `const currentStage = $derived.by(...)` — the current effect retains the last message when no stage is loading, so add an explicit fallback (last non-pending stage's message or the static final-stage label) to preserve UX. F031 PropertiesPanel: key the reset on selection identity — track only `questionItem?.id` + `isChoiceQuestion` and compute `formatChoiceOptions(questionItem)` inside `untrack()` so deep questionItem edits no longer clobber the draft mid-typing. F030: MediaResponseDesigner/DrawingDesigner run the guarded default-seeding once per question at init (plain script-top normalization function invoked on mount / when question.id changes inside untrack) instead of inside the reactive graph; BarChart replaces the `analytics.dataSource = {…}` effect with a `const dataSource = $derived(analytics.dataSource ?? DEFAULT_DATA_SOURCE)` read-through fallback, updating internal readers/writers accordingly.

tasks.title: Sweep deprecated $app/stores to $app/state across the remaining 13 files
tasks.scope: Replace every remaining `import { page } from '$app/stores'` with the fine-grained `$app/state` equivalent and drop the `$page` auto-subscription reads. Where a legacy-mode file feeds `$page` into `$:` statements, migrate that file to runes in the same pass — legacy `$:` blocks track variable identity at compile time, so a bare swap to the never-reassigned `page` object would silently kill reactivity.

tasks.title: Introduce a Context API seam over the designerStore singleton

tasks.approach: Export `class DesignerStore` from designer.svelte.ts. New designer-context.ts: `const KEY = Symbol('designer-store'); export function setDesignerContext(store: DesignerStore) { setContext(KEY, store); } export function getDesignerContext(): DesignerStore { return getContext<DesignerStore>(KEY) ?? designerStore; }` — the singleton fallback keeps any mount point outside the designer route (previews, test-runtime harness) working unchanged while giving tests a clean override. Set context in the designer route +layout.svelte (context set there reaches the +page subtree where all 31 components mount). Mechanical migration per component: replace `import { designerStore } from '$lib/stores/designer.svelte'` with `import { getDesignerContext } from '$lib/stores/designer-context'` + `const designerStore = getDesignerContext();` immediately after imports (getContext must run during component init — verify no file references designerStore in module context). tasks.title: Extract a headless FilloutPageController from the fillout entry page

tasks.precision_notes: FraudDetectionService.checkAll and OfflineSessionService.completeSession are static — the injectable bag must expose them as callables/class refs (e.g. `fraudCheck: typeof FraudDetectionService.checkAll`), otherwise the mock swap won't work and you'll accidentally `new` a static-only service. syncEngine is nullable (`| null`) — the controller owns its lifecycle; preserve the `syncEngine?.syncNow()` optional-chaining. depends_on T3 because the view's locale/page reads migrate to $app/state in T3; if T3 hasn't landed the page still imports $app/stores at :3. High risk because it is the sole client-side fillout entry and touches the offline write path — the offline reconnect QA is mandatory. Behavior-preserving only; the controller is a .svelte.ts runes module so $state/$derived work outside a component.

tasks.title: Replace all native alert()/confirm() with a promise-based ConfirmDialog + Toast
tasks.scope: Introduce a global promise-based confirm dialog (wrapping the existing a11y-solid Dialog component, which already has a focus trap and focus restore) plus toast-based error notices, then replace all 14 native alert()/confirm() call sites across 13 files: participant consent-decline, designer destructive confirmations, admin flows, invite decline, and file-upload/import errors. Also resolve/justify the Dialog.svelte:154 svelte-ignore suppression.
tasks.approach: confirm.svelte.ts: `$state` holding `{ open, title, message, confirmLabel, cancelLabel, destructive, resolve }`; `confirmDialog(opts)` returns a Promise<boolean> resolved by the host's confirm/cancel handlers (resolve(false) on backdrop/Escape close via Dialog's onclose). ConfirmDialogHost renders `<Dialog bind:open title=... closable closeOnEscape>` with message body and a footer snippet using the shared Button component (destructive styling for delete confirms). Mount once in routes/+layout.svelte — it serves (app), (auth), (fillout), and invite routes. Convert each call site: `if (confirm(...)) { X }` becomes `if (await confirmDialog({...})) { X }` — make the enclosing handlers async (all are plain onclick/utility functions; none run inside non-async reactive contexts). alerts: Designer.svelte:74 and MediaManager.svelte:254 -> `toast.error(title, { message })` using lib/stores/toast.ts (Toast host already mounted). FileUpload.svelte:152 is participant-facing: surface the max-files message through the component's existing uploadErrors map/inline error rendering instead of a toast, so it appears next to the control. IMPORTANT: routes/(auth)/reset-password/+page.svelte:73 is `api.passwordReset.confirm(...)` — an API method, NOT a browser confirm; do not touch. Dialog.svelte:154 a11y_no_noninteractive_element_interactions: keep the pattern but add a one-line justification comment (backdrop-click dismissal on a decorative wrapper; keyboard path exists via Escape) — or move the handler to an explicitly role=presentation element.

apps/web/src/lib/modules/questions/file-upload/FileUpload.svelte (:152 alert -> inline validation message in the existing uploadErrors surface)

tasks.title: Scale question ARIA refactor: radiogroup for discrete variants, native slider focus for continuous
tasks.scope: Remove the invalid role=slider container that wraps focusable buttons in Scale.svelte. Buttons/stars variants become a proper radiogroup with role=radio children and roving tabindex; slider/visual-analog variants rely on the native range input's implicit slider role with a real :focus-visible treatment (the current opacity-0 range has no visible focus). Also add a justification comment to the MediaResponse a11y_media_has_caption suppression (participant-recorded media has no caption source).
tasks.approach: Container div (:148-158): drop `role="slider"`, `aria-valuemin/max/now`, and make role conditional — `role={isDiscrete ? 'radiogroup' : undefined}` where `isDiscrete = displayType === 'buttons' || displayType === 'stars'`; keep `aria-label="Rating scale"` (or question title) on the radiogroup; keep the existing onkeydown={handleKeyPress} for arrow/Home/End support but move `tabindex` off the container for discrete variants. Buttons variant: each `.scale-button` gets `role="radio"` + `aria-checked={value === point}` (replacing aria-pressed) + roving `tabindex={value === point || (value == null && point === question.config.min) ? 0 : -1}`; after arrow-key value changes, focus the newly-checked button (query by data-point attr). Stars: same radio pattern, keep the existing per-button aria-label 'Rate N out of M'. Slider variant: remove container tabindex (the native <input type=range> is the focus target and exposes valuemin/max/now natively — the 'unanswered reports min' problem disappears with the container role); add `.slider:focus-visible::-webkit-slider-thumb { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--primary)); outline-offset: 2px; }` + `::-moz-range-thumb` equivalent. Visual-analog: the opacity-0 range input needs `.va-track:has(input:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid hsl(var(--primary)); outline-offset: 2px; }`. Replace the now-dead `.scale-container:focus` rule (:282-286). MediaResponse.svelte:628: add an explanatory comment above the existing svelte-ignore (recorded participant media cannot have authored captions).
tasks.corrected_refs: All accurate. Scale.svelte container role="slider" :148-158 (role :153, aria-valuemin/max/now :154-156), buttons :198-219 (button :201, aria-pressed :208), stars :226-243 (button :229, aria-label :234), slider :159-173 (native range :161-173), visual-analog :250-277 (opacity-0 range :258-267 inside .va-track :252), `.scale-container:focus` :282-286, `.slider::-webkit-slider-thumb` from :299. Container onkeydown={handleKeyPress} at :151.

Container div (:148-158): remove role="slider", aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow; set `role={isDiscrete ? 'radiogroup' : undefined}`, keep aria-label and onkeydown={handleKeyPress}, and make tabindex conditional (drop it for discrete variants).

Buttons variant (:201-217): add `data-point={point}`, `role="radio"`, `aria-checked={value === point}` (replace aria-pressed), roving `tabindex={value === point || (value == null && point === question.config.min) ? 0 : -1}`.

tasks.title: Server perf: set-based cross-project analytics + batched sync_session inserts
tasks.scope: Rewrite the cross_project_analytics per-questionnaire loop (apps/server/src/api/sessions.rs:1036-1130) into three set-based queries, and collapse the sync_session per-row INSERT loops for responses (:2932-2955) and interaction_events (:2958-2978) into chunked multi-row inserts that preserve ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING dedup. The per-variable loop (:2981-3002) stays as-is (few rows, complex normalize/persist_session_variable logic). API response shapes are unchanged, so no contracts regeneration.
tasks.approach: Analytics: (1) summary — one query_as::<QuestionnaireSummary> with WHERE q.id = ANY($1) AND q.deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY q.id, q.name, q.project_id, q.status, collected into HashMap<Uuid, QuestionnaireSummary> (struct already carries id). (2) timing — one query returning (s.questionnaire_id, extract-epoch-ms) rows with questionnaire_id = ANY($1), grouped into HashMap<Uuid, Vec<f64>> in Rust. (3) samples — add load_numeric_samples_batch(executor, source, &[Uuid], key) that generalizes load_numeric_samples' where_parts[0] to s.questionnaire_id = ANY($1) and SELECTs s.questionnaire_id, returning HashMap<Uuid, Vec<NumericSample>>; keep the single-qid load_numeric_samples as a delegating wrapper so aggregate_sessions/compare_sessions call sites are untouched. Assemble per_questionnaire by iterating the caller-supplied questionnaire_ids order filtered by valid_set, preserving current skip-on-missing-summary semantics and the downstream all_timing_values/variable_means/pearson logic verbatim. Sync batching: use sqlx QueryBuilder::push_values chunked at 500 rows (9 cols responses / 6 cols events, well under the 65535-bind limit); for presented_at/answered_at keep Postgres-side parsing via push_bind(&str).push_unseparated("::timestamptz") to match the current $n::timestamptz cast semantics; append ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING and accumulate responses_synced/events_synced from each chunk's rows_affected() (batched rows_affected counts only actually-inserted rows, matching current per-row accounting).

Struct QuestionnaireSummary at sessions.rs:299 (carries id, name, project_id, status, total_responses, completed_sessions, avg_completion_time_ms). load_numeric_samples at :2191, where_parts[0]="s.questionnaire_id = $1", binds questionnaire_id as $1 and key as $2. Sync response loop :2932-2955 (INSERT 9 cols: session_id, question_id, value, reaction_time_us, presented_at::timestamptz, answered_at::timestamptz, metadata COALESCE, client_id, timing_provenance). Sync event loop :2958-2978 (INSERT 6 cols: session_id, event_type, question_id, timestamp_us, metadata COALESCE, client_id). Body item structs: SyncResponseItem at :2760 (client_id is Uuid NOT Option; presented_at/answered_at are Option<String>; has value, metadata, timing_provenance Option<serde_json::Value>), SyncEventItem at :2781 (client_id: Uuid, timestamp_us: i64). No test file cross_project_analytics.rs exists yet; sync_session_dedup.rs exists (3 tests).
step_by_step: Add a batched summary query: SELECT q.id, q.name, q.project_id, q.status, COUNT(DISTINCT r.id), COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN s.status='completed' THEN s.id END), AVG(...) FROM questionnaire_definitions q LEFT JOIN sessions s ... LEFT JOIN responses r ... WHERE q.id = ANY($1) AND q.deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY q.id, q.name, q.project_id, q.status; fetch_all into HashMap<Uuid, QuestionnaireSummary> keyed on summary.id.

Add a batched timing query: SELECT s.questionnaire_id, (EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (s.completed_at - s.started_at))*1000.0)::float8 FROM sessions s WHERE s.questionnaire_id = ANY($1) AND s.completed_at IS NOT NULL AND s.started_at IS NOT NULL; fold rows into HashMap<Uuid, Vec<f64>> in Rust.

Add fn load_numeric_samples_batch(executor, source, &[Uuid], key) generalizing load_numeric_samples: change where_parts[0] to "s.questionnaire_id = ANY($1)", add s.questionnaire_id to the SELECT list of both the Variable and Response branches, and return HashMap<Uuid, Vec<NumericSample>> (extend VariableProjectionRow/ResponseValueRow with a questionnaire_id: Uuid column). Keep the existing single-qid load_numeric_samples as a thin wrapper delegating to the batch fn (or leave it untouched for aggregate_sessions/compare_sessions — do NOT change those call sites).

Replace the for-loop at :1036-1129: iterate questionnaire_ids in original order, skip ids not in valid_set, skip ids missing from the summary HashMap (preserving current skip-on-missing semantics), and assemble per_questionnaire + all_timing_values + variable_means from the three HashMaps. Preserve the downstream all_timing_values/variable_means/overall stats/pearson_correlation logic (:1131-1175) verbatim.

In sync response loop: replace the per-row INSERT with sqlx QueryBuilder over body.responses chunked at 500. Use push_values; for each row bind session_id, question_id, value, reaction_time_us, then push_bind(presented_at as &Option<String>).push_unseparated("::timestamptz"), same for answered_at, then COALESCE metadata (push_bind + push_unseparated("::jsonb") mirroring current COALESCE($7,'{}')), client_id (Uuid), timing_provenance. Append " ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING" via push. Accumulate responses_synced += result.rows_affected() per chunk.

In sync event loop: same QueryBuilder chunking at 500 over body.events; bind session_id, event_type, question_id, timestamp_us, COALESCE metadata jsonb, client_id; ON CONFLICT (client_id) DO NOTHING; events_synced += rows_affected() per chunk.
Leave the per-variable loop (:2981-3002) unchanged.
Extend tests/sync_session_dedup.rs with a mixed-batch test: POST N rows where k share client_ids already present, assert responses_synced == N-k and DB row count correct.

Create tests/cross_project_analytics.rs using the get_test_pool / DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS fixture pattern (see tests/rls_enforcement.rs): seed 2 questionnaires + completed sessions + responses, run the batched summary/timing/samples queries, assert per-questionnaire grouping equals per-qid results.
