Live QA lands with P8-T4 (the new UI drives this endpoint end-to-end).
tasks.precision_notes: Watch the ::timestamptz cast: current code binds presented_at/answered_at as Option<String> with $n::timestamptz — in QueryBuilder use push_bind(&item.presented_at).push_unseparated("::timestamptz") to keep Postgres-side parse semantics; do NOT parse to chrono in Rust. client_id is a required Uuid (not Option) on both sync item structs, so no NULL-conflict edge case. 500 rows * 9 cols = 4500 binds, far under 65535. rows_affected() with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING counts only inserted rows, matching the current per-row `> 0` accounting exactly. Keep GROUP BY columns identical (q.id, q.name, q.project_id, q.status) so the aggregate is unchanged.

tasks.title: Lazy-load mathjs VariableEngine off the fillout route's critical path
tasks.scope: Remove the module-top-level create(all) side effect from VariableEngine, make @qdesigner/scripting-engine tree-shakeable, and dynamically import FilloutRuntime (the only value-level consumer chain on the fillout route) so the mathjs chunk is no longer a static dependency of the public /q/[code] route and its instantiation cost is deferred to first formula use.
tasks.approach: In VariableEngine.ts replace the module-level `const math = create(all); math.import(BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS,{override:true})` with a lazy singleton: `let mathInstance: MathJsInstance | null = null; function getMath() { if (!mathInstance) { mathInstance = create(all as FactoryFunctionMap); mathInstance.import(BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS, { override: true }); } return mathInstance; }`, and swap the six math.* call sites (evaluate :125/:257, parse :129/:277, import :165) to getMath().* — the class API stays fully synchronous, so no consumer changes. Audit the barrel's modules (types/evaluator/functions/parser/ast-evaluator/policies/MonacoConfig are declaration-only; ScriptWorker is a separate worker entry) then add "sideEffects": false to packages/scripting-engine/package.json so Rollup can drop the now-pure VariableEngine module from consumers that only import FormulaParser/ASTEvaluator (variableInterpolation.ts → ModularRenderer → fillout page). In the fillout page, change line 11 to `import type { FilloutRuntime } from '$lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime'` and inside the already-async initializeRuntime() do `const { FilloutRuntime } = await import('$lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime')` immediately before the `runtime = new FilloutRuntime({...})` at :496 — the vite manualChunks 'mathjs' rule (vite.config.ts:73) then makes mathjs an async chunk of that dynamic subgraph. Do NOT touch QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:123 or FilloutRuntime.ts:54 construction (both stay sync).
tasks.corrected_refs: All refs accurate. VariableEngine.ts: create(all) at :7-8, math.import(BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS) at :38, call sites math.evaluate :125/:257, math.parse :129/:277, math.import :165. Fillout page: static import FilloutRuntime at :11, initializeRuntime() at :452, new FilloutRuntime at :496. vite.config.ts manualChunks at :70, mathjs rule at :73. NOTE: packages/scripting-engine/package.json main = "./src/index.ts" — consumers import TS source (Vite-compiled), not a prebuilt dist; sideEffects:false still lets Rollup drop the pure VariableEngine module.
step_by_step: In VariableEngine.ts, remove the module-level `const factories = all as FactoryFunctionMap; const math = create(factories);` (:7-8) and the top-level `math.import(BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS, { override: true });` (:38).

Add `let mathInstance: MathJsInstance | null = null; function getMath() { if (!mathInstance) { mathInstance = create(all as FactoryFunctionMap); mathInstance.import(BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS, { override: true }); } return mathInstance; }` (import MathJsInstance type from mathjs).

Replace the 5 call sites: :125 math.evaluate -> getMath().evaluate, :129 math.parse -> getMath().parse, :165 math.import -> getMath().import, :257 math.evaluate -> getMath().evaluate, :277 math.parse -> getMath().parse. Class API stays synchronous; no consumer changes.
Add `"sideEffects": false` to packages/scripting-engine/package.json (top-level, sibling of main/exports).
In (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte change line 11 to a type-only import: `import type { FilloutRuntime } from '$lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime';`.

Inside initializeRuntime(), immediately before the `runtime = new FilloutRuntime({...})` at :496, add `const { FilloutRuntime } = await import('$lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime');` (the fn is already async and this line is inside the try block).
Do NOT touch QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:123 or FilloutRuntime.ts:54 constructor calls — both remain synchronous.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/scripting-engine test (223 tests green — formula evaluation still works)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web build
After build, inspect .svelte-kit/output/client build manifest / modulepreload for the q/[code] route — its initial chunk graph must NOT reference the mathjs chunk (mathjs must be an async chunk).

Live QA (required): open a published questionnaire with a computed-variable formula + a {{var}} text interpolation in chromium — network tab shows the mathjs chunk fetched only after runtime init (post-Start), and the computed variable + conditional logic evaluate correctly; smoke the designer RealtimePreview and LiveTestRunner (other VariableEngine consumers).
tasks.precision_notes: Because scripting-engine's `main` is TS source compiled by the consumer's Vite, tree-shaking depends on Rollup seeing VariableEngine as side-effect-free — the sideEffects:false flag plus removing the module-level create(all) is what makes it droppable for consumers that only touch FormulaParser/ASTEvaluator (variableInterpolation.ts). Verify no other module-top-level side effects exist in the barrel (types/evaluator/functions/parser/ast-evaluator/policies/customFunctions/MonacoConfig are declaration-only; ScriptWorker is a separate worker entry). The lazy singleton must be module-private, not per-instance, to preserve the current single shared math instance semantics.

tasks.title: Hoist WebGLRenderer per-frame and per-draw allocations
tasks.scope: Remove steady-state allocations from the reaction-timing render loop in WebGLRenderer: the per-rAF sorted-layer array rebuild, the duplicate per-frame RenderContext objects, unnecessary FrameSample/FrameStats construction when nobody subscribes, and drawGeometry's per-draw number[] + two Float32Array allocations with DYNAMIC_DRAW re-upload. Behavior and timing semantics must be bit-identical; FrameSample objects delivered to subscribers stay freshly allocated because ReactionEngine retains them (frameLog.push(sample) at ReactionEngine.ts:648).
tasks.approach: (1) Sorted layers: add `private sortedLayers: number[] = []` rebuilt only in addRenderable/removeRenderable/clearRenderables; renderFrame iterates the cache instead of Array.from(this.renderablesByLayer.keys()).sort() at :432. (2) RenderContext: build ONE context object per rAF tick and pass it to both renderFrame's renderable.render() pass (:430) and the frameCallbacks dispatch (:367), replacing the two createRenderContext calls; before converting to a persistent mutate-in-place object, audit the Renderable implementations driven by ReactionEngine — if any retains the context across frames, keep the single per-frame allocation (still halves churn). (3) Callback guard: when this.frameCallbacks.size === 0, skip constructing the context-for-callbacks, the FrameSample (:368-374), and the getStats() FrameStats object (:376) entirely; when subscribers exist keep allocating fresh FrameSample/FrameStats since ReactionEngine.subscribeFrameLogging pushes the sample into frameLog for timing provenance — pooling would corrupt the log. (4) drawGeometry: keep two preallocated grow-on-demand Float32Array scratch buffers (positions, texcoords); rewrite toClipSpace to write clip-space values directly into the position scratch (dropping the intermediate number[]); cache defaultTexCoords per vertexCount in a Map<number, Float32Array>; upload via gl.bufferData with a .subarray(0, n) view (or bufferSubData over a persistently sized VBO).
files: apps/web/src/lib/renderer/WebGLRenderer.ts (render loop :340-385; renderFrame :420-445; createRenderContext :409-418; getStats :832-858; addRenderable/removeRenderable/clearRenderables :915-943; drawGeometry :776-807; toClipSpace :808-824; defaultTexCoords :825-831)
tasks.corrected_refs: All refs exact. render loop :339-383 (createRenderContext :367 + FrameSample :368-374 + getStats :375 + callback dispatch :376-378); renderFrame :420-443 (second createRenderContext :430, sorted layers Array.from(...keys()).sort at :432); createRenderContext :409-418; drawGeometry :776-806 (toClipSpace :786, new Float32Array(clipVertices) :789, new Float32Array(texCoords||fallback) :793, both DYNAMIC_DRAW); toClipSpace :808-822 (returns new number[]); defaultTexCoords :824-830 (returns new number[]); getStats :832; addRenderable :915-923, removeRenderable :925-939, clearRenderables :941-944.
step_by_step: Add `private sortedLayers: number[] = []` and a private `rebuildSortedLayers()` that sets it to Array.from(this.renderablesByLayer.keys()).sort((a,b)=>a-b). Call it at the end of addRenderable, removeRenderable (only when a layer set is deleted), and clearRenderables. In renderFrame :432, iterate this.sortedLayers instead of rebuilding.

Build ONE RenderContext per rAF tick: in the render closure compute the context once (via createRenderContext or an inline mutate-in-place object) and pass it to both renderFrame (add a `context` param, drop the :430 createRenderContext) and the frameCallbacks dispatch (:377), replacing the two allocations. Before making it a persistent mutate-in-place object, audit the Renderable implementations driven by ReactionEngine — if any retains the context across frames, keep a single fresh per-frame allocation (still halves churn).

Guard the callback path: when this.frameCallbacks.size === 0, skip constructing the FrameSample (:368-374) and the getStats() call (:375) entirely. When subscribers exist, keep allocating fresh FrameSample/FrameStats (ReactionEngine.subscribeFrameLogging pushes the sample into frameLog at ReactionEngine.ts:648 — pooling would corrupt the timing log).

In drawGeometry: add two grow-on-demand Float32Array scratch fields (positionScratch, texCoordScratch). Rewrite toClipSpace into a variant that writes clip-space values directly into positionScratch (dropping the intermediate number[]), growing the buffer when pixelVertices.length exceeds capacity.
Cache defaultTexCoords per vertexCount in a Map<number, Float32Array>; reuse for the fallback path.
Upload via gl.bufferData with a .subarray(0, n) view of the scratch buffers (or bufferSubData over a persistently sized VBO) instead of `new Float32Array(...)` each draw.

Extend WebGLRenderer.test.ts: (a) sortedLayers invalidation on add/remove/clear keeps ascending order; (b) drawGeometry via scratch path produces identical clip-space values to the old path; (c) two consecutive frames deliver distinct FrameSample objects to a subscriber (retention safety).
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (extended WebGLRenderer.test.ts green)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web build

Live QA (required, timing-sensitive): run a reaction-time questionnaire in chromium — stimuli draw correctly across trials, trial results carry a non-empty frame log and sane timing_provenance onsets, dropped-frame stats still update; sanity-compare a few reaction times against pre-change behavior.
tasks.precision_notes: Bit-identical output is the bar: toClipSpace math (clipX=(x/w)*2-1, clipY=-((y/h)*2-1)) must be reproduced exactly when writing into the scratch buffer. FrameSample delivered to subscribers MUST stay freshly allocated per frame (ReactionEngine retains it in frameLog) — only skip allocation when there are zero subscribers. The context object can be reused only if no ReactionEngine-driven Renderable retains it; default to a single fresh per-frame context if unsure (still removes the second per-frame allocation). Scratch buffer growth must never shrink mid-session to avoid reallocation churn.

tasks.title: Real DNS TXT domain-ownership verification (server + admin UI)
tasks.scope: Replace the verify_domain auto-verify stub (organizations.rs:1495-1527) with an actual DNS TXT ownership check against the _qdesigner.{domain} record the UI already instructs admins to create (admin/domains/+page.svelte:336-341), and wire handleVerifyDomain (:129-140) to call it, surfacing honest verified/pending outcomes. This makes the org auto-join feature completable through the product. (It also substantially addresses the verify_domain half of F012, which remains owned by the security phase.)
tasks.approach: Server: keep the OrgRole::Admin gate; fetch the domain row's (domain, verification_token); factor a pure `fn txt_records_contain_token(records: &[String], token: &str) -> bool` (trim whitespace/surrounding quotes, exact match) for unit testing; perform the lookup with hickory_resolver::TokioAsyncResolver::tokio_from_system_conf() querying TXT on `_qdesigner.{domain}` under a ~3s tokio::time::timeout; on token match run the existing UPDATE ... SET verified_at = NOW() and return 200 {verified: true, message}; on missing/mismatched records return 200 {verified: false, message, records_found} so the UI can render guidance rather than an exception (NXDOMAIN and timeout map to verified:false with distinct messages; resolver construction failure maps to ApiError::Internal). Update the utoipa path response to the new VerifyDomainResponse schema and run pnpm contracts:generate. Frontend: make handleVerifyDomain async — call verifyDomain(orgId, domainId); verified → success message + await loadDomains() (status chip flips to Verified, auto-join becomes meaningful); not verified → notice showing the exact TXT name/value to create and the failure reason; drop the misleading .well-known instructions block (:340-341) since only DNS TXT is implemented, and remove the now-false 'not available yet' copy.
files: apps/server/Cargo.toml (add hickory-resolver with tokio runtime feature)
apps/server/src/api/organizations.rs (verify_domain :1495-1527 + new pure helper + VerifyDomainResponse type; utoipa annotations)
tasks.corrected_refs: Server verify_domain at organizations.rs:1495-1528. IMPORTANT: it does NOT currently return 'pending' — it UNCONDITIONALLY auto-verifies (comment 'In development, auto-verify the domain' + UPDATE organization_domains SET verified_at = NOW() at :1514, returns {message:'Domain verified'}). utoipa response currently declares body = crate::openapi::MessageResponse (:1489). OrgRole::Admin gate already present (:1505-1511). Domain row has verification_token column (:1406). hickory-resolver is NOT in apps/server/Cargo.toml (must add). Frontend handleVerifyDomain at admin/domains/+page.svelte:129 does NOT call the server endpoint at all — it only sets a `notice` string ('not available yet') at :139; instruction markup shows DNS TXT name at :337 and a .well-known URL at :340. verifyDomain client helper at domain-verification.ts:81.
step_by_step: Add hickory-resolver (with tokio runtime feature) to apps/server/Cargo.toml.
In organizations.rs, factor a pure `fn txt_records_contain_token(records: &[String], token: &str) -> bool` (trim whitespace and surrounding quotes, exact match) for unit testing.
Rewrite verify_domain: keep the OrgRole::Admin gate; SELECT (domain, verification_token) for the row (id=$1, organization_id=$2); if not found return 404.
Perform a TXT lookup on `_qdesigner.{domain}` via hickory_resolver::TokioAsyncResolver::tokio_from_system_conf() under a ~3s tokio::time::timeout. Resolver construction failure -> ApiError::Internal.
On token match: run the existing UPDATE ... SET verified_at = NOW() and return 200 {verified:true, message}.
On missing/mismatched records: return 200 {verified:false, message, records_found}. NXDOMAIN and timeout map to verified:false with distinct messages (do NOT error).

Define a VerifyDomainResponse struct (verified: bool, message: String, records_found: Vec<String>) and update the utoipa response body from MessageResponse to VerifyDomainResponse; run pnpm contracts:generate.
Add bin unit tests for txt_records_contain_token (quoted, multi-string, whitespace, mismatch).

Frontend: make handleVerifyDomain async — call verifyDomain(orgId, domainId) (domain-verification.ts:81, pass through the new verified flag + message); on verified -> success message + await loadDomains() (status chip flips to Verified); on not-verified -> notice with the exact TXT name/value + failure reason.
Remove the misleading .well-known instructions block (:340) and the 'not available yet' copy (:139/:332) since only DNS TXT is implemented.
verification: cargo check --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml + new bin unit tests for txt_records_contain_token

tasks.title: Self-service account deletion (GDPR erasure path)
tasks.scope: Add DELETE /api/users/me (password-confirmed, sole-owner-guarded soft-delete + PII anonymization + token revocation) and replace the settings-page 'not yet available' copy (settings/+page.svelte:150-159) with a real delete-account flow behind a confirmation Dialog. Research sessions/responses are retained pseudonymously (user PII scrubbed, user_id link kept) — documented in the handler and the dialog copy. Depends on P8-T8 solely to serialize contracts regeneration (both rewrite packages/contracts/src/generated + openapi.json).
tasks.approach: Server: handler takes AuthenticatedUser + State + Tx + Json<DeleteAccountRequest{password}>. (1) Load users.password_hash and verify via the existing auth::password verify function — mismatch → 401. (2) Guard: one query finding organizations where the caller holds the owner role in organization_members AND the org has other active members → if any, return 409 Conflict naming the orgs ('transfer ownership first'). (3) In the request tx: DELETE the caller's organization_members rows; soft-delete (deleted_at = NOW()) organizations where they were the sole member; anonymize the users row — email = format!("deleted-{}@anonymized.invalid", user_id), full_name/avatar_url = NULL, password_hash = a fixed invalid sentinel, deleted_at = NOW() (login already filters deleted_at IS NULL, auth.rs:120-ish). (4) After the middleware commits, revoke credentials via the existing session::revoke_all_user_tokens (pattern at auth.rs:293/:694, runs on &state.pool outside the tx). Sessions/responses untouched. Register the utoipa path, regenerate contracts. Frontend: api.users.deleteAccount(password) calling the generated SDK op; in settings, a destructive-variant Button opens Dialog (ui/overlays/Dialog.svelte) containing a password Input inside FormGroup plus explicit consequence copy; confirm calls deleteAccount with a loading state, maps 401 → 'wrong password' and 409 → the ownership-transfer Alert; on success await auth.signOut() (auth.ts:184) then goto('/login').

epics.title: True resumable sessions: rehydrate the runtime from persisted answers across reload and device
epics.rationale: Closes the single largest gap in the matrix ('Resumable across RELOAD/DEVICE = missing'). Today resume() restarts at question 0 and getSessionResponses is dead code, so any interruption loses all progress — fatal for long/reaction studies. SOTA offline fillout must let a participant close the tab, switch devices, or lose power and continue exactly where they left off against the pinned version.
data_model_changes: FilloutSession (indexeddb.ts): add lastItemIndex:number, lastPageId:string, answeredQuestionIds:string[] (or derive from filloutResponses), updatedAt:number — a durable progress cursor written on every answer.
Add a Dexie v4 upgrade adding an index on filloutResponses.questionId to support fast last-answered lookup; no destructive change.

Server: extend GET /api/sessions/{id} response (already reachable, +page.ts:117) to include prior responses + session_variables so cross-device resume can hydrate (reuse existing get_session/responses/variables handlers; add an include=responses,variables query or a combined resume DTO).
step_by_step: Add QuestionnaireRuntime.hydrate(snapshot: { responses: Response[]; variables: Record<string,unknown>; itemIndex?: number; pageId?: string }): seed this.session.responses, replay each into responseCollector, restore this.currentItemIndex/currentPage, and re-run VariableEngine.setVariable for each stored var before start() so {{var}} interpolation is correct on resume.

Guard hydrate so already-answered items are skipped by showCurrentItem: compute the first unanswered item index from answeredQuestionIds rather than trusting a stored counter (defends against a partial write).
In FilloutRuntime constructor config add optional resumeSnapshot; when present, call this.runtime.hydrate(resumeSnapshot) after construction and before start().

Write the progress cursor: in FilloutRuntime.updateProgress() also persist lastItemIndex/lastPageId/answeredQuestionIds via OfflineSessionService.updateProgress (extend it to accept these typed fields), so a reload has an authoritative cursor even before any sync.

In +page.ts load: when resuming (existingSession online OR findActiveSession offline), assemble a resumeSnapshot — offline from OfflineResponsePersistence.getSessionResponses/getSessionVariables(session.id); online (cross-device) from the extended GET /api/sessions/{id} include=responses,variables — and return it in PageData.

Map the persisted StoredFilloutResponse rows back into the runtime Response shape (reuse the inverse of ResponsePersistenceService.saveResponse field mapping; centralize the mapping in a new responseMapping.ts so save and hydrate stay in lockstep).
In +page.svelte initializeRuntime, thread data.resumeSnapshot into new FilloutRuntime({...}); show a 'Resuming where you left off (question N of M)' toast.

Handle the pinnedFallback case: if the device lacks the pinned snapshot but the server has responses, still hydrate answers but surface a non-blocking notice that the definition is the latest, not the pinned version.
Add a 'completed already' short-circuit: if the resumed session status==='completed', route straight to CompletionScreen instead of re-running.
Emit a resume interaction event (eventType:'session_resumed', metadata:{fromDevice:boolean, restoredCount}) through OfflineResponsePersistence.saveEvent for audit.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit: hydrate() seeds N responses and start() presents item N+1 (assert currentItemIndex and no re-presentation of answered items).
Live QA (prod build, agent-browser): answer 5 of 10 questions, hard-reload /q/CODE?sid=..., confirm it resumes at question 6 with prior answers intact and {{var}} values correct.
Cross-device demo: answer 3 questions online on device A, open the same ?sid= URL on device B, confirm the server-fetched snapshot resumes at question 4.
Offline demo: go offline mid-session, reload, confirm resume from IndexedDB cursor with zero network.

epics.title: Encrypt fillout data at rest (WebCrypto AES-GCM) with a purge-and-destroy key lifecycle
epics.rationale: Matrix 'Encryption-at-rest = missing'. The offline store holds psychological-research PII in plaintext (indexeddb.ts:114-143) on potentially shared/kiosk research devices. P2-T4 only purges after sync; a bulletproof product encrypts every response/variable/session row at rest and destroys the key on shared-device clear so unsynced data left on the device is unreadable.
data_model_changes: New Dexie table filloutKeys: { sessionId:string (PK); wrappedKey:ArrayBuffer; iv:Uint8Array; createdAt:number } storing a per-session AES-GCM key wrapped by a device root key.

filloutResponses/filloutVariables/filloutSessions.metadata: store an encrypted envelope { iv:Uint8Array; ciphertext:ArrayBuffer } in the value/metadata slots instead of plaintext (indexes stay on non-sensitive columns sessionId/clientId/synced/questionId — those remain cleartext for querying).
No server schema change: the sync payload decrypts to the existing wire shape at the FilloutSyncEngine boundary.
step_by_step: Add lib/fillout/services/crypto/FilloutCrypto.ts: deriveOrLoadDeviceRootKey() using a non-extractable CryptoKey persisted via IndexedDB (crypto.subtle.generateKey AES-GCM, stored as a CryptoKey object which IndexedDB can persist without exposing raw bytes), and per-session data keys wrapped/unwrapped with it.

Wrap OfflineResponsePersistence write paths: encrypt data.value and data.metadata (and reactionTime stays cleartext for local scoring? no — encrypt value/metadata only; keep timing columns cleartext for local analytics) before db.filloutResponses.add; decrypt in getUnsyncedResponses/getSessionResponses.
Wrap OfflineSessionService metadata (consent, urlParams, fingerprint) similarly; keep id/questionnaireId/status/version indexes cleartext.
In FilloutSyncEngine.syncSession, decrypt each row into the plaintext sync payload just before api.sessions.sync (the network body is TLS-protected; only at-rest is encrypted).

On successful final sync (completeSession path) call a new purgeSessionData(sessionId) that deletes the session's responses/events/variables AND its filloutKeys row (fold into P2-T4's purge so the two do not diverge).

Add clearThisDevice(): wipe ALL fillout tables + filloutKeys + Cache-API fillout-media-v2, rendering any residual unsynced ciphertext permanently unreadable; expose it from the shared-device UX (E-OFF-6).
Feature-detect crypto.subtle; if unavailable (insecure context) fall back to plaintext with a logged warning and a UX banner that the device is not secure (do not hard-fail participation).
Version-gate the Dexie upgrade (v4/v5) so existing plaintext rows are migrated: read, encrypt, rewrite inside the upgrade transaction.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit: round-trip encrypt→store→getUnsynced→decrypt equals original; wrong key throws; purge deletes key row.
Live QA: answer questions offline, open DevTools > Application > IndexedDB, confirm filloutResponses.value/metadata are ciphertext envelopes not readable JSON.
Demo: sync, then confirm purgeSessionData removed the rows and the key; trigger clearThisDevice and confirm every fillout table + media cache is empty.

epics.title: Bulletproof PWA app-shell: precache the fillout runtime + offline SPA navigation + dev testability
epics.rationale: Matrix 'PWA app-shell precache = partial'. sw.js precaches only '/' and offline.html; a cold-offline visit to a never-loaded /q/CODE cannot boot because the navigation document and SPA fallback are not precached, and SW is disabled in dev so offline is unverifiable. SOTA requires the fillout runtime to load fully offline after a single prior online visit, on any code whose definition is cached.
data_model_changes: none — service-worker + build config only.
step_by_step: Add a SvelteKit SPA fallback (adapter fallback 200.html) or the prerendered shell for the (fillout) group, and add its path to STATIC_CACHE_URLS in sw.js so a document navigation to /q/* resolves to the cached shell offline.

Add a navigation-request handler in sw.js fetch: for mode==='navigate' requests under /q/, serve networkFirst falling back to the cached SPA shell (NOT offline.html) so client routing + +page.ts offline branch take over.

Ensure the vite build-manifest plugin (vite.config.ts:16-40) enumerates the fillout route chunks (verify the code-split chunk for (fillout)/q/[code] is in files[]) so install-time bundle caching covers the runtime, ModularRenderer, and module registry.

Add an explicit 'prepare offline' action surfaced on WelcomeScreen: call FilloutOfflineSyncService.syncForOffline(code, definition) + requestPersistentStorage() so a researcher/participant can pre-provision a kiosk before going offline.
Make the SW usable in a dev/preview offline-QA mode: replace the blanket dev unregister (offline.ts:21-56) with a VITE_SW_DEV flag so offline can be exercised without a full prod deploy.
Bump CACHE_NAME and add the shell + fillout chunks to the activate-time allowlist; keep the existing fillout-media-v2 handling.

Add a service-worker integration test (or Playwright offline lane, tie into remediation P3-T4) that loads /q/CODE online once, goes offline (context.setOffline), and asserts the runtime boots and accepts an answer.
new_files: apps/web/src/routes/(fillout)/+layout.ts (prerender/fallback config for the fillout shell, if adapter-specific)
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web build && confirm build-manifest.json lists the fillout route chunk.
Live QA: visit /q/CODE online once, kill the backend + set browser offline, hard-navigate to /q/CODE, confirm welcome→consent→runtime all render and an answer persists offline.
Playwright offline-coldboot spec green in the CI fullstack lane (P3-T4).
Demo: airplane-mode a tablet after one online visit and complete the whole questionnaire, then reconnect and watch it sync.

epics.title: Sync hardening: ack-reconciled marking, per-session retry, large-payload chunking, unify the two offline queues
epics.rationale: Matrix 'Robust sync = partial' and 'Two-path coherence = partial'. FilloutSyncEngine marks records synced from local ids without confirming which client_ids the server accepted, schedules retries only on outer failure, sends unbounded payloads, and marks ALL a session's variables synced regardless of the batch. A separate SW request-queue retries the same mutations independently. SOTA sync is ack-driven, chunked, and single-sourced.
data_model_changes: Server api.sessions.sync response: return accepted_client_ids:string[] (responses+events) and accepted_variable_names:string[] so the client marks synced only what the server durably wrote (extends the existing sync handler touched by P8-T1's batched inserts).
filloutVariables: add clientId:string so variable acks are per-record, replacing the mark-all-by-session semantics.
step_by_step: Change FilloutSyncEngine.syncSession to mark synced by the server-returned accepted_client_ids set, not by the locally-collected id list (FilloutSyncEngine.ts:232-240); leave unacked rows synced=0 for the next pass.

Add chunking: split responses/events into batches of N (e.g. 200) and loop api.sessions.sync per chunk within the session, so a large reaction run never exceeds the server body limit; aggregate SyncResult across chunks.

Add per-session retry scheduling: on a per-session catch (currently only pushes to errors, :98-101) call scheduleRetry so a single failing session backs off independently instead of waiting for the next online/periodic tick.
Give filloutVariables a clientId and switch markVariablesSynced to mark by accepted names/ids (OfflineResponsePersistence.ts:213-218), fixing the mark-all race.

Deconflict the SW request-queue: exclude /api/sessions/{id}/sync, submit-response, submit-events, and session PUT progress from fetchWithOfflineQueue (sw.js:253-260) since FilloutSyncEngine already owns those idempotently — prevents double retry paths racing on the same client_ids.
Add a bounded in-flight guard + jittered backoff to avoid thundering-herd re-sync when many tabs come online simultaneously.
Surface a structured SyncResult (chunks, accepted, rejected, retriable) to the page so E-OFF-6 can render a real queued-count/last-synced.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit: server returns a subset accepted set → only those rows flip to synced=1; the rest retry next pass.
Unit: 500 queued responses split into 3 chunked sync calls; SyncResult totals equal the sum.
Live QA: queue a large offline reaction run, reconnect, watch the network panel show chunked sync calls and IndexedDB drain to zero with no duplicate rows server-side (verify by client_id count).
Demo: force one session's sync to 500, confirm it backs off and recovers independently while other sessions sync.

epics.title: Integrity + no-silent-loss: record checksums, write-verify, a sync ledger, and eviction guards
epics.rationale: Matrix 'Sync integrity = missing' and 'Storage durability = partial'. There is no defense against a partial IndexedDB write, a silently-evicted store, or a permanently-failing row disappearing. Research data must be provably intact end-to-end: every record checksummed, every write verified, every unsyncable row escalated rather than lost.
data_model_changes: filloutResponses/filloutEvents/filloutVariables: add checksum:string (SHA-256 of the canonicalized record) and attempts:number + lastError?:string for dead-letter tracking.

New Dexie table filloutSyncLedger: { clientId:string PK; sessionId; kind:'response'|'event'|'variable'; state:'pending'|'acked'|'deadletter'; attempts; updatedAt } — an append-only audit of every record's sync journey.
getStorageUsage (indexeddb.ts:401-434): include the fillout tables in the accounting.
step_by_step: Compute a SHA-256 checksum (crypto.subtle.digest, canonical JSON) at write time in OfflineResponsePersistence and store it on the record; recompute+verify on read in getUnsynced* and drop-with-escalation if mismatched.

Write-verify: after db.filloutResponses.add, re-read by clientId and assert presence+checksum before returning success to the runtime, so a failed durable write surfaces immediately instead of being assumed.

Populate filloutSyncLedger on every enqueue and transition entries to acked using E-OFF-4's accepted_client_ids; after attempts>=K without ack, mark deadletter and emit a visible error (never delete silently).

Add an eviction guard: on fillout start, if requestPersistentStorage() was denied AND getStorageEstimate shows <X MB headroom, warn the participant/researcher and block starting a long offline run rather than risk mid-study eviction.

Add a reconcile() that on reconnect diffs local ledger 'acked' rows against a lightweight server GET /api/sessions/{id}/synced-client-ids to detect any client-side over-marking, re-queuing anything the server does not actually have.
Expose ledger stats (pending/deadletter counts) to the connectivity UX (E-OFF-6) and to a researcher-facing 'data integrity' readout.
Add a manual 'export unsynced data' escape hatch (download the pending IndexedDB rows as JSON) so a device that can never reach the server is still recoverable.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit: tampering a stored record's value fails checksum verify and escalates rather than syncing corrupt data.
Unit: a row that 500s K times moves to deadletter and raises a visible error, not a silent delete.
Demo: reconcile() detects a locally-acked-but-server-missing row (simulate by clearing a server insert) and re-queues it.
Live QA: fill getStorageEstimate headroom low and confirm the pre-start eviction warning fires.

epics.title: Participant connectivity UX: queued-count, last-synced, manual sync, save-and-clear, offline quota disclosure
epics.rationale: Matrix 'Connectivity UX = partial' and 'Offline quota = missing'. The status bar shows only a transient state with no queued-count, no last-synced time, no manual control, and no shared-device exit. Participants on flaky research networks need honest, actionable feedback and a safe way to hand a kiosk to the next person.
data_model_changes: none (reads from FilloutSyncEngine SyncResult + SyncLedger stats added in E-OFF-4/E-OFF-5).
step_by_step: Add a persistent, non-transient sync widget replacing the auto-hiding badge (+page.svelte:562-593): shows online/offline, 'N answers pending', and 'last synced HH:MM' sourced from ledger pending counts and the last successful SyncResult.
Wire a manual 'Sync now' button that calls syncEngine.syncNow() and reflects chunk progress; disable while offline with a tooltip.

Add a 'Save & exit (clear this device)' action for shared/kiosk devices: force a final sync, then on success call the E-OFF-2 clearThisDevice()/E-OFF-3 purge so no residual PII remains; if unsynced rows exist, require explicit confirmation and offer the E-OFF-5 export escape hatch first.

Show an honest offline-quota disclosure: when quotaGroups exist but navigator.onLine is false (+page.svelte:417), inform the participant their eligibility will be re-checked on submission rather than silently admitting them (pairs with server-side enforcement in P1-T4).
Localize all new strings through Paraglide so they work offline; ensure the widget is theme-aware and uses semantic tokens (avoid the F020-class raw-triplet regression already fixed by P1-T2).
Add aria-live to the sync widget so screen-reader users hear 'N answers pending / all saved' (coheres with A11y remediation P7-T1).
Persist a lightweight 'unsynced on this device' banner across reloads so a participant who closed the tab with pending data is prompted to reconnect.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Component test: SyncStatusPanel renders the pending count from a mocked ledger and toggles Sync-now enablement on online/offline.
Live QA: answer offline, confirm '3 answers pending' shows; reconnect and confirm it drains to 'All saved · last synced now'.
Demo: 'Save & exit (clear this device)' on a synced session leaves IndexedDB fillout tables + media cache empty; on an unsynced session it forces sync/export first.
Live QA: load an offline questionnaire with quotas and confirm the eligibility-recheck disclosure appears.

epics.title: Multi-channel precision response capture: gamepad, key-hold/release, and spatial-response scoring
epics.rationale: 'Precision keying / response capture' is half the pillar's name yet only keydown-keyboard is fully functional. Closes the 'gamepad' (missing), 'key-hold/release/multi-key' (missing) and 'mouse/touch scoring' (partial) SOTA rows. jsPsych/Gorilla/Labvanced/PsychoPy all offer gamepad and button-hold/release timing and score spatial clicks - required for Go/NoGo button boxes, Posner spatial responses and visual-search target selection.
data_model_changes: Extend ReactionResponseMode (types.ts:3) to 'keyboard'|'mouse'|'touch'|'gamepad'
Add to ReactionResponseCapture (types.ts:117): optional releaseTimestamp, holdDurationMs, gamepadButtonIndex, responseDevice
Add ReactionTrialConfig fields (types.ts:91): captureKeyUp?:boolean, targetRegion?:{x,y,radius}, gamepadButtonMap?:Record<number,string>
Extend TimingMethod union (types.ts:110) with 'gamepad.timestamp' threaded through ReactionTrialProvenance.responseMethod
step_by_step: In ReactionEngine.setupResponseCapture (ReactionEngine.ts:724) add a 'gamepad' branch: on trial start begin a rAF-driven poll of navigator.getGamepads(); on the first button 0->1 transition whose index is in gamepadButtonMap, timestamp via performance.now() and call handleResponse('gamepad', mappedValue, time, 'gamepad.timestamp')
Add key-up capture: when config.captureKeyUp, register a 'keyup' listener recording releaseTimestamp and holdDurationMs = release - keydownTime for the matching key
Generalize handleResponse (ReactionEngine.ts:799) to carry release/hold fields and stamp responseDevice

Rewrite evaluateCorrectness (ReactionEngine.ts:880-902): for object {x,y} values with config.targetRegion, mark correct when the normalized point is within radius; keep string equality for keyboard/gamepad-mapped values
Add pointInRegion(normalizedPoint, region) helper with viewport-independent math
Thread responseDevice/holdDurationMs/gamepadButtonIndex into the runtime->response mapping in ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:146 and ReactionExperimentRuntime.ts:125 instead of dropping them
Add a responseMode selector to ReactionTimeDesigner.svelte writing config.response.mode; make the compiler (reaction-compiler.ts:144,733) read config.response.mode instead of hardcoding 'keyboard'
Add a press-to-bind gamepad button-mapping widget in the designer writing gamepadButtonMap
Validate response.mode/targetRegion/gamepadButtonMap in reaction-normalize.ts
Extend the deterministic test harness (routes/test-runtime) with an injectable gamepad-state source to assert gamepad onset->response without hardware
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit tests: spatialHit inside/outside radius; key-hold duration = release-press; GamepadPoller emits one response on 0->1 transition only

Live QA (agent-browser): author a Go/NoGo custom trial responseMode=gamepad, drive a virtual gamepad via devtools, confirm a captured RT with method 'gamepad.timestamp' and responseDevice='gamepad' persisted
Demo: dot-probe authored with responseMode=touch + targetRegion on the probe; tap the probe, verify isCorrect=true and RT recorded (today always false)

epics.title: Standard-paradigm library expansion: Go/NoGo, SART, Simon, Posner, visual search, Sternberg, PVT, temporal-order, RSVP
epics.rationale: Only 5 paradigms ship; the named competitors all offer the inhibition (Go/NoGo, SART), spatial-conflict (Simon), attention-cueing (Posner), search/memory (visual search, Sternberg), vigilance (PVT) and rapid-serial (RSVP, temporal-order) families. Closes the 'paradigm breadth' partial row and makes the suite a credible cognitive-battery platform, reusing the existing engine/preset/compiler pattern.
data_model_changes: Extend ReactionTaskType (reaction-schema.ts:3-10) with 'go-nogo'|'sart'|'simon'|'posner'|'visual-search'|'sternberg'|'pvt'|'temporal-order'|'rsvp'
Add per-paradigm *TaskConfig interfaces to reaction-schema.ts and slots in ReactionTaskConfig (reaction-schema.ts:127)

Extend DerivedReactionMetrics (reaction-scoring.ts:16) with commissionErrorRate, omissionErrorRate, ssrtMs, simonEffectMs, posnerCueingEffectMs, searchSlopeMsPerItem, sternbergSlopeMsPerItem, pvtLapseCount+meanReciprocalRT, temporalOrderJND
step_by_step: Create seeded preset generators mirroring stroop.ts/flanker.ts each returning ReactionTrialConfig[]: goNoGo.ts, sart.ts, simon.ts, posner.ts, visualSearch.ts (click/touch + targetRegion reusing E-REACT-1), sternberg.ts, pvt.ts (variable 2-10s ISI), temporalOrder.ts, rsvp.ts
For cue/probe/multi-stimulus paradigms use ScheduledPhase (types.ts:74-89) including marksStimulusOnset for the true target onset (Posner target, temporal-order second stimulus)
Register presets in presets/index.ts and add builder functions (buildGoNoGoPlan, etc.) in reaction-compiler.ts following buildStroopPlan (reaction-compiler.ts:257)

Extend computeDerivedReactionMetrics (reaction-scoring.ts:81): SSRT via the integration method for Go/NoGo/SART, Simon/Posner effects as condition RT differences, search/Sternberg slopes via least-squares over set size, PVT lapse count + mean 1/RT
Add per-paradigm designer panels reusing the shared sub-editors (remediation P6-T5) with SOTA defaults
Add starter templates in starter-templates.ts for each paradigm
Add reaction-normalize.ts validation for each new *TaskConfig
Write preset unit tests (trial counts, target ratios, condition labels) and a scoring test asserting SSRT/Simon/search-slope on synthetic trials
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Preset unit tests: Go/NoGo go:nogo ratio honored; PVT ISIs within 2-10s; visual-search set sizes present; temporal-order SOA respected via phases
Scoring tests: SSRT on a synthetic stop set; Simon effect = incongruent-congruent; search slope ~ known ms/item
Live QA: author + run a Go/NoGo block in fillout; confirm commission/omission rates and SSRT in value.derived and persisted
Demo: a Posner cueing task with a 100ms cue phase (marksStimulusOnset on target) producing a valid-vs-invalid cueing effect

epics.title: Frame-accurate stimulus offset and frame-count duration specification
epics.rationale: Onset is frame-exact but offset is a window.setTimeout (ReactionEngine.ts:369) that is not vsync-aligned, so brief-exposure, masking, RSVP and temporal-order durations drift up to a frame. SOTA (PsychoPy-online, jsPsych) let researchers specify durations in frames and remove stimuli on a vsync boundary. Closes the 'frame-accurate offset / frame-count duration' partial row and is a prerequisite for trustworthy RSVP/temporal-order timing added in E-REACT-2.
data_model_changes: Add ReactionTrialConfig fields (types.ts:91): stimulusDurationFrames?:number, preStimulusDelayFrames?:number
Add ScheduledPhase.durationFrames?:number alongside durationMs (types.ts:74)
Add ReactionTrialResult.offsetMethod:'raf'|'timeout' + actualDurationFrames provenance fields (types.ts:215)
step_by_step: Add a WebGLRenderer presented-frame counter/accessor so the engine can schedule removal N presented frames after onset rather than by setTimeout

In ReactionEngine.runStimulus (ReactionEngine.ts:548), when stimulusDurationFrames is set, register a frame-callback that decrements a post-onset counter and calls stimulus.removeStimulus() on the exact presenting frame, stamping stimulusOffsetTime from sample.now and offsetMethod='raf'
Keep the setTimeout path as fallback when only stimulusDurationMs is given; derive a frame budget from the calibrated meanFrameInterval when possible and record offsetMethod accordingly
Allow ScheduledPhase durationFrames driving phase transitions off the presented-frame counter instead of wait(ms)
Expose actualDurationFrames (onset frame index -> offset frame index) in provenance for per-trial exposure verification
Extend the designer duration inputs to accept ms or frames with a live conversion hint using the qualified refresh rate
Harness test asserting a 3-frame stimulusDurationFrames removes the stimulus on exactly the 3rd presented frame after onset
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Harness test: stimulusDurationFrames=3 -> offset stamped on the 3rd presented frame (deterministic clock)
Live QA: RSVP at 100ms/frame on 60Hz shows exactly-6-frame items in provenance actualDurationFrames
Demo: a masking trial with a frame-counted mask phase (marksStimulusOnset), offsetMethod='raf' recorded in timing_provenance

epics.title: Trial-level feedback and criterion-based practice
epics.rationale: The feedback config is normalized (reaction-normalize.ts:170) but NEVER rendered - no correct/incorrect or RT feedback exists in the engine (grep-confirmed). Practice blocks run but give no feedback and cannot gate on an accuracy criterion. Every named competitor shows per-trial feedback and criterion-to-advance practice; closes the 'practice + feedback' partial row and is standard for training participants before test blocks.
data_model_changes: Add ReactionTrialConfig.feedback?:{ show:boolean; mode:'accuracy'|'rt'|'both'; durationMs:number; correctText?; incorrectText?; tooSlowText? } (types.ts:91)
Add ReactionStudyBlock.practiceCriterion?:{ minAccuracy:number; maxAttempts:number } (reaction-schema.ts:46)
step_by_step: Add a runFeedback phase in ReactionEngine.runTrial after the response window (ReactionEngine.ts:392): when config.feedback.show, render a text renderable ('Correct'/'Incorrect'/'Too slow' or the measured RT) for feedback.durationMs, reusing createTextCanvas + the fixation-style renderable path
Compute the verdict from evaluateCorrectness + timeout, honoring E-REACT-1 spatial correctness
Thread feedback config through reaction-compiler.ts trial builders from the normalized study config + per-paradigm defaults

Implement criterion-based practice in ReactionTimeRuntime.run (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:130): after a practice block with practiceCriterion, compute accuracy; if below minAccuracy and attempts remain, re-run the (re-seeded) practice block before the test; surface attempts used in metadata
Add a Feedback section and a Practice-criterion section to ReactionTimeDesigner.svelte reusing shared sub-editors
Validate new fields in reaction-normalize.ts
Unit-test the criterion loop (mock engine returning below- then above-criterion accuracy -> exactly two practice passes then test)
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit test: below-criterion practice re-runs up to maxAttempts then advances
Live QA: author a Flanker task with per-trial feedback + practice criterion 80%; run in fillout, see feedback between trials and a repeated practice block when accuracy is low
Demo: RT-mode feedback showing the measured reaction time after each trial; confirm feedback trials do NOT contaminate scored test averages

epics.title: Full per-trial long-format data capture, provenance columns, and tidy export
epics.rationale: The engine computes rich per-trial data (signed rawRtMs, anticipatory/falseStartCount, displayLatencyMs, outputLatencyMs, videoFrames, phaseTimeline, stimulusOffsetTime - types.ts:215-259) but the runtime->TrialResponse mapping (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:146-170, ReactionExperimentRuntime.ts:125-149) DROPS all of it, persisting only aggregate RT/accuracy + a rolled-up provenance blob. SOTA analysis needs one row per trial. Closes the 'full per-trial export' partial row and unlocks defensible reanalysis (anticipation exclusion, latency auditing).
data_model_changes: Extend TrialResponse (ReactionTimeRuntime.ts:37 and the mirror in ReactionExperimentRuntime.ts:16) with rawRtMs, anticipatory, falseStartCount, displayLatencyMs, outputLatencyMs, stimulusOffsetTime, offsetMethod, responseDevice, holdDurationMs, phaseTimeline(compacted), videoFrameCount
No server migration needed: per-trial data lives in responses.value JSON and responses.timing_provenance JSONB (migration 00024); enrich the aggregate blob with min/max/median latency
Define a canonical tidy-row schema (one object per trial) in a shared type consumed by the export service
step_by_step: Stop dropping fields: in both runtimes' mapping loops copy the full ReactionTrialResult provenance/latency/false-start/offset fields into TrialResponse

Add buildTrialRow() producing the canonical tidy row (session id, block, condition, trialNumber, isPractice, stimulus descriptor, RT, rawRtMs, isCorrect, timeout, anticipatory, onsetMethod, responseMethod, responseDevice, displayLatencyMs, outputLatencyMs, offsetMethod, fps/dropped/jitter, invalid+reason)
Wire the export service (analytics/export) to flatten value.responses[] into long-format CSV/XLSX via buildTrialRow (per trial, not per session)
Keep metadata.timingProvenance for compatibility but enrich with per-trial latency stats
Add a per-trial data preview in the analytics UI before export
Unit-test buildTrialRow completeness and the CSV flattener (N trials -> N rows, stable column order)
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit test: a synthetic ReactionTrialResult with a false start + video frames + audio output latency round-trips into a tidy row with all columns populated
Live QA: run a reaction block, open analytics, export long-format CSV, confirm one row per trial with rawRtMs/anticipatory/displayLatencyMs columns
Demo: an audio trial's exported row shows outputLatencyMs and onsetMethod='audioContext'; a pre-onset keypress trial shows anticipatory=true and is flagged for exclusion

epics.title: Participant-level counterbalancing and stimulus-set assignment
epics.rationale: Randomization is seeded off the questionnaire/question id (reaction-compiler.ts:28-31), so it is identical for every participant; IAT block order is fixed. SOTA platforms assign each participant a counterbalanced condition (block order, key mapping, stimulus subset) via Latin square / round-robin. Closes the 'counterbalancing' partial row and removes an order-confound that currently undermines IAT/Simon/Posner validity.
data_model_changes: Add CounterbalanceScheme to reaction-schema.ts: { factor:'block-order'|'key-mapping'|'stimulus-subset'; levels:string[]; method:'latin-square'|'round-robin'|'random' }[]
Add ReactionStudyConfig.counterbalance?: CounterbalanceScheme[] (reaction-schema.ts:137)
Persist the assigned counterbalance cell into session metadata / result value so it exports as a column and is reproducible
Seed root becomes (questionnaireSeed + sessionId) for deterministic-per-session assignment
step_by_step: Add a counterbalance assignment step that, given the session id + a stable per-questionnaire counter (or session-id hash), assigns each factor a level via Latin-square/round-robin
For block-order, reorder config.blocks (and the IAT block sequence in iat.ts) per the assigned permutation before compileReactionPlan
For key-mapping, swap the response key->condition mapping per assigned level (e.g. IAT combined vs reversed key sides)
For stimulus-subset, select the assigned subset of trial templates / stimulus items
Thread sessionId into compileReactionPlan seedRoot (reaction-compiler.ts:28) so within-block shuffles vary per participant while staying reproducible from the persisted seed+cell
Record the assigned cell in the runtime result metadata and the tidy trial rows from E-REACT-5
Designer: add a Counterbalancing panel to declare factors/levels/method with a level-table preview
Unit-test: N sessions round-robin evenly across levels; same sessionId always yields the same cell; block order matches the Latin square for a given cell
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test
Unit test: even distribution across levels over many sessions; determinism per sessionId; Latin-square coverage
Live QA: run the same IAT questionnaire as two different sessions, confirm the compatible/reversed block order differs and is recorded
Demo: exported trial rows carry a counterbalance-cell column; re-running with the persisted seed+cell reproduces the exact block/trial order

epics.title: Wire the CAT/IRT adaptive engine into the fillout runtime as an adaptive item-bank block
epics.rationale: Closes the 'Adaptive testing' MISSING gap — the single largest SOTA differentiator. CATSession (analytics/CATEngine.ts) is a complete, tested 3PL maximum-information selector but has zero fillout integration. 2026 psychometric platforms (Concerto, TAO, Qualtrics adaptive) treat CAT as table stakes for shortening scales while holding measurement precision. This makes the existing dead engine a live capability.
data_model_changes: Add AdaptiveBlockConfig to questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts: { itemBankId, items: CATItem[] (a,b,c calibration per question id), maxItems, seThreshold, thetaReportVariable, exposureControl?: 'none'|'randomesque' }
Extend Block.type union (questionnaire.ts:92) with 'adaptive' and add Block.adaptive?: AdaptiveBlockConfig
Add session variables _theta, _thetaSE, _adaptiveItemsAdministered registered in initializeVariables (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:336)
Persist the CAT trajectory (administered item ids + running theta/SE) into session.metadata.custom so P1-T1's variable-persistence path carries it
step_by_step: Add AdaptiveBlockConfig + CATItem re-export to packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts and extend Block.type with 'adaptive'

Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/AdaptiveController.ts wrapping CATSession: constructor(bankItems, options), nextQuestionId(), submit(questionId, correct), getEstimate(), isComplete() — maps question responses to boolean correctness via the same evaluateCustomCorrectness path used at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1084
In QuestionnaireRuntime.getPageItems (:606), detect a block with type==='adaptive' and, instead of static ordering, hand control to an adaptive presentation loop

Add presentAdaptiveBlock(block) to QuestionnaireRuntime that: (a) builds a CATSession from block.adaptive.items, (b) loops nextItem()→present the mapped Question via the existing presentQuestion path, (c) on each handleCollectedResponse feeds correctness back via AdaptiveController.submit, (d) writes _theta/_thetaSE to variableEngine after each item, (e) exits when isComplete()
Score correctness for adaptive items using per-item scoreKey (add AdaptiveItemScoring to config) so multiple-choice/scale items resolve to the boolean CATSession expects
Register _theta, _thetaSE, thetaReportVariable in initializeVariables so downstream feedback/branching can read the ability estimate
Snapshot administered item ids + final theta/SE into session.metadata.custom.adaptive at complete() (:1183) so P1-T1 persists it offline-first
Add a FlowControlManager/PropertiesPanel affordance to mark a block adaptive and edit per-item a/b/c calibration (reuse the block sub-editor extracted in P6-T5)
Expose _thetaSE as a flow condition variable so a researcher can branch on measurement precision
Add exposure control (randomesque top-k pick) as an option in AdaptiveController.nextQuestionId to prevent item over-exposure
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (new AdaptiveController.test.ts asserts item selection converges theta on a simulated respondent)
Unit: feed a 40-item calibrated bank + a fixed-theta simulated responder, assert the block stops at SE<=threshold in <maxItems and _theta lands within 0.3 of truth
Live QA (agent-browser): author a questionnaire with one adaptive block, fill it out, confirm items adapt (harder items after correct answers) and the completion screen shows the estimated theta
Demo: a 60-item bank administered adaptively terminates in ~12 items at SE 0.3, with the theta trajectory visible in the persisted session.metadata.custom.adaptive

epics.title: Longitudinal / EMA study series: multi-session scheduling, reminders, and resume-link enrollment
epics.rationale: Closes the ESM/EMA + longitudinal MISSING gap — nothing exists today. Ecological momentary assessment and multi-wave designs are core to 2026 behavioral research (RescueTime/PIEL/MetricWire class). Without a study-series + scheduler + reminder loop, the platform cannot run diary or repeated-measures studies at all.
data_model_changes: New migration 00027_study_series.sql: study_series (id, questionnaire_id, schedule_kind ENUM fixed|random-interval|event, wave_defs JSONB, timezone), series_enrollment (id, series_id, participant_ref, contact_channel, status, next_prompt_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resume_token UUID UNIQUE), series_prompt (id, enrollment_id, wave_index, scheduled_at, delivered_at, opened_at, session_id NULL) with RLS dual-path policies mirroring 00021 (enrollment-token GUC)
Add resume_token to sessions to bind a materialized session back to its prompt
New scheduler tick table series_prompt is the source of truth; index on next_prompt_at WHERE status='active'
step_by_step: Author migration 00027_study_series.sql with the three tables + RLS policies keyed on an app.enrollment_token GUC (sibling of fillout_rls_context.rs)
Add apps/server/src/api/series.rs: enroll (POST), list_enrollments, prompt-window materialization, and GET /api/series/prompt/{resume_token} returning the pinned questionnaire snapshot + wave index

Add a scheduler: a tokio interval task in main.rs (or a periodic Redis-locked worker) that scans series_prompt WHERE scheduled_at<=NOW() AND delivered_at IS NULL, sends the reminder via the existing SMTP/storage email path, sets delivered_at, and computes the next wave's next_prompt_at
Implement schedule kinds in a new module series/schedule.rs: fixed offsets, random-interval (seeded so it is reproducible/auditable), and event-triggered (enqueued by a prior session's completion)

Frontend: extend the fillout entry (+page.svelte) to accept ?token=<resume_token>, resolve the prompt, and pin the correct wave's version snapshot (reuse the per-session version pinning from ADR 0023)
Add a researcher Series designer surface (settings.experimentalDesign sibling) to define waves, cadence, reminder copy, and channel
Expose _waveIndex and _seriesElapsedDays as flow variables so branching can differ across waves (e.g. baseline vs follow-up items)
On session complete(), if the session carries a resume_token, POST completion back so series.rs advances the enrollment and schedules the next prompt
Add opt-out/unsubscribe handling on the reminder channel and a status transition to 'withdrawn'
Add participant-facing 'come back later' completion state that shows the next prompt time for the current wave
verification: cargo check/build/test with a new tests/series_scheduling.rs asserting wave materialization, next_prompt_at computation, and RLS token isolation
Integration: enroll a participant, advance the clock past scheduled_at, assert the scheduler marks delivered and schedules wave 2
Live QA: complete wave 1 via a resume-token link, confirm wave 2 prompt appears with the correct pinned version and _waveIndex=1 available to branching
Demo: a 3-wave daily-diary study enrolls a participant, fires 3 reminders on schedule, and each resume link opens the correct wave with distinct content

epics.title: True save-and-continue: resumable sessions with position and variable-state restore
epics.rationale: Closes the save-and-continue MISSING gap. Today start() always begins at page 0 (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:263) with no restore — a participant who leaves loses their place, unacceptable for long surveys and a prerequisite for E-FLOW-2's multi-wave resume. Offline persistence already stores responses; this connects that data back into a resumable runtime cursor.
data_model_changes: Add ResumeState to QuestionnaireSession: { currentPageIndex, currentItemIndex, loopIterationState, variableSnapshot, presentedItemIds } serialized to the filloutSessions Dexie table and to sessions.state_snapshot on the server
Persist ResumeState on every navigateToPage/showCurrentItem boundary via OfflineResponsePersistence (offline-first, per P1-T1's write path)
step_by_step: Add RuntimeConfig.resumeFrom?: ResumeState to QuestionnaireRuntime and, in start() (:250), if resumeFrom is present, restore variableEngine values (setVariable for each snapshot entry), loopIterations Map, and navigateToPage(resumeFrom.currentPageIndex) with currentItemIndex seeded
Add captureResumeState(): ResumeState to QuestionnaireRuntime returning the cursor + variableEngine.getAllVariables() + loop map

Call captureResumeState after each response commit (handleCollectedResponse :1046, handleQuestionRuntimeResult :984) and persist via OfflineResponsePersistence.saveResumeState (new method) — reuse the offline-first path P1-T1 establishes
In FilloutRuntime, on construction check for an existing local ResumeState for the sessionId and pass it as resumeFrom
Guard restore against definition-version drift: if the pinned snapshot version differs, discard resume and restart (log to session.metadata)
Skip already-answered items on restore so a resumed page does not re-present captured responses (respect presentedItemIds)
Add a 'continue where you left off' vs 'start over' choice on the WelcomeScreen when a resumable session is detected
Server: extend sessions.rs update to accept and return state_snapshot so cross-device resume works when online
Add a resume-link generator (short token) reused by E-FLOW-2 for cross-device continue-later
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test — new resume test drives a runtime to page 3, captures state, reconstructs a fresh runtime with resumeFrom, asserts it presents the page-3 item and prior variables are intact
Live QA: fill 5 of 10 pages, reload the tab, confirm the WelcomeScreen offers continue and lands on page 6 with prior answers preserved
Demo: an offline participant completes half a survey, closes the browser, reopens offline, and resumes exactly where they stopped with variables restored

epics.title: Real repeat/loop blocks with per-iteration variable namespacing, response capture, and piping
epics.rationale: Closes the looping MISSING gap. Loop blocks are currently silently discarded — randomizeBlock expands loop.values into duplicated question ids that randomizePage's `seen` Set collapses back to one presentation (BlockRandomizer.ts:38-52 vs :81-97), and updateQuestionVariables keys only on question.id so iterations would overwrite. Roster/list looping (repeat a battery per stimulus, per named person, per trial) is fundamental to psych batteries and EMA item sets.
data_model_changes: Extend LoopConfig (questionnaire.ts:371) with loopVariableName + optional maxIterations, and formalize a per-iteration key scheme
Response.iterationIndex?: number and Response.loopValue?: unknown added to response.ts so each looped answer is distinct
Variable ids become iteration-scoped: `${questionId}__${iterationIndex}_value` alongside a __loopValue variable exposed to interpolation
step_by_step: Introduce a LoopExpansion structure: instead of duplicate raw ids, randomizeBlock returns ordered { questionId, iterationIndex, loopValue } tuples

Change QuestionnaireRuntime.getPageItems (:606) and currentPageItems to carry PresentedItem = { question, iterationIndex, loopValue } rather than bare Question, removing the dedup collapse in randomizePage for loop blocks
Set a per-iteration loop variable (loopVariableName -> loopValue) in variableEngine before presenting each iteration so {{loopValue}} piping works in prompts/options
Namespace updateQuestionVariables (:1073) by iterationIndex so each loop pass stores a distinct _value/_rt/_correct
Record Response.iterationIndex + loopValue in handleCollectedResponse (:1011) and handleQuestionRuntimeResult (:952)
Reconcile flatline/attention grouping (runFlatlineDetection :1217) to treat per-iteration responses correctly
Extend CarryForward to allow piping the current loop item into options (roster carry)
Add designer UI to define a loop source (static list, prior multi-select answer, or a variable array) and preview iteration count
Bound loops with maxIterations and surface a runtime guard against runaway loops
Expose _iterationIndex and _iterationCount as flow variables so branching can act on loop position
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test — LoopExpansion.test.ts asserts a 3-value loop produces 3 distinct presentations with 3 distinct stored responses (not 1)
Unit: assert {{loopValue}} interpolates to each iteration's value and per-iteration _value variables are all retained at complete()
Live QA: author a block that loops a 3-question battery over ['Mother','Father','Sibling'], fill it, confirm the battery renders 3 times with the piped name and 9 stored responses
Demo: a roster-driven loop repeats a satisfaction battery per named relationship and the exported dataset shows one row per (question, iteration)

epics.title: Unified timer subsystem: per-page time limits, whole-survey deadline, and per-question response deadlines with configurable timeout actions
epics.rationale: Closes the timers PARTIAL gap for the form path. PageSettings.timeLimit and TimingConfig.maxTime are defined but enforced nowhere; the only page timer is a hardcoded 1000ms onTimer interval (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:586). Speeded self-report, timed cognitive form tasks, and whole-session caps are standard 2026 requirements that the reaction path alone cannot serve.
data_model_changes: Add TimeoutAction to TimingConfig: { deadlineMs?, onTimeout: 'auto-submit'|'skip'|'terminate'|'warn', warnAtMs? } for per-question deadlines
Add QuestionnaireSettings.wholeSurveyTimeLimitMs + onSurveyTimeout action
Enforce PageSettings.timeLimit (questionnaire.ts:244) instead of leaving it inert
Record Response.timedOut and page-level timeout events into session.metadata for QualityReport
step_by_step: Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/TimerController.ts owning three timer scopes (survey, page, question) with a single monotonic performance.now() clock and pause/resume awareness
In start(), arm the whole-survey timer from settings.wholeSurveyTimeLimitMs; on expiry run the configured action (complete() with a timeout flag)
In navigateToPage (:512), arm the page timer from PageSettings.timeLimit; on expiry auto-advance or terminate per config, and replace the hardcoded 1000ms onTimer interval with a configurable cadence

In presentQuestion (:737)/presentFormQuestion, arm a per-question deadline from TimingConfig.deadlineMs; drive a visible countdown through FormQuestionHost and on expiry call handleTimeout (:1050) with onTimeout action semantics (auto-submit current value, skip, terminate, or warn)
Surface remaining time to the overlay via a TimerDisplay slot in FormQuestionHost so participants see countdowns (respect prefers-reduced-motion)
Fold timeout occurrences into QualityReport (new timeout dimension) so researchers can flag rushed/abandoned items
Ensure pause()/resume() (:266/:274) freeze all timer scopes so backgrounding does not corrupt deadlines
Persist the survey-timer remaining budget into ResumeState (E-FLOW-3) so resume respects the original cap
Add designer controls for all three timer scopes and their timeout actions
Guard against timers firing after unmount via cancelCurrentExecution (:1247)
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test — TimerController.test.ts with fake timers asserts page/question/survey expiry fires the configured action exactly once and pause/resume preserves remaining budget
Live QA: author a page with a 10s limit and a question with a 5s auto-submit deadline; confirm the countdown renders, auto-submit fires, and Response.timedOut is set
Demo: a whole-survey 5-minute cap terminates with the configured message, and a speeded item auto-submits the partial answer at its deadline

epics.title: Server-atomic between-subjects assignment: wire participantNumber, least-full arm allocation, persisted assignment, working counterbalancing
epics.rationale: Closes the multi-arm PARTIAL gap and fixes a live correctness bug beyond the remediation scope: participantNumber is never passed to the runtime (+page.svelte:496-502), so ConditionAssigner and getBlockOrder always compute for participant 0 (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:374) — every participant receives Latin-square row 0, defeating counterbalancing. Client-side allocation over a fetched count snapshot also races under concurrent starts.
data_model_changes: Add a server assignment endpoint backed by an atomic allocation: new migration 00028_arm_assignment.sql with arm_counts (questionnaire_id, condition_name, assigned_count) and a claim function using UPDATE ... RETURNING to atomically pick the least-full arm
sessions.assigned_condition + assigned_condition_index + participant_number columns persisted at create time
Return participantNumber (a monotonic per-questionnaire counter) from session create so the client seeds counterbalancing
step_by_step: Add POST /api/questionnaires/{id}/assign-arm in sessions.rs that atomically claims the least-full arm (or sequential/random per strategy) inside the request tx and increments arm_counts — replacing the read-only conditionCounts snapshot
On anonymous session create, allocate participant_number via a per-questionnaire sequence and return it
In +page.svelte (:496), pass participantNumber (from create response) and the server-assigned condition into FilloutRuntime/QuestionnaireRuntime config
In initializeExperimentalDesign (:370), prefer a server-provided assignment when present (authoritative), falling back to local ConditionAssigner only offline
Persist assignedCondition/conditionIndex/participant_number through the P1-T1 variable/metadata persistence path so the arm survives to the dataset
Verify getBlockOrder now receives the real participantNumber so Latin-square rows actually rotate across participants
Add an offline-created-session reconciliation: when a locally-assigned session later syncs, confirm/rebalance the arm server-side (idempotent on client_id)
Expose _condition/_conditionIndex (already registered :386-402) as first-class flow variables documented for branching
Add designer readout of live per-arm counts on the analytics page (dovetails with P8-T4)
Add a test proving concurrent claims never over-fill an arm
verification: cargo test arm_assignment — spawn N concurrent claims, assert arm counts differ by <=1 (balanced) and no arm exceeds its cap
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check — participantNumber threaded through FilloutRuntime config types
Live QA: start the same questionnaire in 4 sessions, confirm conditions alternate across arms and block order (counterbalance) differs by participant
Demo: a 2-arm balanced design with 20 concurrent starts lands 10/10 with distinct counterbalanced block orders, all persisted to the dataset

epics.title: Interlocking quota cells + structured pre-survey screener/eligibility gating
epics.rationale: Goes beyond P1-T4 (which fixes per-cell counting) to the SOTA capability: interlocking cross-quotas (e.g. age x gender cells) with least-full assignment and a real screener block that screen-outs ineligible respondents with structured reasons/redirects. Recruitment-grade platforms (Prolific, Qualtrics) require in-survey demographic re-check, not just urlParams.
data_model_changes: Extend QuotaGroup (questionnaire.ts:196) with logic:'cross' cell enumeration and a serialized cell key (variable tuple)
Add server per-cell counters table (migration 00029_quota_cells.sql) with an atomic claim on completion keyed on the cell tuple
Add a ScreenerBlock config: eligibility rules (formula), screenOutReason, screenOutRedirectUrl, and terminate action distinct from over-quota
step_by_step: Add server GET /api/questionnaires/{id}/quota-cells returning true per-cell current/target (building on P1-T4's condition-aware counting) and a POST claim on completion

Rewrite QuotaService.checkQuotas to evaluate the interlocking cell tuple from live in-survey variables (not just the urlParams Map at +page.svelte:419-424), selecting the participant's cell and blocking only when that cell is full
Re-run quota checks after in-survey demographic questions by invoking checkQuotas from a page onPageEnter/onResponse hook, not only at entry
Add a ScreenerController that evaluates eligibility formulas (via variableEngine) at defined screener pages and screen-outs with a structured reason recorded to session.metadata
Route screen-out and over-quota to distinct completion states (ineligible vs quota-full) with their own redirect handling
Make quota claiming atomic at completion so concurrent completions cannot overfill a cell (UPDATE...RETURNING)
Enforce quotas on the offline/sync path too (stop failing open — dovetails with P1-T4) by re-checking at sync time
Add designer UI for interlocking cells (variable tuple builder) and screener rules
Expose _eligible and _quotaCell as flow variables for downstream branching
Add tests for cell selection and atomic claim races
verification: cargo test quota_cells — concurrent completions across cells never exceed per-cell targets
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test — QuotaService cell-selection unit tests over in-survey variables

Live QA: define age x gender interlocking cells, fill out as a male 25-34, confirm blocking only when THAT cell is full, and a screener page screen-outs an ineligible respondent with the right message
Demo: a 2x2 interlocking quota fills each cell independently and an eligibility screener terminates out-of-scope participants with a distinct redirect

epics.title: Position-scoped flow rules + visual branch-graph authoring beyond page-target resolution
epics.rationale: Goes beyond P1-T3 (which makes question-id targets resolve to their page). Today findMatchingFlowRule (QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:1171) scans ALL questionnaire.flow globally with no position scoping, so the first matching rule wins for every page transition — you cannot express 'from page 3, if X go to 7; from page 5, if X go to 9'. SOTA survey logic needs per-source-scoped rules and a validated branch graph (unreachable-page and infinite-loop detection).
data_model_changes: Add FlowControl.source?: string (page/question id the rule fires from) and FlowControl.priority?: number to questionnaire.ts:101 so rules are scoped and deterministically ordered
Add branch-graph validation metadata (computed, not stored): reachability + cycle report
step_by_step: Extend FlowControl with source + priority and update findMatchingFlowRule (:1171) to only consider rules whose source matches the current page/question (or global when source is unset), ordered by priority
Update handleFlowControl (:1128) to evaluate scoped rules at the correct boundary (page-exit and, for question-scoped rules, item-exit) rather than only at page-end
Add a FlowGraph builder (apps/web/src/lib/runtime/core/FlowGraph.ts) that computes reachable pages and detects unconditional cycles / unreachable pages for designer validation
Surface graph validation in FlowControlManager (warn on unreachable pages, dead branches, and loops without a bounded condition)
Add a visual branch-graph view in the designer (nodes=pages, edges=rules) so researchers can see and edit the flow topology
Keep P1-T3's question-target-to-page resolution and extend it to optionally scroll/anchor to the question within the page
Add a dry-run flow simulator (given a variable assignment, show the path) for authoring confidence
Version-bump semantics: adding/removing scoped rules is a structural change (major bump per CLAUDE.md table)
Expose the resolved path into session.metadata for auditing
Add tests for scoped-rule precedence and cycle detection
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test — FlowGraph.test.ts asserts scoped rules fire only from their source page and priority breaks ties; cycle/unreachable detection flagged
Live QA: author two rules with the same condition but different sources/targets, confirm each fires only from its own page
Demo: a screening flow branches differently from page 3 vs page 5 on the same variable, and the branch-graph view highlights an intentionally unreachable page as a warning

epics.title: Resurrect the psychometric scoring engine into the participant feedback path (subscale, reverse, T/z/stanine/percentile, missing-data)
epics.rationale: Closes the 'subscale scoring', 'reverse-scoring', 'percentiles/T-scores/stanines', and 'missing-data handling' gaps. Today analytics/ScoreInterpreter, ScoringPipeline and MissingDataHandler are dead code (imported only by analytics/index.ts); the participant path only produces range-band labels and a cohort z-score. This makes the full normative/subscale scoring available OFFLINE at fillout by defining a first-class ScoringConfig on the questionnaire and computing it deterministically at complete().
data_model_changes: packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts: add ScoringConfig to QuestionnaireSettings (settings.scoring?): { scales: ScaleScoringDef[] } where ScaleScoringDef = { id, name, itemIds: string[], reverseScoredItemIds?: string[], itemMin: number, itemMax: number, aggregation: 'sum'|'mean', missingPolicy: 'listwise'|'mean-impute'|'prorate'|'available', norm?: { mean: number; sd: number; source?: string } }
packages/questionnaire-core/src/questionnaire.ts: add optional Variable.metadata.scoreOf?: string linking a computed variable to a ScaleScoringDef id (for round-trip)
no DB migration: scoring config rides in the existing questionnaire_definitions JSON; computed scale scores persist through the P1-T1 session.variables pipeline
step_by_step: In questionnaire-core add the ScoringConfig / ScaleScoringDef types next to QuestionnaireSettings (:205) and export them from the package index.
