Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/ScaleScorer.ts: pure function scoreScales(responses: Record<string,number|null>, config: ScoringConfig): ScaleScoreResult[] that (a) applies reverse scoring per reverseScoredItemIds via (itemMin+itemMax - value), (b) applies missingPolicy using MissingDataHandler for listwise/mean-impute and a prorate branch (sum * itemsExpected/itemsAnswered), (c) aggregates sum|mean, (d) when norm present, calls analytics/ScoreInterpreter.generateNormativeComparison for z/T/stanine/percentile/CI.
Reuse (do not re-implement) analytics/ScoreInterpreter for the normative math and the canonical normalCDF from P5-T2; delete ScaleScorer's own copies.

Wire ScaleScorer into QuestionnaireRuntime: in complete() (:1201) after the VariableEngine snapshot, read settings.scoring, gather item responses from responseCollector, call scoreScales, and push each ScaleScoreResult into this.session.variables as a namespaced value (e.g. score.<scaleId> = { value, z, tScore, stanine, percentile, band }).
Ensure these computed scores flow through the P1-T1 OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable path so they persist offline and sync.
Extend statistical-feedback engine.ts resolveVariableValue to resolve score.<scaleId>.<field> so a feedback widget can bind a metric to a percentile/T-score, not just a raw mean.
Update StatisticalFeedbackDesigner to add a 'Scale scores' picker sourced from settings.scoring scales (alongside the existing raw-variable picker).
Add engine.test.ts + ScaleScorer.test.ts cases: reverse scoring, prorate vs listwise on a missing item, sum vs mean, and norm → T=50 at the mean.

Add a designer sub-panel (new ScaleScoringEditor.svelte) to define scales (item multiselect, reverse toggles, aggregation, missing policy, optional norm mean/SD) writing to settings.scoring; mount it in the PropertiesPanel questionnaire-settings tab.
Regenerate types and run pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (ScaleScorer.test.ts green: reverse, prorate, listwise, norm→T/z/percentile)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check

Live QA: author a 5-item scale with 2 reverse items + norm(mean,SD), fill it out offline (devtools offline), reach the feedback item, confirm the score, T-score, percentile and band render and match a hand computation; reload to confirm the score.<scaleId> variable persisted in IndexedDB
Demo: a PHQ-9-style questionnaire whose end feedback shows subscale sum, reverse-scored items handled, percentile vs a bundled norm, all computed with the network tab offline

epics.title: Norm/baseline mechanisms: bundled norm-table library + self-baseline/pre-post comparison source
epics.rationale: Directly closes 'bundled norm tables' (missing) and 'self-baseline/pre-post' (missing) and elevates 'norm comparison' beyond the cohort endpoint. A participant should get a percentile/T-score with NO live cohort (using a shipped norm) and be able to see their change across repeated administrations — both fully offline. This is the norm/baseline half of the pillar the cohort work (P1-T5) does not touch.
data_model_changes: New static norm library file (no DB): NormTable = { id, instrument, label, subgroup?, mean, sd, n?, source, citation }
engine.ts StatisticalSourceMode: add 'norm-table' and 'self-baseline'
StatisticalFeedbackDataSourceConfig: add normTableId?: string and baselineVariable?: string (the earlier-administration value to compare against)
ScaleScoringDef.norm (from E1) gains optional normTableId to reference the library instead of inline mean/sd
step_by_step: Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/normTables.ts exporting a typed NormTable[] seeded with a handful of open/public norms (and a clear 'researcher-supplied' custom entry path); include a lookup getNormTable(id).

Add a norm-table source mode to engine.resolveStatisticalFeedbackSeries: when sourceMode==='norm-table', compute z/T/percentile of the participant value against the referenced NormTable using analytics/ScoreInterpreter — no network call, fully offline.

Add a self-baseline mode: resolve baselineVariable from the variables map (e.g. a pre-test score piped via urlParams or a prior session variable), emit a two-point series [Baseline, Current] with a delta + reliable-change-index summary.
Extend the ChartSeriesContract summary with { normSource, tScore, percentile, deltaFromBaseline, rci } so charts/summary tiles can display them.

In StatisticalFeedbackDesigner add: a norm-table dropdown (populated from normTables.ts) shown for norm-table mode, and a baseline-variable picker for self-baseline mode; keep the cohort mode (P1-T5) as a third norm option.
Feed norm mean/sd into ReportGenerator.normativeStats (currently never populated, ReportGenerator.ts:44) so the PDF's Normative Comparison section actually renders.
Add a designer 'Custom norm' inline editor (mean/SD/label) for researchers whose instrument is not in the bundled library, stored on ScaleScoringDef.norm.
Tests: engine.test.ts norm-table percentile at the mean = 50th; self-baseline delta + RCI sign; report includes normative section when norm present.
Run check + test.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (norm-table + self-baseline series tests green)

Live QA offline: pick a bundled norm, confirm percentile/T render with the network tab throttled to offline; configure self-baseline with a piped pre-test value and confirm the Baseline→Current delta chart
Demo: an anxiety scale showing '78th percentile vs adult community norm' with no server, plus a pre/post retest showing a reliable-change arrow — both captured in the downloaded report

epics.title: Designable end-of-survey Report Page: multi-widget composition + wire CompletionScreen scores/variables
epics.rationale: Closes the 'end-of-survey summary PAGE (multi-widget, designable)' gap. Today feedback is a single per-item module and the CompletionScreen report is mounted inert (q/[code]/+page.svelte:700-707 passes no scoreConfigs/variables). The vision is a designable report surface that composes several feedback widgets (per-scale cards, a profile radar, a norm distribution, a table) with conditional sections and piped narrative — shown at the end and exportable.
data_model_changes: QuestionnaireSettings: add reportPage?: { enabled: boolean; title?: string; sections: ReportSection[] } where ReportSection = { id; type: 'scale-summary'|'chart'|'narrative'|'table'|'norm-distribution'; config: unknown; condition?: string (formula) }
No DB change: rides in questionnaire_definitions JSON and persists via existing settings path
step_by_step: Define ReportPageConfig / ReportSection types in questionnaire-core next to QuestionnaireSettings.

Create apps/web/src/lib/runtime/feedback/ReportPage.svelte: given the final variables + settings.reportPage + settings.scoring, render each ReportSection, evaluating section.condition through the SAME QuestionnaireRuntime.evaluateVisibility formula path (so conditional sections reuse existing logic) and interpolating narrative text via the P1-T6 unified interpolation.
Have QuestionnaireRuntime.complete() attach the computed variables snapshot to the completion payload the page already receives (onComplete session), so the page can pass them onward.

In q/[code]/+page.svelte, when settings.reportPage.enabled, render ReportPage before/above CompletionScreen and pass completedSession.variables; otherwise pass scoreConfigs + variables into the existing CompletionScreen props (fixing the inert mount at :700).
Reuse StatisticalFeedback.svelte as the 'chart' and 'scale-summary' section renderers (drive them from the computed score variables) rather than re-implementing charts.

Add a ReportPageDesigner.svelte in the questionnaire-settings tab: add/reorder/remove sections, pick section type, per-section condition formula (FormulaEditor), and a live preview using previewVariables (mirroring StatisticalFeedbackDesigner:567).
Honor P1-T7 require-Continue so the report page does not auto-dismiss.
Add @testing-library/svelte test: ReportPage renders only sections whose condition passes; narrative interpolates a score variable.
check + test.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (ReportPage conditional-section + interpolation tests green)
pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check

Live QA: build a report page with a scale-summary + norm-distribution + a conditional narrative ('if depression High show referral text'); complete the survey and confirm the multi-widget report renders, the conditional section appears only when triggered, and it does not auto-advance
Demo: end-of-survey report with three stacked widgets + a conditional clinical-referral message, all offline

epics.title: Complete the chart-type set: results table, trajectory/pre-post line, and true designable reference bands
epics.rationale: Closes 'chart type: table (missing)', strengthens 'line/trajectory' for pre-post, and turns the score bands into explicit chart reference bands. The vision lists table and trajectory as first-class; today FeedbackChart covers bar/line/radar/scatter/histogram/box and separate Bell/Gauge, but there is no data table and the line chart has no repeated-measures feed.
data_model_changes: engine.ts StatisticalFeedbackConfig.chartType union: add 'table' and 'trajectory'
metadata.ts options.chartTypes and the designer chartType <Select>: add Table and Trajectory entries
step_by_step: Create charts/FeedbackTable.svelte: render series.points (and score.<scaleId> fields: value/T/percentile/band) as an accessible <table> with a caption, using the P7-T5 pattern for participant-facing a11y; color band cells via resolveColor(ColorRule).

Add a 'trajectory' branch: a line chart over an ordered set of administrations (from E-FEEDBACK-2 self-baseline/repeated variables), plotting value with a shaded normal/reference band behind it using chart-utils.generateCurvePoints/ColorRule.
Extend FeedbackChart.svelte dispatch (:62) and StatisticalFeedback.svelte (:243) to route 'table' and 'trajectory' to the new renderers.
Register any new Chart.js pieces through the P5-T3 centralized chart-setup module (do not add a 4th Chart.register site).

Draw designable reference bands as chart annotations: pass colorRules (already derived at StatisticalFeedback.svelte:120) into FeedbackChart and render horizontal band regions behind bar/line/trajectory, not only in the separate range bar.
Give the table and every new canvas the P7-T5 role=img/aria-label + visually-hidden data list.
Tests: chart-utils.test.ts additions for table row-building and trajectory point ordering.
check + test.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test

Live QA: switch a feedback widget's chart type to Table and to Trajectory in the designer live preview; confirm the table lists per-scale value/percentile/band and the trajectory shows multiple administrations with a shaded reference band
Demo: a profile report using a radar for subscales + a table of exact scores + a trajectory of pre/mid/post

epics.title: Real downloadable per-participant PDF with embedded charts (replace print-dialog)
epics.rationale: Closes 'per-participant PDF (partial)'. ReportGenerator.printHTMLAsPDF (ReportGenerator.ts:266) opens the OS print dialog and its header comment misdescribes it as a dependency-free PDF byte-stream (F063). A research participant report should be a true downloadable file with the actual rendered charts, not a browser-dependent print flow. P1-T7 only makes the button label honest; this delivers the real artifact.
data_model_changes: none (report is generated client-side from the computed variables + ReportConfig)
step_by_step: Replace printHTMLAsPDF with a real generator: render the ReportPage/report HTML into an offscreen node, rasterize charts via the existing Chart.js canvases' toDataURL, and assemble a PDF Blob; lazy-import a small PDF lib (dynamic import to keep it off the fillout critical path, consistent with P8-T2's lazy-mathjs posture) or a minimal jsPDF-style builder.
Trigger a genuine file download (URL.createObjectURL + <a download>), not window.print(); keep a window.print() fallback only when Blob/download is unavailable.
Embed the actual score charts (bar/radar/trajectory/distribution) as images plus the score/band/norm sections and E-FEEDBACK-2 normative table; drive from the same computed score.<scaleId> variables.
Fix the misleading module header comment to describe the real implementation.

Add a smoke test (ReportGenerator.test.ts — currently none) that asserts generateReport produces a non-empty application/pdf Blob for a sample config, and that no DOM print is invoked in the Blob path.
Ensure sanitization: the report HTML must pass through the P2-T1 centralized DOMPurify sink before rasterization (researcher-authored titles/descriptions).
check + test.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (ReportGenerator smoke test green: returns a PDF Blob, no print() in blob path)
Live QA: click Download Report on a completed session; confirm a .pdf file lands in Downloads (no print dialog) and contains the rendered charts + normative table
Demo: a downloaded multi-page participant PDF with an embedded radar profile and a norm percentile table

epics.title: Conditional feedback builder: per-band messages with variable piping
epics.rationale: Elevates 'conditional feedback (partial)' and 'interpretive text piping (partial)' to the full capability. evaluateVisibility already supports item/page conditions, but there is no designer surface for band-specific interpretive messages that pipe the participant's own values (e.g. 'Your anxiety score of {{score.anxiety.value}} is in the {{score.anxiety.band}} range — ...'). This makes feedback narrative genuinely personalized and rule-driven.
data_model_changes: ScoreInterpretationRange (runtime/feedback/ScoreInterpreter.ts:13): add optional message?: string (piped narrative shown when the score lands in this band), distinct from the short description
ReportSection narrative (from E-FEEDBACK-3): already carries a condition formula; document the score.<scaleId>.* variables it can reference
step_by_step: Add a per-band 'message' textarea to the range-row editor in StatisticalFeedbackDesigner.svelte:483 (alongside the P1-T7 description input), with a variable-insert helper listing available score.* and question variables.

At runtime, when a score matches a band, interpolate range.message through the P1-T6 unified interpolation against the live variables (including the E-FEEDBACK-1 score.<scaleId> fields) and render it in the interpretation card (StatisticalFeedback.svelte:281 area) guarded on non-empty.
Wire the same piped message into ReportGenerator.buildScoreSection so the PDF shows the personalized narrative.
Expose a rule-summary in the designer: a read-only list showing, per band, the message that will fire — so the researcher can self-verify coverage.
Reuse evaluateVisibility for any section-level condition (no new condition engine); the band message is data-driven off the already-matched band.
Tests: interpretation card renders the piped message with a substituted score value; empty message renders nothing.
check + test.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test (band-message interpolation test green)

Live QA: author band messages containing {{score.anxiety.value}} and {{score.anxiety.band}}; fill out so the score lands in each band and confirm the correct personalized message renders on screen and in the PDF
Demo: three participants with different scores each see a distinct, value-piped interpretive paragraph and referral guidance

epics.title: Enforce project-scoped read isolation so ProjectRole is a real access boundary
epics.rationale: Closes the sota_matrix 'Project-level READ isolation' gap and the correctness inconsistency between the handler gate and RLS. Today access::verify_project_access and list_projects grant every active org member read visibility into every project, so ProjectRole::Viewer/Editor is only meaningful for writes -- while questionnaire_definitions RLS (00014:154-175) already restricts reads to project_members (or org admin/owner). A 2026 SOTA multi-tenant product must let an org contain confidential projects a Member cannot see. This makes the two enforcement layers agree and makes ProjectRole the load-bearing boundary the enum promises.
data_model_changes: New migration 00027_project_read_scoping.sql: add projects_select_via_membership policy admitting a row when caller is org owner/admin OR EXISTS project_members row; keep existing org-member policy only behind an org setting flag 'projectVisibility'='org'|'members'
organizations.settings JSONB gains a typed 'projectVisibility' key (default 'org' for backward compat, 'members' for strict isolation)
step_by_step: Add helper access::verify_project_read_access that admits org owner/admin unconditionally, otherwise requires a project_members row (mirroring the questionnaire RLS predicate at 00014:167-173).
Introduce an org-setting read: fn org_project_visibility(executor, org_id) -> ProjectVisibility reading organizations.settings->>'projectVisibility'.

In projects.rs list_projects (89-141): when visibility=='members' and caller is not org owner/admin, add a JOIN/EXISTS on project_members so only member-projects return; keep current org-wide behavior when visibility=='org'.
In projects.rs get_project (236) replace verify_project_access with verify_project_read_access.
Audit every access::verify_project_access call site (sessions.rs, questionnaires.rs, media.rs, comments.rs) and swap to verify_project_read_access where the resource is project-confidential.
Write migration 00027 adding projects_select_via_membership and gating the org-wide projects_select behind a SQL function public.org_project_visibility(org_id).
Add a RbacManager convenience has_project_read(user, project) delegating to the same predicate for reuse.
Backfill: default all existing orgs to 'org' visibility so no current behavior breaks; expose the toggle in admin settings (E-RBAC-8 wires the UI).
Add rustdoc on access.rs clarifying verify_project_access (org-wide) is retained only for org-visibility='org' and verify_project_read_access is the default.
verification: cargo test --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml -- --include-ignored

New tests/project_read_isolation.rs: seed org with owner A, member B, project P owned by A with B NOT a project_member; assert B's GET /api/projects excludes P and GET /api/projects/{P} returns 403 when visibility='members', includes it when visibility='org'.
Live QA: as a Member without project membership, confirm a confidential project is hidden in the dashboard project list and 403s on direct URL; as org Admin it remains visible.
Demo: create org with two projects, set visibility='members', invite a Member to only one project, screen-record that they see exactly one project.

epics.title: Append-only audit log of privileged actions with admin timeline UI
epics.rationale: Closes the 'Audit log' missing capability -- a hard requirement for any research-compliance/IRB or enterprise buyer. Every role change, invitation, member removal, domain verification, ownership transfer, and org/project deletion must be attributable and immutable. No trail exists today (grep confirms zero audit code).
data_model_changes: 00028_audit_log.sql: audit_events(id uuid pk, organization_id uuid, actor_user_id uuid, action text, resource_type text, resource_id uuid, metadata jsonb, ip inet, created_at timestamptz default now()); index (organization_id, created_at desc); RLS SELECT policy: org admin/owner of organization_id; INSERT restricted to app role, no UPDATE/DELETE policy (append-only)
REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON audit_events FROM qdesigner_app to make immutability schema-enforced
step_by_step: Create apps/server/src/audit/mod.rs exposing async fn record(tx, AuditEvent{org_id, actor, action, resource_type, resource_id, metadata}) that INSERTs on the request tx so it commits atomically with the mutation.

Define an AuditAction enum (MemberRoleChanged, MemberRemoved, InvitationCreated/Revoked, DomainVerified, OrgUpdated, ProjectDeleted, OwnershipTransferred, ApiKeyCreated...) with as_str for stable action strings.

Instrument privileged handlers: change_member_role (organizations.rs:812 after UPDATE), remove_member (669), create/revoke_invitation, verify_domain, delete_organization/delete_project, add/update/remove_project_member -- call audit::record before returning.
Capture actor IP via the ConnectInfo/X-Forwarded-For value threaded for rate limiting (build on P2-T5's peer-IP key) into metadata.
Add GET /api/organizations/{id}/audit (org admin/owner gated) with cursor pagination over created_at; register in api/mod.rs org group.
Write migration 00028 with table, indexes, RLS SELECT policy, and REVOKE for append-only.
Frontend admin/audit/+page.svelte rendering a reverse-chron timeline (actor, action, resource, time, expandable metadata); add api.organizations.audit.list to lib/services/api.ts.
Add filters (action type, actor, date range) driven by query params on the endpoint.
verification: cargo test: tests/audit_log.rs asserts a role change writes exactly one audit_events row with correct actor/action/resource and that UPDATE/DELETE on the row are denied for qdesigner_app.
Live QA: change a member's role in admin UI, then load /admin/audit and confirm the event appears with actor + before/after role in metadata.
Demo: perform role change, invitation, and member removal, then screen-record the audit timeline showing all three attributed events.

epics.title: Activate a granular permission layer + custom roles over the dead Permission enum
epics.rationale: Closes 'Granular per-action/per-resource permissions'. The Permission enum (models.rs:66-95) enumerates 19 actions but is dead code; authorization is coarse role-tiers. SOTA multi-tenant products offer custom roles / permission sets (e.g. an 'Analyst' who can read sessions + export but not edit questionnaires). This introduces a real permission-resolution layer while keeping the built-in roles as presets.
data_model_changes: 00029_custom_roles.sql: org_roles(id, organization_id, name, permissions text[], is_system bool) seeded with the four system OrgRoles mapped to Permission sets; organization_members gains optional custom_role_id uuid references org_roles(id)
Effective permission = union of the system-role default set and any custom_role permissions
step_by_step: Add Permission::from_str and a static ROLE_PERMISSIONS map (OrgRole -> &[Permission]) so the enum stops being dead and system roles resolve to permission sets.
Add RbacManager::resolve_permissions(executor, user, org) returning a HashSet<Permission> from the member's system role plus any custom_role_id row.
Add RbacManager::require_permission(executor, user, org, Permission) -> Result<(),ApiError> used where fine-grained checks are warranted (exports, analytics reads).

Migrate a first tranche of handlers (analytics/export endpoints in sessions.rs, questionnaire publish) from has_org_role tiers to require_permission so behavior is identical for system roles but overridable by custom roles.
CRUD endpoints for custom roles: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/organizations/{id}/roles (owner/admin gated, is_system rows immutable), registered in api/mod.rs.
Write migration 00029 seeding the four system roles per org (backfill trigger for existing orgs) and adding custom_role_id.
Frontend admin/roles/+page.svelte: list system + custom roles, a permission-matrix editor (checkbox grid of the 19 Permission actions), assign custom role in the member row of admin/users.
Wire audit (E-RBAC-2) events RoleCreated/RoleUpdated/PermissionGranted.
verification: cargo test: tests/custom_roles.rs asserts a custom 'Analyst' role (SessionRead+export only) can hit the analytics endpoint but is 403 on questionnaire write, while system Admin retains full access.
Live QA: create an Analyst custom role, assign it to a member, log in as them and confirm read-only analytics access plus blocked editing.
Demo: build a custom role in the matrix editor, assign it, and screen-record the differential access.

epics.title: Project member management UI + seat model
epics.rationale: Closes the 'Project member management UI' missing capability: the server already exposes add/update/remove_project_member (projects.rs:543-782) with correct role gating, but no frontend consumes them, so ProjectRole is unassignable through the product. Also adds an org seat model so member counts are governable (foundation for billing).
data_model_changes: organizations.settings gains 'seatLimit' (nullable int); add_member/create_invitation enforce active-member+pending-invite count < seatLimit when set
No new tables required for the member UI itself
step_by_step: Add api.projects.members.{list,add,update,remove} to lib/services/api.ts mapping the existing caller-less server routes.

Add a Members tab to the project settings surface (routes/(app)/projects/[id]) rendering current project_members with role selects (Viewer/Editor/Admin/Owner) and an add-by-email/existing-org-member picker.
Reuse the Dialog + ConfirmDialog (from remediation P7-T3) for role change / removal instead of confirm().
Enforce seatLimit server-side in organizations.rs add_member and create_invitation (return 409 with a typed 'seat_limit_reached' error) and surface it in admin settings.
Add a seats summary card in admin (used N of M) reading a count endpoint GET /api/organizations/{id}/seats.
Emit audit events (E-RBAC-2) for project member add/update/remove.
Guard: only project owner/admin or org owner/admin may open the Members tab (mirror projects.rs:568-586 checks client-side + server enforced).
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && test
Live QA: as project admin, add an org member as project Editor, confirm they gain edit and a Viewer does not; set seatLimit=2 and confirm the 3rd add is blocked with a seat error.
Demo: screen-record assigning ProjectRole via the new Members tab and the resulting differential edit access in the designer.

epics.title: Explicit org & project ownership transfer
epics.rationale: Closes 'Resource ownership transfer'. change_member_role can grant 'owner' but there is no atomic transfer that demotes the outgoing owner, and project ownership cannot be reassigned at all. Ownership transfer is a standard multi-tenant admin operation (offboarding a departing researcher / PI handover) and must be a single audited, guarded action.
data_model_changes: No schema change (uses existing role columns); 'transferred_from'/'transferred_at' captured in audit metadata
step_by_step: Add POST /api/organizations/{id}/transfer-ownership {new_owner_user_id}: caller must be a current owner; in one tx set new owner to 'owner' and optionally demote caller to 'admin', requiring password re-confirmation (reuse verify_password) for the sensitive action.
Add POST /api/projects/{id}/transfer-ownership {new_owner_user_id}: caller must be project owner or org owner/admin; new owner must already be a project member (or auto-add as owner).
Guard both against transferring to a non-member and against leaving zero owners.
Record OwnershipTransferred audit events with from/to.
Frontend: a 'Transfer ownership' action in admin org settings and project settings, behind a typed ConfirmDialog requiring the target selection + password.
Add api client methods organizations.transferOwnership / projects.transferOwnership.
Reconcile with change_member_role's last-owner guard so transfer is the sanctioned path to change the sole owner.
verification: cargo test: tests/ownership_transfer.rs asserts org transfer promotes target to owner + demotes caller in one tx, rejects transfer to non-member, and never yields zero owners.
Live QA: transfer an org to another member, confirm the old owner loses owner-only controls and the new owner gains them.
Demo: screen-record an org ownership handover including the password confirmation and resulting audit entry.

epics.title: SSO federation (OIDC/SAML) with JIT org membership
epics.rationale: Closes the 'SSO/SAML/OIDC' missing capability -- the single largest enterprise-procurement blocker. Institutions and research labs mandate federated login. Adds per-org IdP configuration, an OIDC/SAML callback that provisions/links users just-in-time, and maps IdP groups to org roles, extending the existing domain auto-join (P8-T8) into true federation.
data_model_changes: 00030_sso.sql: org_identity_providers(id, organization_id, protocol('oidc'|'saml'), issuer/metadata_url, client_id, client_secret_enc, default_role, group_role_map jsonb, enabled bool); users gains external_subject text + idp_id uuid (nullable) with unique(idp_id, external_subject)
Secrets stored encrypted (reuse config-provided key); no plaintext client_secret in DB
step_by_step: Add an oidc crate (openidconnect) dependency; define apps/server/src/api/sso.rs with GET /api/sso/{org_slug}/start (redirect to IdP) and GET /api/sso/callback (code exchange).

On callback: validate id_token, resolve/create the user by external_subject, upsert an active organization_members row with default_role (or group_role_map-derived role), then mint the app JWT via existing session::issue.
Add SAML support behind the same table via a samael-based handler path selected by protocol.
IdP admin CRUD: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/organizations/{id}/sso (owner-gated), storing encrypted secrets; validate metadata_url reachability.
Group->role mapping: map IdP group claims to OrgRole via group_role_map, falling back to default_role.
Write migration 00030 with the tables and unique constraints; encrypt client_secret at rest.
Frontend admin/sso/+page.svelte to configure the IdP; login page gains a 'Sign in with SSO' path resolving the org by email domain (reusing organization_domains).
Emit audit events for SSO login and config changes; enforce that SSO-provisioned members cannot self-change role if org enforces IdP-managed roles.
verification: cargo test with a mock OIDC provider (wiremock): callback with a valid id_token provisions the user + org membership at default_role and issues a JWT; group claim maps to Admin per group_role_map.
Live QA against a Keycloak/dev IdP in docker-compose: complete an end-to-end SSO login and land authenticated with the mapped role.
Demo: screen-record configuring an OIDC IdP and a fresh user signing in via SSO into the correct org and role.

epics.title: SCIM 2.0 provisioning + API keys / service accounts
epics.rationale: Closes 'SCIM provisioning' and 'API keys/service accounts'. Enterprises require automated de/provisioning (offboarding via IdP) and machine identities for exports/integrations. Both are absent today (grep confirms). API keys also unblock programmatic access to the analytics/export endpoints without a human JWT.
data_model_changes: 00031_api_keys.sql: api_keys(id, organization_id, name, key_hash, prefix, scopes text[], created_by, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at); index on prefix
00032_scim.sql: scim_tokens(id, organization_id, token_hash, enabled); reuse organization_members for provisioned users (add source='scim')
step_by_step: API keys: POST /api/organizations/{id}/api-keys (owner/admin) returns the plaintext key ONCE; store Argon2/HMAC key_hash + prefix; list/revoke endpoints.

Add an ApiKey extractor/middleware alongside AuthenticatedUser that resolves an Authorization: Bearer sk_... to an org + scopes (reuse E-RBAC-3 Permission set) and sets app.user_id to a synthetic service principal for RLS.
Gate api-key requests through require_permission so a key scoped to SessionRead+export cannot mutate.

SCIM: implement /scim/v2/Users and /scim/v2/Groups (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE) authenticated by a per-org scim_token; map SCIM create->organization_members active, deactivate->status='suspended', delete->soft remove.
Write migrations 00031/00032; store all secrets hashed; add REVOKE-based immutability where appropriate.
Frontend admin/api-keys/+page.svelte (create/copy-once/revoke, scope picker); admin/sso/+page.svelte gains a SCIM token section.
Emit audit events for key creation/use-first-time/revocation and SCIM provisioning changes.
Enforce last_used_at + expiry checks and a rate limit on api-key traffic (reuse rate_limit middleware keyed by key id).
verification: cargo test: tests/api_keys.rs asserts a scoped key authenticates, respects its permission scope (403 on out-of-scope mutation), and 401s after revoke; tests/scim.rs asserts SCIM create adds an active member and deactivate suspends it.
Live QA: create an export-scoped API key, curl the analytics endpoint with it successfully and a mutation endpoint with 403; run a SCIM create/deactivate cycle via curl.
Demo: screen-record generating an API key, using it from curl, then revoking it and showing the request now 401s.

epics.title: Per-org branding/whitelabel application + org settings surface
epics.rationale: Closes 'Per-org settings & branding application'. The schema already carries organizations.logo_url + settings JSONB and update_organization exists, but nothing applies branding and there is no rich settings surface (the project-visibility toggle from E-RBAC-1, seat limit from E-RBAC-4, and IdP-managed-role flags from E-RBAC-6 all need a home). Whitelabel branding on the participant fillout chrome is a common research/enterprise requirement.
data_model_changes: Type organizations.settings with a documented OrgSettings shape (primaryColor, logoUrl, participantHeader, projectVisibility, seatLimit, idpManagedRoles); no new table
Optional 00033_org_branding.sql adding a CHECK-validated jsonb or leaving as JSONB with server-side validation
step_by_step: Define a typed OrgSettings struct (serde) in organizations.rs and validate it in update_organization (reject unknown color formats/oversized logo URLs).
Expose GET /api/organizations/{id}/branding as a lightweight org-scoped read the fillout route can fetch to theme participant chrome.

Frontend admin/settings/+page.svelte: sections for Branding (logo upload via existing media pipeline, primary color, participant header text), Access (projectVisibility toggle from E-RBAC-1, seatLimit from E-RBAC-4), and IdP (link to admin/sso).

Apply branding in (fillout)/q/[code] chrome: read the org branding and set CSS custom properties for primary color + render the logo, coordinating with the P1-T2 HSL-token fix so tokens resolve correctly.
Guard branding edits behind org admin/owner; emit audit events for settings changes.
Add a live preview of the participant header/logo in the admin branding section.
Fallback to platform defaults when a field is unset.
verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && test; cargo test for OrgSettings validation (rejects malformed color).
Live QA: set a primary color + logo, open a participant fillout link and confirm the chrome is themed accordingly across light/dark.
Demo: screen-record branding an org and the participant-facing questionnaire reflecting the logo and color.

epics.title: Org-level GDPR data export + tenant erasure + data residency tagging
epics.rationale: Closes 'Data residency/export/erasure'. Remediation P8-T10 adds only individual account deletion; a multi-tenant research platform storing participant PII needs org-scoped exportable data bundles (DSAR fulfillment), a controlled tenant hard-erasure path, and at minimum a residency/region tag on the org so data-location commitments are recordable and enforceable in storage routing.
data_model_changes: organizations gains data_region text (default 'eu') and a legal_hold bool; 00034_org_gdpr.sql
data_exports(id, organization_id, requested_by, status, artifact_key, created_at) to track async export jobs
step_by_step: Add POST /api/organizations/{id}/export (owner-gated): enqueue an async job that serializes all org projects, questionnaires, sessions, responses, variables, and members into a signed zip in object storage; return a data_exports row id.
Add GET /api/organizations/{id}/export/{jobId} to poll status and fetch a presigned download when ready.

Add POST /api/organizations/{id}/erase (owner-gated, password + typed confirmation, blocked when legal_hold): soft-then-hard delete of org data with PII anonymization of participant rows, reusing P8-T10 anonymization primitives at org scope.
Add data_region to organizations and thread it into the storage key prefix / bucket selection in storage/ so new media/exports land in the region bucket (enforcement point for residency claims).
Write migration 00034; add legal_hold guard so erasure/export respect holds.
Frontend admin Data & Privacy section: request export (with progress), set data region (create-time only, immutable after data exists), and a guarded Delete Organization Data flow.
Emit audit events (E-RBAC-2) for export requested/downloaded and erasure executed; retain audit_events themselves through erasure per compliance.
Rate-limit export requests and expire artifacts after N days.
verification: cargo test: tests/gdpr.rs asserts export produces a bundle covering all org resources, erase anonymizes participant PII while preserving audit_events, and legal_hold blocks erasure.
Live QA: request an org export, download the zip, verify it contains sessions+responses; then run erasure on a throwaway org and confirm PII is gone but audit remains.
Demo: screen-record a DSAR-style export download and a guarded tenant erasure.

epics.title: Cross-project & external-guest sharing controls
epics.rationale: Closes 'Cross-project/cross-org sharing'. Once E-RBAC-1 makes projects confidential-by-default, researchers need an explicit way to share a project (or a single questionnaire's analytics) with a collaborator in another project or another org (a guest reviewer), rather than the current all-or-nothing org membership. This is the collaboration primitive the current model lacks.
data_model_changes: 00035_resource_shares.sql: resource_shares(id, resource_type('project'|'questionnaire'), resource_id, grantee_user_id nullable, grantee_email nullable, role, created_by, expires_at); index on resource_id
Extend access::verify_project_read_access/verify_questionnaire_access to also admit an active resource_shares grant
step_by_step: Add a share model + endpoints: POST/GET/DELETE /api/projects/{id}/shares and /api/questionnaires/{id}/shares (project admin/owner gated), granting a ProjectRole-equivalent to an external user by email.
On share to a non-member email, create a pending grant resolved when that user authenticates (link resource_shares.grantee_user_id on first login by email).

Extend the E-RBAC-1 read predicates and verify_questionnaire_access (access.rs:121) to OR-in an EXISTS on resource_shares (not expired), so shared collaborators gain scoped read/edit without full org membership.
Add a corresponding RLS policy branch so DB-layer isolation also honors shares (mirror the handler predicate in 00035).
Guest cap: shares confer at most the share's role and never org-management permissions; enforce in require_permission resolution.
Write migration 00035 with the table + RLS branch + expiry.
Frontend: a Share dialog on project and questionnaire surfaces (email + role + expiry), and a 'Shared with me' section on the dashboard listing externally-granted resources.
Emit audit events for share created/revoked and expiry cleanup job.
verification: cargo test: tests/resource_shares.rs asserts a user with only a project share can read/edit per the share role but cannot list other org projects nor manage members, and that expired shares deny access at both handler and RLS layers.
Live QA: share one questionnaire's analytics with a user in a different org, confirm they see exactly that resource in 'Shared with me' and nothing else.
Demo: screen-record sharing a project with an external collaborator and their scoped, time-limited access.
